  request: LS0tLS1CRUdJTiBDRVJUSUZJQ0FURSBSRVFVRVNULS0tLS0K...
  signerName: kubernetes.io/kubelet-serving
  expirationSeconds: 2592000
  usages:
  - server auth
  - digital signature
  - key encipherment
Example 3: Author Service Multi-Usage Spec

This example illustrates an Author service spec with multiple key usages.

spec:
  request: LS0tLS1CRUdJTiBDRVJUSUZJQ0FURSBSRVFVRVNULS0tLS0K...
  signerName: kubernetes.io/kube-apiserver-client
  usages:
  - client auth
  - server auth
  - digital signature
  - key encipherment
  - content commitment
Example 4: Location Service Kubelet Spec

This example demonstrates a Location service spec for kubelet client certificates.

spec:
  request: LS0tLS1CRUdJTiBDRVJUSUZJQ0FURSBSRVFVRVNULS0tLS0K...
  signerName: kubernetes.io/kube-apiserver-client-kubelet
  expirationSeconds: 86400
  usages:
  - client auth
  - digital signature
Example 5: JobPost Service Custom Signer Spec

This example shows a JobPost service spec using a custom certificate signer.

spec:
  request: LS0tLS1CRUdJTiBDRVJUSUZJQ0FURSBSRVFVRVNULS0tLS0K...
  signerName: anihpj.io/custom-signer
  expirationSeconds: 604800
  usages:
  - client auth
  - server auth
  - digital signature
  - key agreement

CertificateSigningRequestStatus

Official Docs: CertificateSigningRequestStatus API | API Version: certificates.k8s.io/v1

CertificateSigningRequestStatus contains conditions indicating approved/denied/failed status and the issued certificate.

Full Description

CertificateSigningRequestStatus provides real-time information about your anihpj Django application certificate requests. This status object tracks the approval state, issued certificates, and failure conditions for certificate requests from your Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost services, enabling automated certificate lifecycle management.

Syntax

status:
  certificate: LS0tLS1CRUdJTiBDRVJUSUZJQ0FURS0tLS0t...
  conditions:
  - type: Approved
    status: "True"
    reason: AutoApproved
    message: "Certificate request approved by anihpj-controller"
    lastUpdateTime: "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z"

Parameters Description

  • certificate: []byte - PEM-encoded issued certificate (set after approval)
  • conditions: []CertificateSigningRequestCondition - Current conditions (Approved, Denied, Failed)
  • conditions[].type: string - Condition type (Approved, Denied, Failed)
  • conditions[].status: string - Condition status (True, False, Unknown)
  • conditions[].reason: string - Brief reason for the condition
  • conditions[].message: string - Human-readable message with condition details
  • conditions[].lastUpdateTime: Time - Last condition update timestamp
  • conditions[].lastTransitionTime: Time - Last condition transition timestamp

5 Django Examples

Example 1: Skills Service Approved Status

This example demonstrates an approved CertificateSigningRequestStatus for Skills service.

status:
  certificate: LS0tLS1CRUdJTiBDRVJUSUZJQ0FURS0tLS0t...
  conditions:
  - type: Approved
    status: "True"
    reason: AutoApproved
    message: "Certificate request approved by anihpj-controller"
    lastUpdateTime: "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z"
Example 2: User Service Pending Status

This example shows a User service certificate request waiting for approval.

status:
  conditions:
  - type: Pending
    status: "True"
    reason: WaitingForApproval
    message: "Certificate request submitted and waiting for manual approval"
    lastUpdateTime: "2024-01-15T10:25:00Z"
Example 3: Author Service Denied Status

This example illustrates a denied certificate request for Author service.

status:
  conditions:
  - type: Denied
    status: "True"
    reason: InvalidRequest
    message: "Certificate request denied: invalid subject name format"
    lastUpdateTime: "2024-01-15T10:20:00Z"
Example 4: Location Service Failed Status

This example demonstrates a failed certificate issuance for Location service.

status:
  conditions:
  - type: Failed
    status: "True"
    reason: SignerFailure
    message: "Certificate signer failed to issue certificate: CA unavailable"
    lastUpdateTime: "2024-01-15T10:15:00Z"
Example 5: JobPost Service Multi-Condition Status

This example shows a JobPost service with multiple status conditions over time.

status:
  certificate: LS0tLS1CRUdJTiBDRVJUSUZJQ0FURS0tLS0t...
  conditions:
  - type: Approved
    status: "True"
    reason: ManualApproval
    message: "Certificate request manually approved by admin"
    lastUpdateTime: "2024-01-15T10:35:00Z"
    lastTransitionTime: "2024-01-15T10:35:00Z"

CertificateSigningRequestList

Official Docs: CertificateSigningRequestList API | API Version: certificates.k8s.io/v1

CertificateSigningRequestList is a collection of CertificateSigningRequest objects returned by list operations.

Full Description

CertificateSigningRequestList provides a comprehensive view of all certificate requests in your anihpj Django application cluster. This collection enables bulk operations, status monitoring, and administrative oversight of certificate lifecycle management across your Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost services.

Syntax

# List all CertificateSigningRequests
kubectl get csr

# List with labels
kubectl get csr -l app=anihpj

# List in JSON format
kubectl get csr -o json

Parameters Description

  • apiVersion: string - API version, always "certificates.k8s.io/v1"
  • kind: string - Resource type, always "CertificateSigningRequestList"
  • metadata: ListMeta - Standard list metadata including resource version
  • items: []CertificateSigningRequest - Array of CertificateSigningRequest objects

5 Django Examples

Example 1: Skills Service Certificate List

This example demonstrates listing all Skills service certificate requests.

# List Skills service certificate requests
kubectl get csr -l app=anihpj,service=skills
NAME AGE SIGNERNAME REQUESTOR REQUESTEDDURATION CONDITION anihpj-skills-client-csr 5m kubernetes.io/kube-apiserver-client system:serviceaccount:default:skills <none> Approved,Issued anihpj-skills-server-csr 3m kubernetes.io/kubelet-serving system:serviceaccount:default:skills 24h Pending
Example 2: User Service Certificate Collection

This example shows retrieving User service certificate requests in JSON format.

# Get User service certificate list as JSON
kubectl get csr -l service=user -o json
{ "apiVersion": "v1", "kind": "List", "items": [ { "apiVersion": "certificates.k8s.io/v1", "kind": "CertificateSigningRequest", "metadata": { "name": "anihpj-user-serving-csr", "labels": {"app": "anihpj", "service": "user"} } } ] }
Example 3: Author Service Certificate Status Overview

This example illustrates viewing Author service certificate status in wide format.

# List Author service certificates with detailed status
kubectl get csr -l service=author -o wide
NAME AGE SIGNERNAME REQUESTOR REQUESTEDDURATION CONDITION anihpj-author-30day-csr 10m kubernetes.io/kube-apiserver-client system:serviceaccount:default:author 30d Approved,Issued anihpj-author-client-csr 8m kubernetes.io/kube-apiserver-client system:serviceaccount:default:author <none> Denied
Example 4: Location Service Certificate Watch

This example demonstrates watching Location service certificate changes in real-time.

# Watch Location service certificate requests
kubectl get csr -l service=location --watch
NAME AGE SIGNERNAME REQUESTOR CONDITION anihpj-location-kubelet-csr 1s kubernetes.io/kube-apiserver-client-kubelet system:serviceaccount:default:location Pending anihpj-location-kubelet-csr 5s kubernetes.io/kube-apiserver-client-kubelet system:serviceaccount:default:location Approved,Issued
Example 5: JobPost Service Certificate Summary

This example shows summarizing all JobPost service certificate requests with custom columns.

# Get JobPost service certificate summary
kubectl get csr -l service=jobpost -o custom-columns="NAME:.metadata.name,STATUS:.status.conditions[*].type,SIGNER:.spec.signerName"
NAME STATUS SIGNER anihpj-jobpost-multi-csr Approved kubernetes.io/kube-apiserver-client anihpj-jobpost-client-csr Pending kubernetes.io/kube-apiserver-client anihpj-jobpost-serving-csr Approved,Issued kubernetes.io/kubelet-serving

Operations

Operations Overview: Complete CertificateSigningRequest API operations for managing certificate lifecycle

All CertificateSigningRequest operations for managing certificate lifecycle in your anihpj Django application.

Operations Overview

get - Read the specified CertificateSigningRequest
get approval - Read approval of the specified CertificateSigningRequest
get status - Read status of the specified CertificateSigningRequest
list - List or watch objects of kind CertificateSigningRequest
create - Create a CertificateSigningRequest
update - Replace the specified CertificateSigningRequest
update approval - Replace approval of the specified CertificateSigningRequest
update status - Replace status of the specified CertificateSigningRequest
patch - Partially update the specified CertificateSigningRequest
patch approval - Partially update approval of the specified CertificateSigningRequest
patch status - Partially update status of the specified CertificateSigningRequest
delete - Delete a CertificateSigningRequest
deletecollection - Delete collection of CertificateSigningRequest

create - create a CertificateSigningRequest

Create a new CertificateSigningRequest to request X.509 certificates for your anihpj Django application services.

HTTP Request
POST /apis/certificates.k8s.io/v1/certificatesigningrequests
Parameters
  • body (in body): CertificateSigningRequest, required - CertificateSigningRequest object
  • dryRun (in query): string - When present, indicates that modifications should not be persisted
  • fieldManager (in query): string - FieldManager is a name associated with the actor or entity
  • fieldValidation (in query): string - FieldValidation instructs the server on how to handle objects
  • pretty (in query): string - If 'true', then the output is pretty printed
Return Value
  • 200 (CertificateSigningRequest): OK
  • 201 (CertificateSigningRequest): Created
  • 202 (CertificateSigningRequest): Accepted
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Django Create Examples
Example 1: Create Skills Service Client Certificate Request

This example demonstrates creating a CertificateSigningRequest for the Skills model service client authentication.

# Create Skills service client certificate request
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: certificates.k8s.io/v1
kind: CertificateSigningRequest
metadata:
  name: anihpj-skills-client-csr
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    service: skills
spec:
  request: $(cat skills-client.csr | base64 | tr -d '\n')
  signerName: kubernetes.io/kube-apiserver-client
  usages:
  - client auth
  - digital signature
EOF
certificatesigningrequest.certificates.k8s.io/anihpj-skills-client-csr created
Example 2: Create User Service Serving Certificate Request

This example shows creating a serving certificate request for User model HTTPS endpoints.

# Create User service serving certificate request
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: certificates.k8s.io/v1
kind: CertificateSigningRequest
metadata:
  name: anihpj-user-serving-csr
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    service: user
spec:
  request: $(cat user-serving.csr | base64 | tr -d '\n')
  signerName: kubernetes.io/kubelet-serving
  expirationSeconds: 2592000
  usages:
  - server auth
  - digital signature
  - key encipherment
EOF
certificatesigningrequest.certificates.k8s.io/anihpj-user-serving-csr created
Example 3: Create Author Service Multi-Usage Certificate Request

This example illustrates creating a multi-purpose certificate request for Author content management service.

# Create Author service multi-usage certificate request
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: certificates.k8s.io/v1
kind: CertificateSigningRequest
metadata:
  name: anihpj-author-multi-csr
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    service: author
spec:
  request: $(cat author-multi.csr | base64 | tr -d '\n')
  signerName: kubernetes.io/kube-apiserver-client
  usages:
  - client auth
  - server auth
  - digital signature
  - key encipherment
EOF
certificatesigningrequest.certificates.k8s.io/anihpj-author-multi-csr created
Example 4: Create Location Service Kubelet Certificate Request

This example demonstrates creating a kubelet certificate request for Location geolocation service.

# Create Location service kubelet certificate request
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: certificates.k8s.io/v1
kind: CertificateSigningRequest
metadata:
  name: anihpj-location-kubelet-csr
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    service: location
spec:
  request: $(cat location-kubelet.csr | base64 | tr -d '\n')
  signerName: kubernetes.io/kube-apiserver-client-kubelet
  expirationSeconds: 86400
  usages:
  - client auth
  - digital signature
EOF
certificatesigningrequest.certificates.k8s.io/anihpj-location-kubelet-csr created
Example 5: Create JobPost Service Custom Certificate Request

This example shows creating a certificate request using custom signer for JobPost job management service.

# Create JobPost service custom certificate request
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: certificates.k8s.io/v1
kind: CertificateSigningRequest
metadata:
  name: anihpj-jobpost-custom-csr
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    service: jobpost
spec:
  request: $(cat jobpost-custom.csr | base64 | tr -d '\n')
  signerName: anihpj.io/custom-signer
  expirationSeconds: 604800
  usages:
  - client auth
  - server auth
  - digital signature
  - key agreement
EOF
certificatesigningrequest.certificates.k8s.io/anihpj-jobpost-custom-csr created

get - read the specified CertificateSigningRequest

Read details of a specific CertificateSigningRequest including its status, certificate, and approval conditions.

HTTP Request
GET /apis/certificates.k8s.io/v1/certificatesigningrequests/{name}
Parameters
  • name (in path): string, required - name of the CertificateSigningRequest
  • pretty (in query): string - pretty print output
Return Value
  • 200 (CertificateSigningRequest): OK
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Django Read Examples
Example 1: Read Skills Service Certificate Request Details

This example demonstrates reading the Skills model certificate request details.

# Read Skills service certificate request
kubectl get csr anihpj-skills-client-csr -o yaml
apiVersion: certificates.k8s.io/v1 kind: CertificateSigningRequest metadata: name: anihpj-skills-client-csr labels: app: anihpj service: skills spec: signerName: kubernetes.io/kube-apiserver-client usages: - client auth - digital signature status: conditions: - type: Approved status: "True"
Example 2: Read User Service Certificate Status

This example shows reading User service certificate request status information.

# Read User service certificate status
kubectl describe csr anihpj-user-serving-csr
Name: anihpj-user-serving-csr Labels: app=anihpj service=user API Version: certificates.k8s.io/v1 Kind: CertificateSigningRequest Spec: Signer Name: kubernetes.io/kubelet-serving Usages: server auth digital signature Status: Certificate: LS0tLS1CRUdJTiBDRVJUSUZJQ0FURS0tLS0t... Conditions: Type: Approved Status: True
Example 3: Read Author Service Certificate JSON

This example illustrates reading Author service certificate request in JSON format.

# Read Author service certificate as JSON
kubectl get csr anihpj-author-multi-csr -o json | jq '.spec'
{ "request": "LS0tLS1CRUdJTiBDRVJUSUZJQ0FURSBSRVFVRVNULS0tLS0K...", "signerName": "kubernetes.io/kube-apiserver-client", "usages": [ "client auth", "server auth", "digital signature", "key encipherment" ] }
Example 4: Read Location Service Certificate Conditions

This example demonstrates reading Location service certificate conditions status.

# Read Location service certificate conditions
kubectl get csr anihpj-location-kubelet-csr -o jsonpath='{.status.conditions[*]}'
{"type":"Approved","status":"True","reason":"AutoApproved","message":"Certificate approved by anihpj-controller","lastUpdateTime":"2024-01-15T10:30:00Z"}
Example 5: Read JobPost Service Certificate Summary

This example shows reading JobPost service certificate request summary information.

# Read JobPost service certificate summary
kubectl get csr anihpj-jobpost-custom-csr -o custom-columns="NAME:.metadata.name,STATUS:.status.conditions[*].type,SIGNER:.spec.signerName"
NAME STATUS SIGNER anihpj-jobpost-custom-csr Approved anihpj.io/custom-signer

get approval - read approval of the specified CertificateSigningRequest

Read the approval status of a specific CertificateSigningRequest to check if it has been approved or denied.

HTTP Request
GET /apis/certificates.k8s.io/v1/certificatesigningrequests/{name}/approval
Parameters
  • name (in path): string, required - name of the CertificateSigningRequest
  • pretty (in query): string - pretty print output
Return Value
  • 200 (CertificateSigningRequest): OK
  • 401: Unauthorized

get status - read status of the specified CertificateSigningRequest

Read the status of a specific CertificateSigningRequest to check its current state and certificate information.

HTTP Request
GET /apis/certificates.k8s.io/v1/certificatesigningrequests/{name}/status
Parameters
  • name (in path): string, required - name of the CertificateSigningRequest
  • pretty (in query): string - pretty print output
Return Value
  • 200 (CertificateSigningRequest): OK
  • 401: Unauthorized

list - list CertificateSigningRequests

List all CertificateSigningRequests in the cluster with optional filtering and sorting capabilities.

HTTP Request
GET /apis/certificates.k8s.io/v1/certificatesigningrequests
Parameters
  • labelSelector (in query): string - label selector to filter results
  • fieldSelector (in query): string - field selector to filter results
  • watch (in query): boolean - watch for changes
  • limit (in query): integer - maximum number of results
  • continue (in query): string - continue token for pagination
  • pretty (in query): string - pretty print output
Return Value
  • 200 (CertificateSigningRequestList): OK
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Django List Examples
Example 1: List All anihpj Certificate Requests

This example demonstrates listing all anihpj Django application certificate requests.

# List all anihpj certificate requests
kubectl get csr -l app=anihpj
NAME AGE SIGNERNAME REQUESTOR CONDITION anihpj-skills-client-csr 5m kubernetes.io/kube-apiserver-client system:serviceaccount:default:skills Approved,Issued anihpj-user-serving-csr 3m kubernetes.io/kubelet-serving system:serviceaccount:default:user Pending anihpj-author-multi-csr 2m kubernetes.io/kube-apiserver-client system:serviceaccount:default:author Approved,Issued
Example 2: List User Service Certificate Requests with Status

This example shows listing User service certificate requests with detailed status.

# List User service certificates with wide output
kubectl get csr -l service=user -o wide
NAME AGE SIGNERNAME REQUESTOR REQUESTEDDURATION CONDITION anihpj-user-client-csr 10m kubernetes.io/kube-apiserver-client system:serviceaccount:default:user 24h Approved,Issued anihpj-user-serving-csr 8m kubernetes.io/kubelet-serving system:serviceaccount:default:user <none> Pending
Example 3: List Author Service Approved Certificates

This example illustrates listing only approved Author service certificate requests.

# List approved Author service certificates
kubectl get csr -l service=author --field-selector=status.conditions[0].type=Approved
NAME AGE SIGNERNAME REQUESTOR CONDITION anihpj-author-multi-csr 15m kubernetes.io/kube-apiserver-client system:serviceaccount:default:author Approved,Issued
Example 4: List Location Service Certificates in JSON

This example demonstrates listing Location service certificates in JSON format.

# List Location service certificates as JSON
kubectl get csr -l service=location -o json | jq '.items[].metadata.name'
"anihpj-location-kubelet-csr" "anihpj-location-server-csr"
Example 5: List JobPost Service Certificates with Watch

This example shows listing and watching JobPost service certificate changes in real-time.

# Watch JobPost service certificate requests
kubectl get csr -l service=jobpost --watch
NAME AGE SIGNERNAME REQUESTOR CONDITION anihpj-jobpost-custom-csr 30s anihpj.io/custom-signer system:serviceaccount:default:jobpost Pending anihpj-jobpost-custom-csr 35s anihpj.io/custom-signer system:serviceaccount:default:jobpost Approved,Issued

update - replace the specified CertificateSigningRequest

Replace an existing CertificateSigningRequest. Note that the spec is immutable after creation, so this is mainly used for metadata updates.

HTTP Request
PUT /apis/certificates.k8s.io/v1/certificatesigningrequests/{name}
Parameters
  • name (in path): string, required - name of the CertificateSigningRequest
  • body: CertificateSigningRequest, required - updated CertificateSigningRequest object
  • dryRun (in query): string - dry run mode
  • fieldManager (in query): string - field manager name
  • pretty (in query): string - pretty print output
Return Value
  • 200 (CertificateSigningRequest): OK
  • 201 (CertificateSigningRequest): Created
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Django Update Examples
Example 1: Update Skills Service Certificate Labels

This example demonstrates updating Skills service certificate request labels.

# Update Skills service certificate labels
kubectl label csr anihpj-skills-client-csr environment=production --overwrite
certificatesigningrequest.certificates.k8s.io/anihpj-skills-client-csr labeled
Example 2: Update User Service Certificate Annotations

This example shows updating User service certificate request annotations.

# Update User service certificate annotations
kubectl annotate csr anihpj-user-serving-csr cert.anihpj.io/managed-by=controller --overwrite
certificatesigningrequest.certificates.k8s.io/anihpj-user-serving-csr annotated
Example 3: Update Author Service Certificate Metadata

This example illustrates updating Author service certificate request metadata.

# Update Author service certificate metadata
kubectl patch csr anihpj-author-multi-csr --type='merge' -p='{"metadata":{"labels":{"version":"v2.0"}}}'
certificatesigningrequest.certificates.k8s.io/anihpj-author-multi-csr patched
Example 4: Update Location Service Certificate with File

This example demonstrates updating Location service certificate request from YAML file.

# Update Location service certificate from file
kubectl replace -f location-csr-updated.yaml
certificatesigningrequest.certificates.k8s.io/anihpj-location-kubelet-csr replaced
Example 5: Update JobPost Service Certificate Priority

This example shows updating JobPost service certificate request with priority labels.

# Update JobPost service certificate priority
kubectl label csr anihpj-jobpost-custom-csr priority=high deployment=blue --overwrite
certificatesigningrequest.certificates.k8s.io/anihpj-jobpost-custom-csr labeled

update approval - replace approval of the specified CertificateSigningRequest

Replace the approval status of a specific CertificateSigningRequest, typically to approve or deny certificate issuance.

HTTP Request
PUT /apis/certificates.k8s.io/v1/certificatesigningrequests/{name}/approval
Parameters
  • name (in path): string, required - name of the CertificateSigningRequest
  • body: CertificateSigningRequest, required - updated approval status
  • dryRun (in query): string - dry run mode
  • fieldManager (in query): string - field manager
  • fieldValidation (in query): string - field validation
  • pretty (in query): string - pretty print output
Return Value
  • 200 (CertificateSigningRequest): OK
  • 201 (CertificateSigningRequest): Created
  • 401: Unauthorized

update status - replace status of the specified CertificateSigningRequest

Replace the status of a specific CertificateSigningRequest, typically to update certificate information or conditions.

HTTP Request
PUT /apis/certificates.k8s.io/v1/certificatesigningrequests/{name}/status
Parameters
  • name (in path): string, required - name of the CertificateSigningRequest
  • body: CertificateSigningRequest, required - updated status
  • dryRun (in query): string - dry run mode
  • fieldManager (in query): string - field manager
  • fieldValidation (in query): string - field validation
  • pretty (in query): string - pretty print output
Return Value
  • 200 (CertificateSigningRequest): OK
  • 201 (CertificateSigningRequest): Created
  • 401: Unauthorized

patch - partially update the specified CertificateSigningRequest

Partially update a CertificateSigningRequest, typically used for updating labels, annotations, or metadata.

HTTP Request
PATCH /apis/certificates.k8s.io/v1/certificatesigningrequests/{name}
Parameters
  • name (in path): string, required - name of the CertificateSigningRequest
  • body: Patch, required - patch operations
  • dryRun (in query): string - dry run mode
  • fieldManager (in query): string - field manager name
  • force (in query): boolean - force patch
  • pretty (in query): string - pretty print output
Return Value
  • 200 (CertificateSigningRequest): OK
  • 201 (CertificateSigningRequest): Created
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Django Patch Examples
Example 1: Patch Skills Service Certificate Labels

This example demonstrates patching Skills service certificate request labels using merge patch.

# Patch Skills service certificate labels
kubectl patch csr anihpj-skills-client-csr --type='merge' -p='{"metadata":{"labels":{"tier":"frontend"}}}'
certificatesigningrequest.certificates.k8s.io/anihpj-skills-client-csr patched
Example 2: Patch User Service Certificate Annotations

This example shows patching User service certificate request annotations using JSON patch.

# Patch User service certificate annotations
kubectl patch csr anihpj-user-serving-csr --type='json' -p='[{"op":"add","path":"/metadata/annotations/cert.anihpj.io~1renewal","value":"automatic"}]'
certificatesigningrequest.certificates.k8s.io/anihpj-user-serving-csr patched
Example 3: Patch Author Service Certificate Strategic Merge

This example illustrates patching Author service certificate request using strategic merge patch.

# Patch Author service certificate with strategic merge
kubectl patch csr anihpj-author-multi-csr --type='strategic' -p='{"metadata":{"labels":{"version":"v2.1","environment":"production"}}}'
certificatesigningrequest.certificates.k8s.io/anihpj-author-multi-csr patched
Example 4: Patch Location Service Certificate from File

This example demonstrates patching Location service certificate request from patch file.

# Patch Location service certificate from file
kubectl patch csr anihpj-location-kubelet-csr --patch-file location-csr-patch.yaml
certificatesigningrequest.certificates.k8s.io/anihpj-location-kubelet-csr patched
Example 5: Patch JobPost Service Certificate Multiple Fields

This example shows patching multiple fields in JobPost service certificate request.

# Patch JobPost service certificate multiple fields
kubectl patch csr anihpj-jobpost-custom-csr --type='merge' -p='{"metadata":{"labels":{"managed-by":"anihpj-controller"},"annotations":{"cert.anihpj.io/auto-approve":"true"}}}'
certificatesigningrequest.certificates.k8s.io/anihpj-jobpost-custom-csr patched

patch approval - partially update approval of the specified CertificateSigningRequest

Partially update the approval status of a specific CertificateSigningRequest using strategic merge patch.

HTTP Request
PATCH /apis/certificates.k8s.io/v1/certificatesigningrequests/{name}/approval
Parameters
  • name (in path): string, required - name of the CertificateSigningRequest
  • body: Patch, required - patch data
  • dryRun (in query): string - dry run mode
  • fieldManager (in query): string - field manager
  • fieldValidation (in query): string - field validation
  • force (in query): boolean - force update
  • pretty (in query): string - pretty print output
Return Value
  • 200 (CertificateSigningRequest): OK
  • 201 (CertificateSigningRequest): Created
  • 401: Unauthorized

patch status - partially update status of the specified CertificateSigningRequest

Partially update the status of a specific CertificateSigningRequest using strategic merge patch.

HTTP Request
PATCH /apis/certificates.k8s.io/v1/certificatesigningrequests/{name}/status
Parameters
  • name (in path): string, required - name of the CertificateSigningRequest
  • body: Patch, required - patch data
  • dryRun (in query): string - dry run mode
  • fieldManager (in query): string - field manager
  • fieldValidation (in query): string - field validation
  • force (in query): boolean - force update
  • pretty (in query): string - pretty print output
Return Value
  • 200 (CertificateSigningRequest): OK
  • 201 (CertificateSigningRequest): Created
  • 401: Unauthorized

delete - delete a CertificateSigningRequest

Delete a CertificateSigningRequest. This removes the certificate request but does not affect already issued certificates.

HTTP Request
DELETE /apis/certificates.k8s.io/v1/certificatesigningrequests/{name}
Parameters
  • name (in path): string, required - name of the CertificateSigningRequest
  • body: DeleteOptions - delete options
  • dryRun (in query): string - dry run mode
  • gracePeriodSeconds (in query): integer - grace period in seconds
  • propagationPolicy (in query): string - propagation policy
  • pretty (in query): string - pretty print output
Return Value
  • 200 (Status): OK
  • 202 (Status): Accepted
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Django Delete Examples
Example 1: Delete Skills Service Certificate Request

This example demonstrates deleting a Skills service certificate request.

# Delete Skills service certificate request
kubectl delete csr anihpj-skills-client-csr
certificatesigningrequest.certificates.k8s.io "anihpj-skills-client-csr" deleted
Example 2: Delete User Service Certificate with Grace Period

This example shows deleting User service certificate request with grace period.

# Delete User service certificate with grace period
kubectl delete csr anihpj-user-serving-csr --grace-period=30
certificatesigningrequest.certificates.k8s.io "anihpj-user-serving-csr" deleted
Example 3: Delete Author Service Certificate by Label

This example illustrates deleting Author service certificate requests using label selector.

# Delete Author service certificates by label
kubectl delete csr -l service=author,version=v1.0
certificatesigningrequest.certificates.k8s.io "anihpj-author-multi-csr" deleted
Example 4: Force Delete Location Service Certificate

This example demonstrates force deleting Location service certificate request.

# Force delete Location service certificate
kubectl delete csr anihpj-location-kubelet-csr --force --grace-period=0
warning: Immediate deletion does not wait for confirmation that the running resource has been terminated. certificatesigningrequest.certificates.k8s.io "anihpj-location-kubelet-csr" force deleted
Example 5: Delete JobPost Service Certificate from File

This example shows deleting JobPost service certificate request using YAML file.

# Delete JobPost service certificate from file
kubectl delete -f jobpost-csr.yaml
certificatesigningrequest.certificates.k8s.io "anihpj-jobpost-custom-csr" deleted

deletecollection - delete collection of CertificateSigningRequest

Delete a collection of CertificateSigningRequest objects based on label selectors and field selectors.

HTTP Request
DELETE /apis/certificates.k8s.io/v1/certificatesigningrequests
Parameters
  • body: DeleteOptions - delete options
  • continue (in query): string - continuation token
  • dryRun (in query): string - dry run mode
  • fieldSelector (in query): string - field selector
  • gracePeriodSeconds (in query): integer - grace period
  • labelSelector (in query): string - label selector
  • limit (in query): integer - limit results
  • pretty (in query): string - pretty print output
  • propagationPolicy (in query): string - propagation policy
  • resourceVersion (in query): string - resource version
Return Value
  • 200 (Status): OK
  • 401: Unauthorized

approve - approve a certificate request

Approve a CertificateSigningRequest, allowing the certificate to be issued by the configured signer.

HTTP Request
PUT /apis/certificates.k8s.io/v1/certificatesigningrequests/{name}/approval
Parameters
  • name (in path): string, required - name of the CertificateSigningRequest
  • body: CertificateSigningRequest, required - updated approval status
  • dryRun (in query): string - dry run mode
  • pretty (in query): string - pretty print output
Return Value
  • 200 (CertificateSigningRequest): OK
  • 201 (CertificateSigningRequest): Created
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Django Approve Examples
Example 1: Approve Skills Service Certificate Request

This example demonstrates approving a Skills service certificate request.

# Approve Skills service certificate request
kubectl certificate approve anihpj-skills-client-csr
certificatesigningrequest.certificates.k8s.io/anihpj-skills-client-csr approved
Example 2: Approve User Service Certificate with Reason

This example shows approving User service certificate request with custom reason.

# Approve User service certificate with reason
kubectl certificate approve anihpj-user-serving-csr --reason="SecurityReview" --message="Approved after security review"
certificatesigningrequest.certificates.k8s.io/anihpj-user-serving-csr approved
Example 3: Auto-Approve Author Service Certificate

This example illustrates auto-approving Author service certificate request.

# Auto-approve Author service certificate
kubectl certificate approve anihpj-author-multi-csr --reason="AutoApproved" --message="Auto-approved by anihpj-controller"
certificatesigningrequest.certificates.k8s.io/anihpj-author-multi-csr approved
Example 4: Approve Location Service Kubelet Certificate

This example demonstrates approving Location service kubelet certificate request.

# Approve Location service kubelet certificate
kubectl certificate approve anihpj-location-kubelet-csr
certificatesigningrequest.certificates.k8s.io/anihpj-location-kubelet-csr approved
Example 5: Approve JobPost Service Custom Certificate

This example shows approving JobPost service certificate with custom signer.

# Approve JobPost service custom certificate
kubectl certificate approve anihpj-jobpost-custom-csr --reason="ManualApproval" --message="Manually approved for production use"
certificatesigningrequest.certificates.k8s.io/anihpj-jobpost-custom-csr approved

deny - deny a certificate request

Deny a CertificateSigningRequest, preventing the certificate from being issued.

HTTP Request
PUT /apis/certificates.k8s.io/v1/certificatesigningrequests/{name}/approval
Parameters
  • name (in path): string, required - name of the CertificateSigningRequest
  • body: CertificateSigningRequest, required - updated denial status
  • dryRun (in query): string - dry run mode
  • pretty (in query): string - pretty print output
Return Value
  • 200 (CertificateSigningRequest): OK
  • 201 (CertificateSigningRequest): Created
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Django Deny Examples
Example 1: Deny Skills Service Certificate Request

This example demonstrates denying a Skills service certificate request for invalid subject.

# Deny Skills service certificate request
kubectl certificate deny anihpj-skills-client-csr --reason="InvalidSubject" --message="Subject name does not match policy"
certificatesigningrequest.certificates.k8s.io/anihpj-skills-client-csr denied
Example 2: Deny User Service Certificate for Security

This example shows denying User service certificate request due to security concerns.

# Deny User service certificate for security
kubectl certificate deny anihpj-user-serving-csr --reason="SecurityViolation" --message="Certificate request violates security policy"
certificatesigningrequest.certificates.k8s.io/anihpj-user-serving-csr denied
Example 3: Deny Author Service Certificate for Invalid Usage

This example illustrates denying Author service certificate request for invalid key usage.

# Deny Author service certificate for invalid usage
kubectl certificate deny anihpj-author-multi-csr --reason="InvalidUsage" --message="Requested key usages not permitted"
certificatesigningrequest.certificates.k8s.io/anihpj-author-multi-csr denied
Example 4: Deny Location Service Certificate for Expiration

This example demonstrates denying Location service certificate request for excessive expiration.

# Deny Location service certificate for expiration
kubectl certificate deny anihpj-location-kubelet-csr --reason="ExcessiveExpiration" --message="Requested expiration period exceeds maximum allowed"
certificatesigningrequest.certificates.k8s.io/anihpj-location-kubelet-csr denied
Example 5: Deny JobPost Service Certificate for Unauthorized Signer

This example shows denying JobPost service certificate request for unauthorized signer.

# Deny JobPost service certificate for unauthorized signer
kubectl certificate deny anihpj-jobpost-custom-csr --reason="UnauthorizedSigner" --message="Custom signer not permitted for this namespace"
certificatesigningrequest.certificates.k8s.io/anihpj-jobpost-custom-csr denied

LocalSubjectAccessReview

LocalSubjectAccessReview Overview

Operations - API operations for management
Official Docs: LocalSubjectAccessReview v1 API | API Version: authorization.k8s.io/v1

LocalSubjectAccessReview checks whether a user or ServiceAccount can perform an action in a specific namespace for namespace-level RBAC validation.

Full Description

LocalSubjectAccessReview provides a way to check if a subject (user or ServiceAccount) has permission to perform a specific action on a resource within a namespace. Unlike SubjectAccessReview which is cluster-scoped, LocalSubjectAccessReview is namespace-scoped and respects namespace-level RoleBindings.

This is particularly useful for managing authentication across all services in your anihpj Django application, allowing you to verify namespace-level permissions for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost models before granting access. By testing permissions in advance, you can ensure proper RBAC configurations and prevent runtime authorization failures.

Key Features:

  • Namespace-Scoped Checks: Verifies permissions within a specific namespace context
  • RBAC Validation: Tests Role and RoleBinding configurations for services
  • Service Permission Auditing: Validates that anihpj services have correct namespace-level permissions
  • Security Testing: Confirms permission boundaries before granting access
  • Immediate Response: Returns permission check results without modifying any resources

Syntax

# Create a LocalSubjectAccessReview from YAML file
kubectl create -f localsubjectaccessreview.yaml
# Create a LocalSubjectAccessReview using raw API
kubectl create --raw /apis/authorization.k8s.io/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/localsubjectaccessreviews -f permission-check.json
# Check if current user can create pods in namespace
kubectl auth can-i create pods -n skills
# Check if ServiceAccount can perform action
kubectl auth can-i create deployments --as=system:serviceaccount:user:anihpj-user-sa -n user

YAML Structure

apiVersion: authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: LocalSubjectAccessReview

metadata:
  name: string
  namespace: string

spec:
  resourceAttributes:
    namespace: string
    verb: string
    group: string
    version: string
    resource: string
    subresource: string
    name: string
  nonResourceAttributes:
    path: string
    verb: string
  user: string
  groups:
    - string
  extra:
    key:
      - string
  uid: string

status:
  allowed: boolean
  denied: boolean
  reason: string
  evaluationError: string

Parameters Description

  • apiVersion: string - API version, always "authorization.k8s.io/v1" for LocalSubjectAccessReview
  • kind: string - Resource type, always "LocalSubjectAccessReview"
  • metadata: ObjectMeta - Standard object metadata. Must include namespace field for namespace-scoped checks
  • spec: SubjectAccessReviewSpec (required) - Specification of the permission check to perform
  • spec.resourceAttributes: ResourceAttributes - Attributes for resource-based permission checks (namespace, verb, group, version, resource, subresource, name)
  • spec.nonResourceAttributes: NonResourceAttributes - Attributes for non-resource URL checks (path, verb)
  • spec.user: string - User to check permissions for (omit to check current user)
  • spec.groups: []string - Groups the user belongs to
  • spec.extra: map[string][]string - Additional attributes
  • spec.uid: string - UID of the user
  • status: SubjectAccessReviewStatus - Result of the permission check (populated by API server)
  • status.allowed: boolean - True if the action is allowed
  • status.denied: boolean - True if explicitly denied by policy
  • status.reason: string - Human-readable reason for the decision
  • status.evaluationError: string - Error message if evaluation failed

Return Value

  • 200 (LocalSubjectAccessReview): OK - Request successful, permission check completed
  • 201 (LocalSubjectAccessReview): Created - Permission check successfully created
  • 202 (LocalSubjectAccessReview): Accepted - Request accepted for processing
  • 401: Unauthorized - Authentication required

5 Practical Django Examples

Example 1: Check Skills Service Pod Creation Permission

This example demonstrates checking if the Skills service account can create pods in the skills namespace.

apiVersion: authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: LocalSubjectAccessReview
metadata:
  namespace: skills
spec:
  resourceAttributes:
    namespace: skills
    verb: create
    group: ""
    version: v1
    resource: pods
  user: system:serviceaccount:skills:anihpj-skills-sa
# Check Skills service pod creation permission
kubectl create -f skills-permission-check.yaml
localsubjectaccessreview.authorization.k8s.io/skills-permission-check created Status: allowed=true Reason: RBAC: allowed by RoleBinding 'skills-pod-creator' in namespace 'skills'
Example 2: Check User Service Deployment Update Permission

This example verifies if the User service can update deployments in the user namespace.

apiVersion: authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: LocalSubjectAccessReview
metadata:
  namespace: user
spec:
  resourceAttributes:
    namespace: user
    verb: update
    group: apps
    version: v1
    resource: deployments
    name: anihpj-user-api
  user: system:serviceaccount:user:anihpj-user-sa
# Check User service deployment update permission
kubectl create -f user-permission-check.yaml
kubectl describe localsubjectaccessreview -n user
localsubjectaccessreview.authorization.k8s.io/user-permission-check created Name: user-permission-check Namespace: user API Version: authorization.k8s.io/v1 Kind: LocalSubjectAccessReview Status: Allowed: true Reason: RBAC: allowed by RoleBinding 'user-deployment-manager' in namespace 'user'
Example 3: Check Author Service Secret Read Permission

This example checks if the Author service can read secrets in the author namespace.

apiVersion: authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: LocalSubjectAccessReview
metadata:
  namespace: author
spec:
  resourceAttributes:
    namespace: author
    verb: get
    group: ""
    version: v1
    resource: secrets
  user: system:serviceaccount:author:anihpj-author-sa
# Check Author service secret read permission
kubectl create -f author-permission-check.yaml -o yaml
apiVersion: authorization.k8s.io/v1 kind: LocalSubjectAccessReview metadata: namespace: author spec: resourceAttributes: namespace: author verb: get resource: secrets user: system:serviceaccount:author:anihpj-author-sa status: allowed: false denied: true reason: "RBAC: no Role or RoleBinding found in namespace 'author' for secret access"
Example 4: Check Location Service ConfigMap List Permission

This example verifies if the Location service can list ConfigMaps in the location namespace.

apiVersion: authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: LocalSubjectAccessReview
metadata:
  namespace: location
spec:
  resourceAttributes:
    namespace: location
    verb: list
    group: ""
    version: v1
    resource: configmaps
  user: system:serviceaccount:location:anihpj-location-sa
# Check Location service ConfigMap list permission
kubectl create -f location-permission-check.yaml
kubectl get localsubjectaccessreview -n location -o jsonpath='{.status}'
localsubjectaccessreview.authorization.k8s.io/location-permission-check created {"allowed":true,"reason":"RBAC: allowed by RoleBinding 'location-config-reader' in namespace 'location'"}
Example 5: Check JobPost Service StatefulSet Delete Permission

This example checks if the JobPost service can delete StatefulSets in the jobpost namespace.

apiVersion: authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: LocalSubjectAccessReview
metadata:
  namespace: jobpost
spec:
  resourceAttributes:
    namespace: jobpost
    verb: delete
    group: apps
    version: v1
    resource: statefulsets
    name: anihpj-jobpost-db
  user: system:serviceaccount:jobpost:anihpj-jobpost-sa
# Check JobPost service StatefulSet delete permission
kubectl create -f jobpost-permission-check.yaml
kubectl describe localsubjectaccessreview -n jobpost
localsubjectaccessreview.authorization.k8s.io/jobpost-permission-check created Name: jobpost-permission-check Namespace: jobpost API Version: authorization.k8s.io/v1 Kind: LocalSubjectAccessReview Status: Allowed: false Denied: true Reason: RBAC: user lacks delete permission on statefulsets in namespace 'jobpost'

LocalSubjectAccessReview Operations

Operations Overview

create - Create a LocalSubjectAccessReview

Operations allow you to interact with LocalSubjectAccessReview resources through the Kubernetes API. These operations enable you to validate namespace-level permissions for your anihpj Django application services, ensuring secure access to resources within specific namespaces.

create - create a LocalSubjectAccessReview

Create a LocalSubjectAccessReview to check if a subject can perform an action within a namespace.

HTTP Request
POST /apis/authorization.k8s.io/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/localsubjectaccessreviews
Parameters
  • namespace (in path): string, required - namespace to check permissions in
  • body: LocalSubjectAccessReview, required - the permission check specification
  • dryRun (in query): string - dry run mode
  • fieldManager (in query): string - field manager name
  • fieldValidation (in query): string - field validation policy (Strict, Warn, Ignore)
  • pretty (in query): string - pretty print output
Return Value
  • 200 (LocalSubjectAccessReview): OK
  • 201 (LocalSubjectAccessReview): Created
  • 202 (LocalSubjectAccessReview): Accepted
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: Validate Skills Service Job Creation Rights

This example demonstrates validating if Skills service can create Jobs for background processing.

apiVersion: authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: LocalSubjectAccessReview
metadata:
  namespace: skills
spec:
  resourceAttributes:
    namespace: skills
    verb: create
    group: batch
    version: v1
    resource: jobs
  user: system:serviceaccount:skills:anihpj-skills-sa
# Validate Skills service job creation rights
kubectl create -f skills-job-permission.yaml
localsubjectaccessreview.authorization.k8s.io/skills-job-permission created Status: allowed=true
Example 2: Verify User Service PVC Management Permission

This example shows verifying User service permission to manage PersistentVolumeClaims.

apiVersion: authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: LocalSubjectAccessReview
metadata:
  namespace: user
spec:
  resourceAttributes:
    namespace: user
    verb: update
    group: ""
    version: v1
    resource: persistentvolumeclaims
  user: system:serviceaccount:user:anihpj-user-sa
# Verify User service PVC management permission
kubectl create -f user-pvc-permission.yaml
kubectl describe localsubjectaccessreview -n user
localsubjectaccessreview.authorization.k8s.io/user-pvc-permission created Name: user-pvc-permission Namespace: user Status: Allowed: true Reason: RBAC: allowed by RoleBinding 'user-pvc-manager'
Example 3: Check Author Service Service Watch Permission

This example illustrates checking if Author service can watch Service resources.

apiVersion: authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: LocalSubjectAccessReview
metadata:
  namespace: author
spec:
  resourceAttributes:
    namespace: author
    verb: watch
    group: ""
    version: v1
    resource: services
  user: system:serviceaccount:author:anihpj-author-sa
# Check Author service Service watch permission
kubectl create -f author-service-watch.yaml -o yaml
apiVersion: authorization.k8s.io/v1 kind: LocalSubjectAccessReview metadata: namespace: author status: allowed: false reason: "RBAC: watch permission not granted"
Example 4: Audit Location Service Event Creation Permission

This example demonstrates auditing whether Location service can create Events.

apiVersion: authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: LocalSubjectAccessReview
metadata:
  namespace: location
spec:
  resourceAttributes:
    namespace: location
    verb: create
    group: ""
    version: v1
    resource: events
  user: system:serviceaccount:location:anihpj-location-sa
# Audit Location service event creation permission
kubectl create -f location-event-permission.yaml
kubectl get localsubjectaccessreview -n location -o jsonpath='{.status}'
localsubjectaccessreview.authorization.k8s.io/location-event-permission created {"allowed":true,"reason":"RBAC: allowed by RoleBinding 'location-event-creator'"}
{ "kind": "LocalSubjectAccessReview", "apiVersion": "authorization.k8s.io/v1", "metadata": { "creationTimestamp": null }, "spec": { "resourceAttributes": { "namespace": "location", "verb": "create", "resource": "events" }, "user": "system:serviceaccount:location:anihpj-location-logger" }, "status": { "allowed": true, "reason": "RBAC: allowed by RoleBinding 'location-event-creator'" } }
Example 5: Test JobPost Service ReplicaSet Scale Permission

This example shows testing JobPost service permission to scale ReplicaSets.

apiVersion: authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: LocalSubjectAccessReview
metadata:
  namespace: jobpost
spec:
  resourceAttributes:
    namespace: jobpost
    verb: update
    group: apps
    version: v1
    resource: replicasets
    subresource: scale
  user: system:serviceaccount:jobpost:anihpj-jobpost-sa
# Test JobPost service ReplicaSet scale permission
kubectl create -f jobpost-scale-permission.yaml
kubectl describe localsubjectaccessreview -n jobpost
localsubjectaccessreview.authorization.k8s.io/jobpost-scale-permission created Name: jobpost-scale-permission Namespace: jobpost Status: Allowed: true Reason: RBAC: allowed by RoleBinding 'jobpost-scaler'

SelfSubjectAccessReview

SelfSubjectAccessReview Overview

SelfSubjectAccessReviewSpec - Access request specification
Operations - API operations for management
Official Docs: SelfSubjectAccessReview v1 API | API Version: authorization.k8s.io/v1

SelfSubjectAccessReview checks whether the current user can perform an action, enabling self-service permission validation.

Full Description

SelfSubjectAccessReview provides a way for users to check their own permissions before attempting actions. Unlike SubjectAccessReview and LocalSubjectAccessReview which check permissions for other users, SelfSubjectAccessReview is specifically for the current authenticated user to validate their own access rights.

This is particularly useful for your anihpj Django application where services need to verify their own capabilities before performing operations. For example, the Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost services can check if they have permission to create, update, or delete resources without actually attempting the action. Not specifying a namespace means "in all namespaces," making it flexible for both cluster-wide and namespace-scoped checks.

Key Features:

  • Self-Permission Checks: Users validate their own permissions without admin privileges
  • Cluster-Wide or Namespace-Scoped: Check permissions across all namespaces or within specific ones
  • Pre-Action Validation: Verify access before attempting operations to prevent errors
  • Service Account Auditing: ServiceAccounts can check their own RBAC permissions
  • No Side Effects: Permission checks don't modify any resources or leave audit trails

Syntax

# Check if current user can create pods
kubectl auth can-i create pods
# Check if current user can delete deployments in specific namespace
kubectl auth can-i delete deployments -n skills
# Check if current user can list secrets cluster-wide
kubectl auth can-i list secrets --all-namespaces
# Create SelfSubjectAccessReview from YAML file
kubectl create -f selfsubjectaccessreview.yaml

YAML Structure

apiVersion: authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: SelfSubjectAccessReview

metadata:
  name: string

spec:
  resourceAttributes:
    namespace: string
    verb: string
    group: string
    version: string
    resource: string
    subresource: string
    name: string
  nonResourceAttributes:
    path: string
    verb: string

status:
  allowed: boolean
  denied: boolean
  reason: string
  evaluationError: string

Parameters Description

  • apiVersion: string - API version, always "authorization.k8s.io/v1" for SelfSubjectAccessReview
  • kind: string - Resource type, always "SelfSubjectAccessReview"
  • metadata: ObjectMeta - Standard object metadata
  • spec: SelfSubjectAccessReviewSpec (required) - Specification of the access request for the current user
  • spec.resourceAttributes: ResourceAttributes - Attributes for resource-based permission checks (namespace, verb, group, version, resource, subresource, name)
  • spec.nonResourceAttributes: NonResourceAttributes - Attributes for non-resource URL checks (path, verb)
  • status: SubjectAccessReviewStatus - Result of the permission check (populated by API server)
  • status.allowed: boolean - True if the action is allowed
  • status.denied: boolean - True if explicitly denied by policy
  • status.reason: string - Human-readable reason for the decision
  • status.evaluationError: string - Error message if evaluation failed

Return Value

  • 200 (SelfSubjectAccessReview): OK - Request successful, permission check completed
  • 201 (SelfSubjectAccessReview): Created - Permission check successfully created
  • 202 (SelfSubjectAccessReview): Accepted - Request accepted for processing
  • 401: Unauthorized - Authentication required

5 Practical Django Examples

Example 1: Check Skills Service Pod Creation Permission

This example demonstrates the Skills service checking if it can create pods in its namespace.

apiVersion: authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: SelfSubjectAccessReview
metadata:
  name: skills-pod-check
spec:
  resourceAttributes:
    namespace: skills
    verb: create
    group: ""
    version: v1
    resource: pods
# Check Skills service pod creation permission
kubectl create -f skills-pod-check.yaml
selfsubjectaccessreview.authorization.k8s.io/skills-pod-check created Status: allowed=true Reason: RBAC: allowed by RoleBinding 'skills-pod-creator'
Example 2: Check User Service Deployment Management Permission

This example shows the User service verifying deployment update permissions.

apiVersion: authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: SelfSubjectAccessReview
metadata:
  name: user-deployment-check
spec:
  resourceAttributes:
    namespace: user
    verb: update
    group: apps
    version: v1
    resource: deployments
# Check User service deployment management permission
kubectl create -f user-deployment-check.yaml
kubectl describe selfsubjectaccessreview user-deployment-check
selfsubjectaccessreview.authorization.k8s.io/user-deployment-check created Name: user-deployment-check API Version: authorization.k8s.io/v1 Kind: SelfSubjectAccessReview Status: Allowed: true Reason: RBAC: allowed by RoleBinding 'user-deployment-manager'
Example 3: Check Author Service Secret Read Permission

This example illustrates the Author service checking secret access permissions.

apiVersion: authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: SelfSubjectAccessReview
metadata:
  name: author-secret-check
spec:
  resourceAttributes:
    namespace: author
    verb: get
    group: ""
    version: v1
    resource: secrets
# Check Author service secret read permission
kubectl create -f author-secret-check.yaml -o yaml
apiVersion: authorization.k8s.io/v1 kind: SelfSubjectAccessReview metadata: name: author-secret-check spec: resourceAttributes: namespace: author verb: get resource: secrets status: allowed: false denied: true reason: "RBAC: no Role or RoleBinding found for secret access"
Example 4: Check Location Service ConfigMap List Permission

This example demonstrates the Location service validating ConfigMap listing permissions.

apiVersion: authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: SelfSubjectAccessReview
metadata:
  name: location-configmap-check
spec:
  resourceAttributes:
    namespace: location
    verb: list
    group: ""
    version: v1
    resource: configmaps
# Check Location service ConfigMap list permission
kubectl create -f location-configmap-check.yaml
kubectl get selfsubjectaccessreview location-configmap-check -o jsonpath='{.status}'
selfsubjectaccessreview.authorization.k8s.io/location-configmap-check created {"allowed":true,"reason":"RBAC: allowed by RoleBinding 'location-config-reader'"}
Example 5: Check JobPost Service Cluster-Wide Resource Access

This example shows the JobPost service checking permissions across all namespaces.

apiVersion: authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: SelfSubjectAccessReview
metadata:
  name: jobpost-cluster-check
spec:
  resourceAttributes:
    verb: list
    group: ""
    version: v1
    resource: namespaces
# Check JobPost service cluster-wide namespace list permission
kubectl create -f jobpost-cluster-check.yaml
kubectl describe selfsubjectaccessreview jobpost-cluster-check
selfsubjectaccessreview.authorization.k8s.io/jobpost-cluster-check created Name: jobpost-cluster-check API Version: authorization.k8s.io/v1 Kind: SelfSubjectAccessReview Status: Allowed: true Reason: RBAC: allowed by ClusterRoleBinding 'jobpost-namespace-reader'

SelfSubjectAccessReviewSpec

Official Docs: SelfSubjectAccessReviewSpec v1 API | API Version: authorization.k8s.io/v1

SelfSubjectAccessReviewSpec describes the access request for the current user.

Full Description

SelfSubjectAccessReviewSpec is the specification of the permission check request for the current authenticated user. It defines what action the user wants to check permission for, using either resourceAttributes for Kubernetes resource access or nonResourceAttributes for non-resource URL paths. Exactly one of these must be set.

For your anihpj Django application services, this spec allows each service to define precisely what permission they want to verify before attempting operations, ensuring smoother operation and better error handling.

Parameters Description

  • resourceAttributes: ResourceAttributes - Describes attributes for resource-based access checks (namespace, verb, group, version, resource, subresource, name, fieldSelector, labelSelector)
  • nonResourceAttributes: NonResourceAttributes - Describes attributes for non-resource URL access checks (path, verb)

Return Value

  • SelfSubjectAccessReviewSpec: Specification object defining the permission check request

5 Practical Django Examples

Example 1: Skills Service Resource Check Specification

This example demonstrates creating a spec for Skills service Job creation check.

spec:
  resourceAttributes:
    namespace: skills
    verb: create
    group: batch
    version: v1
    resource: jobs
# Skills service checks Job creation permission
kubectl auth can-i create jobs -n skills
yes
Example 2: User Service Specific Resource Check

This example shows a spec for checking access to a specific User deployment.

spec:
  resourceAttributes:
    namespace: user
    verb: delete
    group: apps
    version: v1
    resource: deployments
    name: anihpj-user-api
# User service checks specific deployment deletion permission
kubectl auth can-i delete deployment/anihpj-user-api -n user
no
Example 3: Author Service Non-Resource URL Check

This example illustrates a spec for checking API endpoint access.

spec:
  nonResourceAttributes:
    path: /api/v1
    verb: get
# Author service checks API endpoint access
kubectl auth can-i get /api/v1
yes
Example 4: Location Service Subresource Check

This example demonstrates checking access to a resource subresource like logs.

spec:
  resourceAttributes:
    namespace: location
    verb: get
    group: ""
    version: v1
    resource: pods
    subresource: log
# Location service checks pod log access
kubectl auth can-i get pods/log -n location
yes
Example 5: JobPost Service Cluster-Wide Check Specification

This example shows a spec for checking permissions without namespace restriction.

spec:
  resourceAttributes:
    verb: list
    group: ""
    version: v1
    resource: persistentvolumes
# JobPost service checks cluster-wide PV listing
kubectl auth can-i list persistentvolumes --all-namespaces
yes

SelfSubjectAccessReview Operations

Operations Overview

create - Create a SelfSubjectAccessReview

Operations allow you to interact with SelfSubjectAccessReview resources through the Kubernetes API. These operations enable your anihpj Django application services to validate their own permissions before performing actions, ensuring secure and error-free operations.

create - create a SelfSubjectAccessReview

Create a SelfSubjectAccessReview to check if the current user can perform an action.

HTTP Request
POST /apis/authorization.k8s.io/v1/selfsubjectaccessreviews
Parameters
  • body: SelfSubjectAccessReview, required - the permission check specification
  • dryRun (in query): string - dry run mode
  • fieldManager (in query): string - field manager name
  • fieldValidation (in query): string - field validation policy (Strict, Warn, Ignore)
  • pretty (in query): string - pretty print output
Return Value
  • 200 (SelfSubjectAccessReview): OK
  • 201 (SelfSubjectAccessReview): Created
  • 202 (SelfSubjectAccessReview): Accepted
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: Skills Service Validates StatefulSet Creation

This example demonstrates the Skills service checking if it can create StatefulSets.

apiVersion: authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: SelfSubjectAccessReview
metadata:
  name: skills-statefulset-create
spec:
  resourceAttributes:
    namespace: skills
    verb: create
    group: apps
    version: v1
    resource: statefulsets
# Skills service validates StatefulSet creation permission
kubectl create -f skills-statefulset-create.yaml
selfsubjectaccessreview.authorization.k8s.io/skills-statefulset-create created Status: allowed=true
Example 2: User Service Checks PVC Management Permission

This example shows the User service verifying PersistentVolumeClaim update rights.

apiVersion: authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: SelfSubjectAccessReview
metadata:
  name: user-pvc-update
spec:
  resourceAttributes:
    namespace: user
    verb: update
    group: ""
    version: v1
    resource: persistentvolumeclaims
# User service checks PVC update permission
kubectl create -f user-pvc-update.yaml
kubectl describe selfsubjectaccessreview user-pvc-update
selfsubjectaccessreview.authorization.k8s.io/user-pvc-update created Name: user-pvc-update Status: Allowed: true Reason: RBAC: allowed by RoleBinding 'user-storage-manager'
Example 3: Author Service Validates Service Watch Permission

This example illustrates the Author service checking if it can watch Services.

apiVersion: authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: SelfSubjectAccessReview
metadata:
  name: author-service-watch
spec:
  resourceAttributes:
    namespace: author
    verb: watch
    group: ""
    version: v1
    resource: services
# Author service validates Service watch permission
kubectl create -f author-service-watch.yaml -o yaml
apiVersion: authorization.k8s.io/v1 kind: SelfSubjectAccessReview metadata: name: author-service-watch status: allowed: false reason: "RBAC: watch permission not granted"
Example 4: Location Service Audits Event Creation Rights

This example demonstrates the Location service checking Event creation permission.

apiVersion: authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: SelfSubjectAccessReview
metadata:
  name: location-event-create
spec:
  resourceAttributes:
    namespace: location
    verb: create
    group: ""
    version: v1
    resource: events
# Location service audits Event creation rights
kubectl create -f location-event-create.yaml
kubectl get selfsubjectaccessreview location-event-create -o jsonpath='{.status}'
selfsubjectaccessreview.authorization.k8s.io/location-event-create created {"allowed":true,"reason":"RBAC: allowed by RoleBinding 'location-event-creator'"}
Example 5: JobPost Service Tests DaemonSet Deletion Permission

This example shows the JobPost service checking DaemonSet deletion rights.

apiVersion: authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: SelfSubjectAccessReview
metadata:
  name: jobpost-daemonset-delete
spec:
  resourceAttributes:
    namespace: jobpost
    verb: delete
    group: apps
    version: v1
    resource: daemonsets
# JobPost service tests DaemonSet deletion permission
kubectl create -f jobpost-daemonset-delete.yaml
kubectl describe selfsubjectaccessreview jobpost-daemonset-delete
selfsubjectaccessreview.authorization.k8s.io/jobpost-daemonset-delete created Name: jobpost-daemonset-delete Status: Allowed: false Denied: true Reason: RBAC: delete permission not granted for daemonsets

SelfSubjectRulesReview

SelfSubjectRulesReview Overview

SelfSubjectRulesReviewSpec - Request specification
Operations - API operations for management
Official Docs: SelfSubjectRulesReview v1 API | API Version: authorization.k8s.io/v1

SelfSubjectRulesReview enumerates the set of actions the current user can perform within a namespace.

Full Description

SelfSubjectRulesReview provides a comprehensive list of all actions (rules) that the current authenticated user can perform within a specific namespace. This is particularly useful for UIs to show or hide actions based on user permissions, and for users to quickly understand what they're allowed to do.

For your anihpj Django application, this resource helps services discover their complete permission sets within namespaces. For example, the Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost services can query their full list of allowed actions, making it easier to implement dynamic UI behaviors and permission-aware features. The returned list may be incomplete depending on the server's authorization mode.

Important Note: SelfSubjectRulesReview is designed for UI display and user reasoning, NOT for driving authorization decisions by external systems. For authorization decisions, use SubjectAccessReview or LocalSubjectAccessReview instead.

Key Features:

  • Complete Rule Enumeration: Lists all allowed actions for the current user in a namespace
  • Resource and Non-Resource Rules: Covers both Kubernetes resource actions and API endpoint access
  • UI-Friendly: Perfect for showing/hiding UI elements based on permissions
  • Self-Discovery: Users can discover their own permissions without admin help
  • Incomplete Indication: Flags when the rule list may not be complete

Syntax

# Create SelfSubjectRulesReview from YAML file
kubectl create -f selfsubjectrulesreview.yaml
# View the returned rules
kubectl describe selfsubjectrulesreview [name]
# Check rules in JSON format
kubectl create -f selfsubjectrulesreview.yaml -o json

YAML Structure

apiVersion: authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: SelfSubjectRulesReview

metadata:
  name: string

spec:
  namespace: string

status:
  incomplete: boolean
  resourceRules:
  - verbs: []string
    apiGroups: []string
    resources: []string
    resourceNames: []string
  nonResourceRules:
  - verbs: []string
    nonResourceURLs: []string
  evaluationError: string

Parameters Description

  • apiVersion: string - API version, always "authorization.k8s.io/v1" for SelfSubjectRulesReview
  • kind: string - Resource type, always "SelfSubjectRulesReview"
  • metadata: ObjectMeta - Standard object metadata
  • spec: SelfSubjectRulesReviewSpec (required) - Specification of the rules review request
  • spec.namespace: string (required) - Namespace to evaluate rules for
  • status: SubjectRulesReviewStatus - Result of the rules check (populated by API server)
  • status.incomplete: boolean (required) - True when returned rules are incomplete
  • status.resourceRules: []ResourceRule (required) - List of allowed actions on resources
  • status.nonResourceRules: []NonResourceRule (required) - List of allowed actions on non-resources
  • status.evaluationError: string - Error message if rule evaluation failed

Return Value

  • 200 (SelfSubjectRulesReview): OK - Request successful, rules enumerated
  • 201 (SelfSubjectRulesReview): Created - Rules review successfully created
  • 202 (SelfSubjectRulesReview): Accepted - Request accepted for processing
  • 401: Unauthorized - Authentication required

5 Practical Django Examples

Example 1: Skills Service Discovers All Permissions

This example demonstrates the Skills service enumerating all its permissions in the skills namespace.

apiVersion: authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: SelfSubjectRulesReview
metadata:
  name: skills-rules-check
spec:
  namespace: skills
# Skills service discovers all permissions
kubectl create -f skills-rules-check.yaml -o yaml
apiVersion: authorization.k8s.io/v1 kind: SelfSubjectRulesReview metadata: name: skills-rules-check spec: namespace: skills status: incomplete: false resourceRules: - verbs: ["get", "list", "watch", "create", "update"] apiGroups: [""] resources: ["pods", "services", "configmaps"] - verbs: ["get", "list"] apiGroups: ["apps"] resources: ["deployments", "replicasets"] nonResourceRules: - verbs: ["get"] nonResourceURLs: ["/api", "/api/*"]
Example 2: User Service Checks Deployment Permissions

This example shows the User service checking its complete rule set for deployments.

apiVersion: authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: SelfSubjectRulesReview
metadata:
  name: user-rules-check
spec:
  namespace: user
# User service checks all permissions
kubectl create -f user-rules-check.yaml
kubectl describe selfsubjectrulesreview user-rules-check
selfsubjectrulesreview.authorization.k8s.io/user-rules-check created Name: user-rules-check API Version: authorization.k8s.io/v1 Kind: SelfSubjectRulesReview Status: Incomplete: false Resource Rules: Verbs: ["get", "list", "watch", "create", "update", "patch", "delete"] API Groups: ["apps"] Resources: ["deployments", "statefulsets"] Non Resource Rules: Verbs: ["get", "post"] Non Resource URLs: ["/apis", "/apis/*"]
Example 3: Author Service with Incomplete Rules

This example illustrates an incomplete rules response from an external authorizer.

apiVersion: authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: SelfSubjectRulesReview
metadata:
  name: author-rules-check
spec:
  namespace: author
# Author service gets incomplete rules
kubectl create -f author-rules-check.yaml -o yaml
apiVersion: authorization.k8s.io/v1 kind: SelfSubjectRulesReview metadata: name: author-rules-check status: incomplete: true resourceRules: - verbs: ["get", "list"] apiGroups: [""] resources: ["secrets", "configmaps"] evaluationError: "external authorizer does not support rules evaluation"
Example 4: Location Service Checks Storage Permissions

This example demonstrates the Location service discovering its storage-related permissions.

apiVersion: authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: SelfSubjectRulesReview
metadata:
  name: location-rules-check
spec:
  namespace: location
# Location service checks storage permissions
kubectl create -f location-rules-check.yaml
kubectl get selfsubjectrulesreview location-rules-check -o jsonpath='{.status}'
selfsubjectrulesreview.authorization.k8s.io/location-rules-check created {"incomplete":false,"resourceRules":[{"verbs":["create","get","list","watch"],"apiGroups":[""],"resources":["persistentvolumeclaims"]},{"verbs":["get","list"],"apiGroups":["storage.k8s.io"],"resources":["storageclasses"]}]}
Example 5: JobPost Service Discovers All Namespace Rules

This example shows the JobPost service enumerating comprehensive permissions.

apiVersion: authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: SelfSubjectRulesReview
metadata:
  name: jobpost-rules-check
spec:
  namespace: jobpost
# JobPost service discovers all rules
kubectl create -f jobpost-rules-check.yaml
kubectl describe selfsubjectrulesreview jobpost-rules-check
selfsubjectrulesreview.authorization.k8s.io/jobpost-rules-check created Name: jobpost-rules-check API Version: authorization.k8s.io/v1 Kind: SelfSubjectRulesReview Status: Incomplete: false Resource Rules: Verbs: ["*"] API Groups: ["", "apps", "batch"] Resources: ["*"] Non Resource Rules: Verbs: ["get", "post", "put", "delete"] Non Resource URLs: ["*"]

SelfSubjectRulesReviewSpec

Official Docs: SelfSubjectRulesReviewSpec v1 API | API Version: authorization.k8s.io/v1

SelfSubjectRulesReviewSpec defines the specification for SelfSubjectRulesReview.

Full Description

SelfSubjectRulesReviewSpec is the specification for a rules review request. It contains only one field: the namespace in which to evaluate the current user's permissions. This simple spec makes it easy to discover all allowed actions within a specific namespace scope.

For your anihpj Django application services, this spec allows each service to specify which namespace they want to check permissions for, enabling namespace-scoped permission discovery for better access control management.

Parameters Description

  • namespace: string (required) - The namespace to evaluate rules for

Return Value

  • SelfSubjectRulesReviewSpec: Specification object defining the namespace for rules evaluation

5 Practical Django Examples

Example 1: Skills Namespace Rules Specification

This example demonstrates a spec for checking Skills namespace permissions.

spec:
  namespace: skills
# Skills service specifies its namespace
kubectl create -f skills-rules-spec.yaml
selfsubjectrulesreview.authorization.k8s.io/skills-rules-spec created
Example 2: User Namespace Rules Specification

This example shows a spec targeting the User namespace.

spec:
  namespace: user
# User service checks its namespace rules
kubectl create -f user-rules-spec.yaml
selfsubjectrulesreview.authorization.k8s.io/user-rules-spec created
Example 3: Author Namespace Rules Specification

This example illustrates a spec for the Author namespace.

spec:
  namespace: author
# Author service enumerates its permissions
kubectl create -f author-rules-spec.yaml
selfsubjectrulesreview.authorization.k8s.io/author-rules-spec created
Example 4: Location Namespace Rules Specification

This example demonstrates a spec for Location namespace permission discovery.

spec:
  namespace: location
# Location service discovers its rules
kubectl create -f location-rules-spec.yaml
selfsubjectrulesreview.authorization.k8s.io/location-rules-spec created
Example 5: JobPost Namespace Rules Specification

This example shows a spec for comprehensive JobPost namespace rules.

spec:
  namespace: jobpost
# JobPost service checks all its permissions
kubectl create -f jobpost-rules-spec.yaml
selfsubjectrulesreview.authorization.k8s.io/jobpost-rules-spec created

SelfSubjectRulesReview Operations

Operations Overview

create - Create a SelfSubjectRulesReview

Operations allow you to interact with SelfSubjectRulesReview resources through the Kubernetes API. These operations enable your anihpj Django application services to discover their complete permission sets within namespaces, facilitating permission-aware UI behaviors and access control management.

create - create a SelfSubjectRulesReview

Create a SelfSubjectRulesReview to enumerate all actions the current user can perform in a namespace.

HTTP Request
POST /apis/authorization.k8s.io/v1/selfsubjectrulesreviews
Parameters
  • body: SelfSubjectRulesReview, required - the rules review specification
  • dryRun (in query): string - dry run mode
  • fieldManager (in query): string - field manager name
  • fieldValidation (in query): string - field validation policy (Strict, Warn, Ignore)
  • pretty (in query): string - pretty print output
Return Value
  • 200 (SelfSubjectRulesReview): OK
  • 201 (SelfSubjectRulesReview): Created
  • 202 (SelfSubjectRulesReview): Accepted
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: Skills Service Enumerates Pod Management Rules

This example demonstrates the Skills service discovering all pod-related permissions.

apiVersion: authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: SelfSubjectRulesReview
metadata:
  name: skills-pod-rules
spec:
  namespace: skills
# Skills service discovers pod management rules
kubectl create -f skills-pod-rules.yaml -o yaml
apiVersion: authorization.k8s.io/v1 kind: SelfSubjectRulesReview metadata: name: skills-pod-rules status: incomplete: false resourceRules: - verbs: ["create", "get", "list", "watch", "delete"] apiGroups: [""] resources: ["pods", "pods/log"]
Example 2: User Service Checks All Deployment Permissions

This example shows the User service getting comprehensive deployment rules.

apiVersion: authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: SelfSubjectRulesReview
metadata:
  name: user-deployment-rules
spec:
  namespace: user
# User service enumerates deployment rules
kubectl create -f user-deployment-rules.yaml
kubectl describe selfsubjectrulesreview user-deployment-rules
selfsubjectrulesreview.authorization.k8s.io/user-deployment-rules created Name: user-deployment-rules Status: Incomplete: false Resource Rules: Verbs: ["get", "list", "watch", "create", "update", "patch"] API Groups: ["apps"] Resources: ["deployments", "deployments/scale"]
Example 3: Author Service Discovers Secret Access Rules

This example illustrates the Author service checking its secret-related permissions.

apiVersion: authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: SelfSubjectRulesReview
metadata:
  name: author-secret-rules
spec:
  namespace: author
# Author service checks secret rules
kubectl create -f author-secret-rules.yaml -o yaml
apiVersion: authorization.k8s.io/v1 kind: SelfSubjectRulesReview metadata: name: author-secret-rules status: incomplete: false resourceRules: - verbs: ["get", "list"] apiGroups: [""] resources: ["secrets"] - verbs: ["get", "list", "watch"] apiGroups: [""] resources: ["configmaps"]
Example 4: Location Service Enumerates Storage Rules

This example demonstrates the Location service discovering all storage permissions.

apiVersion: authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: SelfSubjectRulesReview
metadata:
  name: location-storage-rules
spec:
  namespace: location
# Location service checks storage rules
kubectl create -f location-storage-rules.yaml
kubectl get selfsubjectrulesreview location-storage-rules -o jsonpath='{.status.resourceRules}'
selfsubjectrulesreview.authorization.k8s.io/location-storage-rules created [{"verbs":["create","get","list","watch","delete"],"apiGroups":[""],"resources":["persistentvolumeclaims"]},{"verbs":["get","list"],"apiGroups":["storage.k8s.io"],"resources":["storageclasses","volumeattachments"]}]
Example 5: JobPost Service Gets Complete Permission Set

This example shows the JobPost service discovering all its allowed actions.

apiVersion: authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: SelfSubjectRulesReview
metadata:
  name: jobpost-all-rules
spec:
  namespace: jobpost
# JobPost service gets complete rules
kubectl create -f jobpost-all-rules.yaml
kubectl describe selfsubjectrulesreview jobpost-all-rules
selfsubjectrulesreview.authorization.k8s.io/jobpost-all-rules created Name: jobpost-all-rules Status: Incomplete: false Resource Rules: Verbs: ["*"] API Groups: ["", "apps", "batch"] Resources: ["*"] Non Resource Rules: Verbs: ["get", "post", "put", "delete"] Non Resource URLs: ["*"]

SubjectAccessReview

SubjectAccessReview Overview

SubjectAccessReviewSpec - Access request parameters
SubjectAccessReviewStatus - Authorization decision
Operations - API operations for management
Official Docs: SubjectAccessReview v1 API | API Version: authorization.k8s.io/v1
