SubjectAccessReview checks whether a specified user, group, or service account can perform an action - privileged operation for administrators.

Full Description

SubjectAccessReview checks whether or not a specified user, group, or service account can perform an action. Unlike SelfSubjectAccessReview which only checks the current user's permissions, SubjectAccessReview is a privileged operation that allows administrators to verify what actions other subjects can perform. This is commonly used by administrators to validate RBAC configurations, troubleshoot permission issues, and verify that security policies are correctly enforced before granting access to resources.

In your anihpj Django application, cluster administrators can use SubjectAccessReview to verify permissions for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost service accounts before deploying critical workloads. This ensures that RBAC policies are correctly configured and that services have exactly the permissions they need—no more, no less—following the principle of least privilege for enhanced security.

Syntax

# Create a SubjectAccessReview from YAML file
kubectl create -f subjectaccessreview.yaml
# Check if a user can perform an action
kubectl auth can-i --list --as={user}
# Check specific permission for a user
kubectl auth can-i {verb} {resource} --as={user}
# Check permission in specific namespace
kubectl auth can-i {verb} {resource} --as={user} -n {namespace}

YAML Structure

apiVersion: authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: SubjectAccessReview
metadata:
  name: check-user-permissions
spec:
  user: system:serviceaccount:default:anihpj-skills-sa
  groups:
  - system:serviceaccounts
  - system:authenticated
  resourceAttributes:
    namespace: default
    verb: create
    group: apps
    version: v1
    resource: deployments
status:
  allowed: true
  reason: "RBAC: allowed by RoleBinding"

Parameters Description

  • spec.user: User identity to check permissions for. If specified without groups, checks permissions as if user has no group memberships.
  • spec.groups: Groups the user belongs to for authorization check. Corresponds to user.Info.GetGroups().
  • spec.extra: Additional information about the user. Corresponds to user.Info.GetExtra() from the authenticator.
  • spec.uid: UID information about the requesting user.
  • spec.resourceAttributes: Describes resource access request (mutually exclusive with nonResourceAttributes).
  • spec.nonResourceAttributes: Describes non-resource access request like /api or /healthz (mutually exclusive with resourceAttributes).
  • status.allowed: True if the action would be allowed, false otherwise (required).
  • status.denied: True if action explicitly denied. Can be false while allowed is also false if authorizer has no opinion.
  • status.reason: Human-readable explanation for authorization decision.
  • status.evaluationError: Indicates errors during authorization check. Authorization may still be determined despite errors.

Return Value

  • 200 (SubjectAccessReview): OK - Request successful, SubjectAccessReview returned
  • 201 (SubjectAccessReview): Created - SubjectAccessReview successfully created
  • 202 (SubjectAccessReview): Accepted - Request accepted for processing
  • 401: Unauthorized - Authentication required

5 Practical Django Examples

Example 1: Skills Admin Checks User Service Permissions

This example shows the Skills service admin verifying whether the User service can create JobPost resources.

# skills-check-user.yaml
apiVersion: authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: SubjectAccessReview
metadata:
  name: skills-check-user
spec:
  user: system:serviceaccount:default:anihpj-user-sa
  resourceAttributes:
    namespace: default
    verb: create
    group: apps
    version: v1
    resource: jobposts
# Skills admin checks User service permissions
kubectl create -f skills-check-user.yaml
kubectl get subjectaccessreview skills-check-user -o yaml
subjectaccessreview.authorization.k8s.io/skills-check-user created status: allowed: true reason: "RBAC: allowed by ClusterRoleBinding 'anihpj-user-sa-binding' of ClusterRole 'jobpost-creator'"
Example 2: Location Admin Validates Author Service Access

This example demonstrates the Location service admin checking whether Author service can list Location resources.

# location-check-author.yaml
apiVersion: authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: SubjectAccessReview
metadata:
  name: location-check-author
spec:
  user: system:serviceaccount:default:anihpj-author-sa
  groups:
    - system:serviceaccounts
    - system:serviceaccounts:default
  resourceAttributes:
    namespace: default
    verb: list
    group: ""
    version: v1
    resource: locations
# Location admin validates Author service access
kubectl create -f location-check-author.yaml
kubectl get subjectaccessreview location-check-author -o jsonpath='{.status}'
subjectaccessreview.authorization.k8s.io/location-check-author created {"allowed":true,"reason":"RBAC: allowed by RoleBinding 'location-reader/default' of Role 'location-reader'"}
Example 3: JobPost Admin Verifies Skills Service Update Rights

This example shows the JobPost admin checking if Skills service can update specific JobPost resources.

# jobpost-check-skills.yaml
apiVersion: authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: SubjectAccessReview
metadata:
  name: jobpost-check-skills
spec:
  user: system:serviceaccount:jobpost:anihpj-skills-sa
  resourceAttributes:
    namespace: jobpost
    verb: update
    group: batch
    version: v1
    resource: jobs
    name: skills-processing-job
# JobPost admin verifies Skills service update rights
kubectl create -f jobpost-check-skills.yaml
kubectl describe subjectaccessreview jobpost-check-skills
subjectaccessreview.authorization.k8s.io/jobpost-check-skills created Name: jobpost-check-skills Spec: User: system:serviceaccount:jobpost:anihpj-skills-sa Resource Attributes: Namespace: jobpost Verb: update Resource: jobs Name: skills-processing-job Status: Allowed: false Denied: true Reason: RBAC: User does not have update permissions on jobs/skills-processing-job
Example 4: User Admin Checks Group Permissions

This example demonstrates checking permissions for a user with specific group memberships.

# user-group-check.yaml
apiVersion: authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: SubjectAccessReview
metadata:
  name: user-group-check
spec:
  user: john-developer
  groups:
    - developers
    - anihpj-team
  resourceAttributes:
    namespace: default
    verb: delete
    group: ""
    version: v1
    resource: pods
# User admin checks group-based permissions
kubectl create -f user-group-check.yaml
kubectl get subjectaccessreview user-group-check -o jsonpath='{.status.allowed}'
subjectaccessreview.authorization.k8s.io/user-group-check created true
Example 5: Author Admin Checks Non-Resource URL Access

This example shows verifying access to non-resource endpoints like API discovery URLs.

# author-api-check.yaml
apiVersion: authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: SubjectAccessReview
metadata:
  name: author-api-check
spec:
  user: system:serviceaccount:default:anihpj-author-sa
  nonResourceAttributes:
    path: /apis/batch/v1
    verb: get
# Author admin checks API discovery access
kubectl create -f author-api-check.yaml
kubectl get subjectaccessreview author-api-check -o yaml
subjectaccessreview.authorization.k8s.io/author-api-check created status: allowed: true reason: "RBAC: allowed by ClusterRoleBinding 'system:discovery' of ClusterRole 'system:discovery'"

SubjectAccessReviewSpec

Official Docs: SubjectAccessReviewSpec v1 API | API Version: authorization.k8s.io/v1

SubjectAccessReviewSpec describes the access request being evaluated for authorization.

Full Description

SubjectAccessReviewSpec is a description of the access request being evaluated. It specifies the subject (user, groups, service account) and the action they want to perform. Exactly one of resourceAttributes or nonResourceAttributes must be set - resourceAttributes for checking access to Kubernetes resources (like pods, deployments) and nonResourceAttributes for checking access to non-resource URLs (like /api, /healthz, /metrics). The spec includes user identity information, group memberships, and optional extra attributes that may be used by custom authorizers.

Parameters Description

  • user: string - User identity to check permissions for. If specified without groups, checks as if user has no groups
  • groups: []string - Groups the user belongs to. Corresponds to user.Info.GetGroups() from authenticator
  • extra: map[string][]string - Extra information about the user. Corresponds to user.Info.GetExtra() method
  • uid: string - UID information about the requesting user
  • resourceAttributes: ResourceAttributes - Information for resource access request (mutually exclusive with nonResourceAttributes)
  • resourceAttributes.namespace: string - Namespace of the resource. Empty means "all" for cluster-scoped or all namespaces
  • resourceAttributes.verb: string - Kubernetes API verb: get, list, watch, create, update, delete, patch, proxy. "*" means all
  • resourceAttributes.group: string - API Group of the resource. "*" means all. Empty for core API
  • resourceAttributes.version: string - API Version of the resource. "*" means all
  • resourceAttributes.resource: string - Resource type (pods, deployments, etc). "*" means all
  • resourceAttributes.subresource: string - Subresource (status, scale, etc). Empty means none
  • resourceAttributes.name: string - Name of specific resource instance. Empty means all
  • nonResourceAttributes: NonResourceAttributes - Information for non-resource URL access (mutually exclusive with resourceAttributes)
  • nonResourceAttributes.path: string - URL path of request (/api, /healthz, /metrics)
  • nonResourceAttributes.verb: string - Standard HTTP verb (get, post, put, delete, patch)

Return Value

  • Part of SubjectAccessReview - SubjectAccessReviewSpec is processed by the server which populates the status field

5 Practical Django Examples

Example 1: Skills Service Checks User Service Pod Access

This example shows checking if User service can get pods in the default namespace.

# skills-check-user-pods.yaml
apiVersion: authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: SubjectAccessReview
metadata:
  name: skills-check-user-pods
spec:
  user: system:serviceaccount:default:anihpj-user-sa
  groups:
    - system:serviceaccounts
    - system:serviceaccounts:default
  resourceAttributes:
    namespace: default
    verb: get
    group: ""
    version: v1
    resource: pods
# Check User service pod access
kubectl create -f skills-check-user-pods.yaml
kubectl get subjectaccessreview skills-check-user-pods -o jsonpath='{.spec}'
subjectaccessreview.authorization.k8s.io/skills-check-user-pods created {"groups":["system:serviceaccounts","system:serviceaccounts:default"],"resourceAttributes":{"group":"","namespace":"default","resource":"pods","verb":"get","version":"v1"},"user":"system:serviceaccount:default:anihpj-user-sa"}
Example 2: Location Admin Checks Deployment Update with Name

This example demonstrates checking permissions for a specific named deployment resource.

# location-check-deploy.yaml
apiVersion: authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: SubjectAccessReview
metadata:
  name: location-check-deploy
spec:
  user: system:serviceaccount:jobpost:anihpj-location-sa
  resourceAttributes:
    namespace: jobpost
    verb: update
    group: apps
    version: v1
    resource: deployments
    name: location-api-deployment
# Check specific deployment update access
kubectl create -f location-check-deploy.yaml
kubectl describe subjectaccessreview location-check-deploy
subjectaccessreview.authorization.k8s.io/location-check-deploy created Name: location-check-deploy Spec: User: system:serviceaccount:jobpost:anihpj-location-sa Resource Attributes: Namespace: jobpost Verb: update Group: apps Version: v1 Resource: deployments Name: location-api-deployment
Example 3: Author Service Checks Scale Subresource Access

This example shows checking permissions for deployment scale subresource.

# author-check-scale.yaml
apiVersion: authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: SubjectAccessReview
metadata:
  name: author-check-scale
spec:
  user: system:serviceaccount:default:anihpj-author-sa
  resourceAttributes:
    namespace: default
    verb: update
    group: apps
    version: v1
    resource: deployments
    subresource: scale
    name: author-processor
# Check scale subresource permissions
kubectl create -f author-check-scale.yaml
kubectl get subjectaccessreview author-check-scale -o yaml
subjectaccessreview.authorization.k8s.io/author-check-scale created spec: resourceAttributes: group: apps name: author-processor namespace: default resource: deployments subresource: scale verb: update version: v1 user: system:serviceaccount:default:anihpj-author-sa status: allowed: true
Example 4: JobPost Admin Checks Non-Resource Metrics Access

This example demonstrates checking access to non-resource /metrics endpoint.

# jobpost-check-metrics.yaml
apiVersion: authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: SubjectAccessReview
metadata:
  name: jobpost-check-metrics
spec:
  user: prometheus-scraper
  groups:
    - monitoring
  nonResourceAttributes:
    path: /metrics
    verb: get
# Check metrics endpoint access
kubectl create -f jobpost-check-metrics.yaml
kubectl get subjectaccessreview jobpost-check-metrics -o jsonpath='{.spec.nonResourceAttributes}'
subjectaccessreview.authorization.k8s.io/jobpost-check-metrics created {"path":"/metrics","verb":"get"}
Example 5: User Service Checks Wildcard Resource Access

This example shows checking permissions for all resources using wildcards.

# user-check-all.yaml
apiVersion: authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: SubjectAccessReview
metadata:
  name: user-check-all
spec:
  user: cluster-admin-user
  groups:
    - system:masters
  resourceAttributes:
    namespace: ""
    verb: "*"
    group: "*"
    version: "*"
    resource: "*"
# Check wildcard permissions
kubectl create -f user-check-all.yaml
kubectl describe subjectaccessreview user-check-all
subjectaccessreview.authorization.k8s.io/user-check-all created Name: user-check-all Spec: User: cluster-admin-user Groups: [system:masters] Resource Attributes: Verb: * Group: * Version: * Resource: * Status: Allowed: true Reason: RBAC: user is member of system:masters with cluster-admin privileges

SubjectAccessReviewStatus

Official Docs: SubjectAccessReviewStatus v1 API | API Version: authorization.k8s.io/v1

SubjectAccessReviewStatus contains the server's authorization decision with allowed/denied status.

Full Description

SubjectAccessReviewStatus is filled in by the server and indicates whether the requested action is allowed or not. The status includes the authorization decision (allowed or denied), an optional human-readable reason explaining the decision, and an optional evaluation error field for cases where errors occurred during authorization processing. Note that both allowed and denied can be false simultaneously, indicating that the authorizer has no opinion on the request. An action may still be determined despite evaluation errors, as authorization systems like RBAC can make decisions even when some roles are missing.

Parameters Description

  • allowed: boolean (required) - True if the action would be allowed, false otherwise
  • denied: boolean - True if action explicitly denied. Can be false while allowed is also false if authorizer has no opinion
  • reason: string - Human-readable explanation for the authorization decision. Typically contains RBAC rule information
  • evaluationError: string - Indicates errors during authorization check. Authorization may still be determined despite errors (e.g., RBAC missing a role but enough roles present to decide)

Return Value

  • Part of SubjectAccessReview - SubjectAccessReviewStatus is populated by the server in the returned SubjectAccessReview object

5 Practical Django Examples

Example 1: Skills Service Check Returns Allowed Status

This example shows a successful authorization check where User service is allowed to create jobs.

# skills-user-allowed.yaml
apiVersion: authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: SubjectAccessReview
metadata:
  name: skills-user-allowed
spec:
  user: system:serviceaccount:default:anihpj-user-sa
  resourceAttributes:
    namespace: default
    verb: create
    group: batch
    version: v1
    resource: jobs
# Check returns allowed status
kubectl create -f skills-user-allowed.yaml
kubectl get subjectaccessreview skills-user-allowed -o jsonpath='{.status}'
subjectaccessreview.authorization.k8s.io/skills-user-allowed created {"allowed":true,"reason":"RBAC: allowed by RoleBinding 'job-creator/default' of Role 'job-creator' to ServiceAccount 'anihpj-user-sa/default'"}
Example 2: Location Service Check Returns Denied Status

This example demonstrates an authorization check where Author service is explicitly denied from deleting secrets.

# location-author-denied.yaml
apiVersion: authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: SubjectAccessReview
metadata:
  name: location-author-denied
spec:
  user: system:serviceaccount:default:anihpj-author-sa
  resourceAttributes:
    namespace: default
    verb: delete
    group: ""
    version: v1
    resource: secrets
# Check returns denied status
kubectl create -f location-author-denied.yaml
kubectl get subjectaccessreview location-author-denied -o yaml
subjectaccessreview.authorization.k8s.io/location-author-denied created status: allowed: false denied: true reason: "RBAC: User 'system:serviceaccount:default:anihpj-author-sa' cannot delete secrets in namespace 'default'"
Example 3: JobPost Check Shows No Opinion Status

This example shows both allowed and denied as false, indicating the authorizer has no opinion.

# jobpost-no-opinion.yaml
apiVersion: authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: SubjectAccessReview
metadata:
  name: jobpost-no-opinion
spec:
  user: external-system-user
  resourceAttributes:
    namespace: external
    verb: watch
    group: custom.io
    version: v1
    resource: customresources
# Check shows no opinion
kubectl create -f jobpost-no-opinion.yaml
kubectl describe subjectaccessreview jobpost-no-opinion
subjectaccessreview.authorization.k8s.io/jobpost-no-opinion created Name: jobpost-no-opinion Status: Allowed: false Denied: false Reason: No authorizer has an opinion on this request
Example 4: Author Service Check with Evaluation Error

This example demonstrates authorization proceeding despite evaluation errors.

# author-eval-error.yaml
apiVersion: authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: SubjectAccessReview
metadata:
  name: author-eval-error
spec:
  user: system:serviceaccount:jobpost:anihpj-author-sa
  resourceAttributes:
    namespace: jobpost
    verb: update
    group: apps
    version: v1
    resource: deployments
# Check with evaluation error
kubectl create -f author-eval-error.yaml
kubectl get subjectaccessreview author-eval-error -o jsonpath='{.status}'
subjectaccessreview.authorization.k8s.io/author-eval-error created {"allowed":true,"evaluationError":"RBAC: error getting Role 'missing-role' in namespace 'jobpost': roles.rbac.authorization.k8s.io 'missing-role' not found","reason":"RBAC: allowed by ClusterRoleBinding 'author-admin-binding' despite missing Role"}
Example 5: User Service Check with Detailed Reason

This example shows detailed reason explaining the authorization decision.

# user-detailed-reason.yaml
apiVersion: authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: SubjectAccessReview
metadata:
  name: user-detailed-reason
spec:
  user: jane-developer
  groups:
    - developers
    - anihpj-devops
  resourceAttributes:
    namespace: production
    verb: delete
    group: ""
    version: v1
    resource: pods
# Check with detailed reason
kubectl create -f user-detailed-reason.yaml
kubectl get subjectaccessreview user-detailed-reason -o yaml
subjectaccessreview.authorization.k8s.io/user-detailed-reason created status: allowed: true denied: false reason: "RBAC: allowed by RoleBinding 'developers-admin/production' of ClusterRole 'admin' to Group 'developers'; Group 'anihpj-devops' provides additional ClusterRoleBinding 'devops-cluster-admin' privileges"

SubjectAccessReview Operations

Operations Overview

create - Create a SubjectAccessReview

SubjectAccessReview operations allow cluster administrators to check whether other users, groups, or service accounts can perform specific actions. This privileged operation is essential for validating RBAC configurations in your anihpj Django application.

create - create a SubjectAccessReview

Create a SubjectAccessReview to check if a specified subject can perform an action.

HTTP Request
POST /apis/authorization.k8s.io/v1/subjectaccessreviews
Parameters
  • body: SubjectAccessReview, required - SubjectAccessReview object to create
  • dryRun (in query): string - When present, indicates request should not be persisted
  • fieldManager (in query): string - Name of the manager associated with this operation
  • fieldValidation (in query): string - Field validation level (Ignore, Warn, Strict)
  • pretty (in query): string - If true, return pretty-printed output
Return Value
  • 200 (SubjectAccessReview): OK
  • 201 (SubjectAccessReview): Created
  • 202 (SubjectAccessReview): Accepted
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: Skills Admin Checks User Service Deployment Access

This example shows the Skills admin verifying if User service can create deployments.

# skills-check-user-deploy.yaml
apiVersion: authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: SubjectAccessReview
metadata:
  name: skills-check-user-deploy
spec:
  user: system:serviceaccount:default:anihpj-user-sa
  resourceAttributes:
    namespace: default
    verb: create
    group: apps
    version: v1
    resource: deployments
# Create SubjectAccessReview
kubectl create -f skills-check-user-deploy.yaml
kubectl get subjectaccessreview skills-check-user-deploy -o yaml
subjectaccessreview.authorization.k8s.io/skills-check-user-deploy created status: allowed: true reason: "RBAC: allowed by RoleBinding 'deployment-creator/default' of Role 'deployment-creator'"
Example 2: Location Service Validates Author ConfigMap Access

This example demonstrates checking if Author service can read configmaps.

# location-check-author-cm.yaml
apiVersion: authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: SubjectAccessReview
metadata:
  name: location-check-author-cm
spec:
  user: system:serviceaccount:jobpost:anihpj-author-sa
  groups:
    - system:serviceaccounts
    - system:serviceaccounts:jobpost
  resourceAttributes:
    namespace: jobpost
    verb: get
    group: ""
    version: v1
    resource: configmaps
    name: author-config
# Validate ConfigMap access
kubectl create -f location-check-author-cm.yaml
kubectl describe subjectaccessreview location-check-author-cm
subjectaccessreview.authorization.k8s.io/location-check-author-cm created Name: location-check-author-cm Spec: User: system:serviceaccount:jobpost:anihpj-author-sa Groups: [system:serviceaccounts system:serviceaccounts:jobpost] Resource Attributes: Namespace: jobpost Verb: get Resource: configmaps Name: author-config Status: Allowed: true Reason: RBAC: allowed by RoleBinding 'configmap-reader/jobpost'
Example 3: JobPost Admin Tests Skills Service Delete Permissions

This example shows testing if Skills service can delete PersistentVolumeClaims.

# jobpost-test-skills-pvc.yaml
apiVersion: authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: SubjectAccessReview
metadata:
  name: jobpost-test-skills-pvc
spec:
  user: system:serviceaccount:default:anihpj-skills-sa
  resourceAttributes:
    namespace: default
    verb: delete
    group: ""
    version: v1
    resource: persistentvolumeclaims
# Test delete permissions
kubectl create -f jobpost-test-skills-pvc.yaml
kubectl get subjectaccessreview jobpost-test-skills-pvc -o jsonpath='{.status}'
subjectaccessreview.authorization.k8s.io/jobpost-test-skills-pvc created {"allowed":false,"denied":true,"reason":"RBAC: User 'system:serviceaccount:default:anihpj-skills-sa' cannot delete persistentvolumeclaims in namespace 'default'"}
Example 4: Author Admin Checks User API Discovery Access

This example demonstrates checking non-resource URL access for API discovery.

# author-check-user-api.yaml
apiVersion: authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: SubjectAccessReview
metadata:
  name: author-check-user-api
spec:
  user: system:serviceaccount:default:anihpj-user-sa
  nonResourceAttributes:
    path: /apis
    verb: get
# Check API discovery
kubectl create -f author-check-user-api.yaml
kubectl get subjectaccessreview author-check-user-api -o yaml
subjectaccessreview.authorization.k8s.io/author-check-user-api created status: allowed: true reason: "RBAC: allowed by ClusterRoleBinding 'system:discovery' of ClusterRole 'system:discovery'"
Example 5: User Service Validates Group-Based Permissions

This example shows checking permissions for a user with multiple group memberships.

# user-validate-groups.yaml
apiVersion: authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: SubjectAccessReview
metadata:
  name: user-validate-groups
spec:
  user: maria-admin
  groups:
    - system:masters
    - anihpj-admins
  resourceAttributes:
    verb: "*"
    group: "*"
    version: "*"
    resource: "*"
# Validate group permissions
kubectl create -f user-validate-groups.yaml
kubectl describe subjectaccessreview user-validate-groups
subjectaccessreview.authorization.k8s.io/user-validate-groups created Name: user-validate-groups Spec: User: maria-admin Groups: [system:masters anihpj-admins] Resource Attributes: Verb: * Group: * Version: * Resource: * Status: Allowed: true Reason: RBAC: user is member of system:masters which grants cluster-admin privileges

ClusterRole

ClusterRole Overview

ClusterRoleList - Collection of ClusterRoles
Operations - API operations for management
Official Docs: ClusterRole v1 API | API Version: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1

ClusterRole is a cluster level, logical grouping of PolicyRules that can be referenced as a unit by a RoleBinding or ClusterRoleBinding.

Full Description

ClusterRole is a cluster-scoped resource that defines a set of permissions (PolicyRules) for accessing Kubernetes resources across all namespaces. Unlike Roles which are namespace-scoped, ClusterRoles can grant access to cluster-scoped resources (like nodes, persistent volumes) or be used across all namespaces. ClusterRoles support aggregation through AggregationRule, allowing you to compose permissions from multiple ClusterRoles dynamically. This is commonly used by cluster administrators to define reusable permission sets for RBAC (Role-Based Access Control) configurations.

In your anihpj Django application, ClusterRoles define permissions for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost service accounts to access cluster-wide resources. For example, you might create a ClusterRole that allows reading all ConfigMaps across namespaces, or managing persistent volumes for database storage. ClusterRoles are bound to subjects (users, groups, or service accounts) using ClusterRoleBindings for cluster-wide access, or RoleBindings for namespace-specific access.

Syntax

# Create a ClusterRole from YAML file
kubectl create -f clusterrole.yaml
# Get a specific ClusterRole
kubectl get clusterrole {name}
# List all ClusterRoles
kubectl get clusterroles
# Delete a ClusterRole
kubectl delete clusterrole {name}

YAML Structure

apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: ClusterRole
metadata:
  name: anihpj-cluster-reader
  labels:
    app: anihpj
rules:
- apiGroups: [""]
  resources: ["pods", "services", "configmaps"]
  verbs: ["get", "list", "watch"]
- apiGroups: ["apps"]
  resources: ["deployments", "statefulsets"]
  verbs: ["get", "list"]

Parameters Description

  • apiVersion: string - API version (rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1)
  • kind: string - Resource type (ClusterRole)
  • metadata: ObjectMeta - Standard object's metadata including name, labels, annotations
  • aggregationRule: AggregationRule - Optional field that describes how to build Rules from other ClusterRoles
  • aggregationRule.clusterRoleSelectors: []LabelSelector - Selectors to find ClusterRoles and aggregate their rules
  • rules: []PolicyRule - List of PolicyRules for this ClusterRole (controller managed if aggregationRule is set)
  • rules.apiGroups: []string - API groups that contain the resources ("" for core, "*" for all)
  • rules.resources: []string - List of resources this rule applies to ("*" for all resources)
  • rules.verbs: []string, required - List of verbs (get, list, create, update, delete, "*" for all)
  • rules.resourceNames: []string - Optional whitelist of specific resource names
  • rules.nonResourceURLs: []string - Partial URLs for non-resource access (only for ClusterRoleBindings)

Return Value

  • ClusterRole: Returns ClusterRole object with metadata, rules, and aggregation configuration
  • Status: HTTP status codes (200 OK, 201 Created, 401 Unauthorized, 404 Not Found)

5 Practical Django Examples

Example 1: Create ClusterRole for Skills Service Read Access

This example creates a ClusterRole allowing Skills service to read ConfigMaps and Secrets across all namespaces.

# skills-reader-clusterrole.yaml
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: ClusterRole
metadata:
  name: anihpj-skills-reader
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    model: skills
    component: rbac
rules:
- apiGroups: [""]
  resources: ["configmaps", "secrets"]
  verbs: ["get", "list", "watch"]
kubectl create -f skills-reader-clusterrole.yaml
kubectl get clusterrole anihpj-skills-reader
clusterrole.rbac.authorization.k8s.io/anihpj-skills-reader created NAME CREATED AT anihpj-skills-reader 2025-11-03T10:15:00Z
Example 2: Create ClusterRole for User Service with Multiple Resources

This example shows creating a ClusterRole for User service to manage deployments and services.

# user-manager-clusterrole.yaml
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: ClusterRole
metadata:
  name: anihpj-user-manager
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    model: user
    component: rbac
  annotations:
    description: "User service cluster-wide management permissions"
rules:
- apiGroups: ["apps"]
  resources: ["deployments", "replicasets"]
  verbs: ["get", "list", "watch", "create", "update"]
- apiGroups: [""]
  resources: ["services", "endpoints"]
  verbs: ["get", "list", "watch"]
kubectl create -f user-manager-clusterrole.yaml
kubectl describe clusterrole anihpj-user-manager
clusterrole.rbac.authorization.k8s.io/anihpj-user-manager created Name: anihpj-user-manager Labels: app=anihpj component=rbac model=user Annotations: description: User service cluster-wide management permissions PolicyRule: Resources Non-Resource URLs Resource Names Verbs --------- ----------------- -------------- ----- deployments.apps [] [] [get list watch create update] replicasets.apps [] [] [get list watch create update] endpoints [] [] [get list watch] services [] [] [get list watch]
Example 3: Create Aggregated ClusterRole for Author Service

This example demonstrates creating a ClusterRole that aggregates permissions from other ClusterRoles.

# author-aggregated-clusterrole.yaml
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: ClusterRole
metadata:
  name: anihpj-author-aggregated
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    model: author
    rbac.anihpj.io/aggregate-to-author: "true"
aggregationRule:
  clusterRoleSelectors:
  - matchLabels:
      rbac.anihpj.io/aggregate-to-author: "true"
rules: []
# Create the aggregated ClusterRole
kubectl create -f author-aggregated-clusterrole.yaml
kubectl get clusterrole anihpj-author-aggregated -o yaml
clusterrole.rbac.authorization.k8s.io/anihpj-author-aggregated created apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1 kind: ClusterRole metadata: name: anihpj-author-aggregated labels: app: anihpj model: author rbac.anihpj.io/aggregate-to-author: "true" aggregationRule: clusterRoleSelectors: - matchLabels: rbac.anihpj.io/aggregate-to-author: "true" rules: - apiGroups: [""] resources: ["pods", "configmaps"] verbs: ["get", "list"]
Example 4: Create ClusterRole for Location Service with Persistent Volumes

This example creates a ClusterRole allowing Location service to manage persistent volumes cluster-wide.

# location-pv-clusterrole.yaml
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: ClusterRole
metadata:
  name: anihpj-location-pv-admin
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    model: location
    component: storage
rules:
- apiGroups: [""]
  resources: ["persistentvolumes", "persistentvolumeclaims"]
  verbs: ["get", "list", "watch", "create", "update", "patch", "delete"]
- apiGroups: ["storage.k8s.io"]
  resources: ["storageclasses"]
  verbs: ["get", "list", "watch"]
kubectl create -f location-pv-clusterrole.yaml
kubectl get clusterrole anihpj-location-pv-admin -o jsonpath='{.rules}'
clusterrole.rbac.authorization.k8s.io/anihpj-location-pv-admin created [{"apiGroups":[""],"resources":["persistentvolumes","persistentvolumeclaims"],"verbs":["get","list","watch","create","update","patch","delete"]},{"apiGroups":["storage.k8s.io"],"resources":["storageclasses"],"verbs":["get","list","watch"]}]
Example 5: Create ClusterRole for JobPost Service with Non-Resource URLs

This example shows creating a ClusterRole for JobPost service to access API discovery endpoints.

# jobpost-api-discovery-clusterrole.yaml
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: ClusterRole
metadata:
  name: anihpj-jobpost-api-discovery
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    model: jobpost
    component: api-discovery
  annotations:
    description: "JobPost service API discovery and health check access"
rules:
- nonResourceURLs: ["/api", "/api/*", "/apis", "/apis/*", "/healthz", "/version"]
  verbs: ["get"]
- apiGroups: ["batch"]
  resources: ["jobs", "cronjobs"]
  verbs: ["get", "list", "watch", "create", "update"]
kubectl create -f jobpost-api-discovery-clusterrole.yaml
kubectl describe clusterrole anihpj-jobpost-api-discovery
clusterrole.rbac.authorization.k8s.io/anihpj-jobpost-api-discovery created Name: anihpj-jobpost-api-discovery Labels: app=anihpj component=api-discovery model=jobpost Annotations: description: JobPost service API discovery and health check access PolicyRule: Resources Non-Resource URLs Resource Names Verbs --------- ----------------- -------------- ----- [/api /api/* /apis /apis/* /healthz /version] [] [get] cronjobs.batch [] [] [get list watch create update] jobs.batch [] [] [get list watch create update]

ClusterRoleList

Official Docs: ClusterRoleList v1 API | API Version: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1

ClusterRoleList is a collection of ClusterRoles returned by list operations.

Full Description

ClusterRoleList is a collection object that contains an array of ClusterRole resources. It is returned when you list all ClusterRoles in the cluster using kubectl or the Kubernetes API. The list includes standard metadata and an items array containing all ClusterRole objects that match the query parameters. This is useful for discovering available cluster-wide permissions, auditing RBAC configurations, and managing authorization policies across your Kubernetes cluster.

Parameters Description

  • apiVersion: string - API version (rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1)
  • kind: string - Resource type (ClusterRoleList)
  • metadata: ListMeta - Standard list metadata including resource version and continue token
  • items: []ClusterRole, required - Array of ClusterRole objects

Return Value

  • ClusterRoleList: Returns collection with metadata and items array of ClusterRoles
  • Status: HTTP status codes (200 OK, 401 Unauthorized)

ClusterRole Operations

Operations Overview

get - Read the specified ClusterRole
list - List or watch objects of kind ClusterRole
create - Create a ClusterRole
update - Replace the specified ClusterRole
patch - Partially update the specified ClusterRole
delete - Delete a ClusterRole
deletecollection - Delete collection of ClusterRole

ClusterRole operations allow cluster administrators to manage cluster-wide RBAC permissions for your anihpj Django application. These operations enable you to define, update, and delete permission sets that can be assigned to service accounts across all namespaces.

get - read the specified ClusterRole

Read the specified ClusterRole by name.

HTTP Request
GET /apis/rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1/clusterroles/{name}
Parameters
  • name (in path): string, required - Name of the ClusterRole
  • pretty (in query): string - Pretty print output
Return Value
  • 200 (ClusterRole): OK
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: Get Skills ClusterRole Details

This example demonstrates retrieving details of the Skills service ClusterRole.

# Get Skills ClusterRole
kubectl get clusterrole anihpj-skills-reader -o yaml
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1 kind: ClusterRole metadata: name: anihpj-skills-reader labels: app: anihpj model: skills rules: - apiGroups: [""] resources: ["configmaps", "secrets"] verbs: ["get", "list", "watch"]
Example 2: Get User ClusterRole with Describe

This example shows retrieving User service ClusterRole with detailed description.

# Get User ClusterRole details
kubectl describe clusterrole anihpj-user-manager
Name: anihpj-user-manager Labels: app=anihpj component=rbac model=user Annotations: description: User service cluster-wide management permissions PolicyRule: Resources Non-Resource URLs Resource Names Verbs --------- ----------------- -------------- ----- deployments.apps [] [] [get list watch create update] replicasets.apps [] [] [get list watch create update] endpoints [] [] [get list watch] services [] [] [get list watch]
Example 3: Get Author ClusterRole JSON Format

This example illustrates retrieving Author service ClusterRole in JSON format.

# Get Author ClusterRole as JSON
kubectl get clusterrole anihpj-author-aggregated -o json
{ "apiVersion": "rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1", "kind": "ClusterRole", "metadata": { "name": "anihpj-author-aggregated", "labels": { "app": "anihpj", "model": "author" } }, "aggregationRule": { "clusterRoleSelectors": [ { "matchLabels": { "rbac.anihpj.io/aggregate-to-author": "true" } } ] }, "rules": [] }
Example 4: Get Location ClusterRole Rules

This example demonstrates extracting specific rules from Location service ClusterRole.

# Get Location ClusterRole rules
kubectl get clusterrole anihpj-location-pv-admin -o jsonpath='{.rules}'
[{"apiGroups":[""],"resources":["persistentvolumes","persistentvolumeclaims"],"verbs":["get","list","watch","create","update","patch","delete"]},{"apiGroups":["storage.k8s.io"],"resources":["storageclasses"],"verbs":["get","list","watch"]}]
Example 5: Get JobPost ClusterRole Custom Columns

This example shows retrieving JobPost service ClusterRole with custom output columns.

# Get JobPost ClusterRole with custom columns
kubectl get clusterrole anihpj-jobpost-api-discovery -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,CREATED:.metadata.creationTimestamp
NAME CREATED anihpj-jobpost-api-discovery 2025-11-03T12:00:00Z

list - list or watch objects of kind ClusterRole

List all ClusterRoles in the cluster.

HTTP Request
GET /apis/rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1/clusterroles
Parameters
  • allowWatchBookmarks (in query): boolean - Enable watch bookmarks
  • continue (in query): string - Continue token for pagination
  • fieldSelector (in query): string - Field selector for filtering
  • labelSelector (in query): string - Label selector for filtering
  • limit (in query): integer - Maximum number of results to return
  • pretty (in query): string - Pretty print output
  • resourceVersion (in query): string - Resource version for list
  • timeoutSeconds (in query): integer - Timeout for the list/watch call
  • watch (in query): boolean - Watch for changes
Return Value
  • 200 (ClusterRoleList): OK
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Django List Examples
Example 1: List Skills Model ClusterRoles

This example demonstrates listing all ClusterRoles for Skills service.

# List Skills ClusterRoles
kubectl get clusterroles -l model=skills
NAME CREATED AT anihpj-skills-reader 2025-11-03T10:15:00Z anihpj-skills-monitor 2025-11-03T11:00:00Z
Example 2: List User Service ClusterRoles with Wide Output

This example shows listing User model ClusterRoles with detailed output.

# List User ClusterRoles with wide output
kubectl get clusterroles -l model=user -o wide
NAME CREATED AT anihpj-user-manager 2025-11-03T10:30:00Z anihpj-user-cronjob-admin 2025-11-03T11:15:00Z
Example 3: List Author ClusterRoles by App Label

This example illustrates listing Author service ClusterRoles using app label selector.

# List Author ClusterRoles by app
kubectl get clusterroles -l app=anihpj,model=author
NAME CREATED AT anihpj-author-aggregated 2025-11-03T10:45:00Z anihpj-author-configmap-reader 2025-11-03T11:30:00Z
Example 4: List Location ClusterRoles with JSON Output

This example demonstrates listing Location service ClusterRoles in JSON format.

# List Location ClusterRoles as JSON
kubectl get clusterroles -l model=location -o json
{ "apiVersion": "v1", "kind": "List", "items": [ { "apiVersion": "rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1", "kind": "ClusterRole", "metadata": { "name": "anihpj-location-pv-admin", "labels": { "app": "anihpj", "model": "location" } } } ] }
Example 5: List JobPost ClusterRoles with Custom Fields

This example shows listing JobPost service ClusterRoles with specific field selection.

# List JobPost ClusterRoles with custom fields
kubectl get clusterroles -l model=jobpost -o jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{.metadata.name}{"\t"}{.metadata.creationTimestamp}{"\n"}{end}'
anihpj-jobpost-api-discovery 2025-11-03T12:00:00Z anihpj-jobpost-secret-admin 2025-11-03T12:15:00Z

create - create a ClusterRole

Create a new ClusterRole.

HTTP Request
POST /apis/rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1/clusterroles
Parameters
  • body: ClusterRole, required - ClusterRole object to create
  • dryRun (in query): string - When present, indicates request should not be persisted
  • fieldManager (in query): string - Name of the manager associated with this operation
  • fieldValidation (in query): string - Field validation level (Ignore, Warn, Strict)
  • pretty (in query): string - Pretty print output
Return Value
  • 200 (ClusterRole): OK
  • 201 (ClusterRole): Created
  • 202 (ClusterRole): Accepted
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: Create ClusterRole for Skills Monitoring

This example creates a ClusterRole for Skills service to monitor pods and events.

# skills-monitor-clusterrole.yaml
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: ClusterRole
metadata:
  name: anihpj-skills-monitor
rules:
- apiGroups: [""]
  resources: ["pods", "events"]
  verbs: ["get", "list", "watch"]
kubectl create -f skills-monitor-clusterrole.yaml
kubectl get clusterrole anihpj-skills-monitor
clusterrole.rbac.authorization.k8s.io/anihpj-skills-monitor created NAME CREATED AT anihpj-skills-monitor 2025-11-03T11:00:00Z
Example 2: Create ClusterRole for User Service CronJobs

This example shows creating a ClusterRole for User service to manage CronJobs.

# user-cronjob-clusterrole.yaml
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: ClusterRole
metadata:
  name: anihpj-user-cronjob-admin
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    model: user
rules:
- apiGroups: ["batch"]
  resources: ["cronjobs", "jobs"]
  verbs: ["*"]
kubectl create -f user-cronjob-clusterrole.yaml
kubectl describe clusterrole anihpj-user-cronjob-admin
clusterrole.rbac.authorization.k8s.io/anihpj-user-cronjob-admin created Name: anihpj-user-cronjob-admin Labels: app=anihpj model=user PolicyRule: Resources Non-Resource URLs Resource Names Verbs --------- ----------------- -------------- ----- cronjobs.batch [] [] [*] jobs.batch [] [] [*]
Example 3: Create ClusterRole for Author Service ConfigMaps

This example demonstrates creating a ClusterRole for Author service with specific ConfigMap access.

# author-configmap-clusterrole.yaml
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: ClusterRole
metadata:
  name: anihpj-author-configmap-reader
rules:
- apiGroups: [""]
  resources: ["configmaps"]
  resourceNames: ["author-config", "anihpj-config"]
  verbs: ["get", "list"]
kubectl create -f author-configmap-clusterrole.yaml
kubectl get clusterrole anihpj-author-configmap-reader -o yaml
clusterrole.rbac.authorization.k8s.io/anihpj-author-configmap-reader created apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1 kind: ClusterRole metadata: name: anihpj-author-configmap-reader rules: - apiGroups: [""] resources: ["configmaps"] resourceNames: ["author-config", "anihpj-config"] verbs: ["get", "list"]
Example 4: Create ClusterRole for Location Service Nodes

This example creates a ClusterRole for Location service to view node information.

# location-node-viewer-clusterrole.yaml
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: ClusterRole
metadata:
  name: anihpj-location-node-viewer
  annotations:
    description: "Location service node discovery"
rules:
- apiGroups: [""]
  resources: ["nodes"]
  verbs: ["get", "list", "watch"]
- apiGroups: [""]
  resources: ["nodes/status"]
  verbs: ["get"]
kubectl create -f location-node-viewer-clusterrole.yaml
kubectl auth can-i get nodes --as=system:serviceaccount:default:anihpj-location-sa
clusterrole.rbac.authorization.k8s.io/anihpj-location-node-viewer created no (requires ClusterRoleBinding)
Example 5: Create ClusterRole for JobPost Service with Dry Run

This example shows creating a ClusterRole with dry run to validate before actual creation.

# jobpost-secret-admin-clusterrole.yaml
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: ClusterRole
metadata:
  name: anihpj-jobpost-secret-admin
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    model: jobpost
    security: high
rules:
- apiGroups: [""]
  resources: ["secrets"]
  verbs: ["get", "list", "watch", "create", "update", "patch", "delete"]
# Dry run to validate
kubectl create -f jobpost-secret-admin-clusterrole.yaml --dry-run=client -o yaml
# Actually create
kubectl create -f jobpost-secret-admin-clusterrole.yaml
clusterrole.rbac.authorization.k8s.io/anihpj-jobpost-secret-admin created (dry run) clusterrole.rbac.authorization.k8s.io/anihpj-jobpost-secret-admin created

update - replace the specified ClusterRole

Replace the specified ClusterRole.

HTTP Request
PUT /apis/rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1/clusterroles/{name}
Parameters
  • name (in path): string, required - Name of the ClusterRole
  • body: ClusterRole, required - Updated ClusterRole object
  • dryRun (in query): string - When present, indicates request should not be persisted
  • fieldManager (in query): string - Name of the manager associated with this operation
  • fieldValidation (in query): string - Field validation level (Ignore, Warn, Strict)
  • pretty (in query): string - Pretty print output
Return Value
  • 200 (ClusterRole): OK
  • 201 (ClusterRole): Created
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Django Update Examples
Example 1: Update Skills ClusterRole Rules

This example demonstrates updating Skills service ClusterRole with additional permissions.

# skills-reader-updated.yaml
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: ClusterRole
metadata:
  name: anihpj-skills-reader
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    model: skills
rules:
- apiGroups: [""]
  resources: ["configmaps", "secrets", "pods"]
  verbs: ["get", "list", "watch"]
- apiGroups: ["apps"]
  resources: ["deployments"]
  verbs: ["get", "list"]
# Update Skills ClusterRole
kubectl replace -f skills-reader-updated.yaml
clusterrole.rbac.authorization.k8s.io/anihpj-skills-reader replaced
Example 2: Update User ClusterRole with New Resources

This example shows updating User service ClusterRole to include PersistentVolume access.

# Update User ClusterRole from file
kubectl replace -f user-manager-updated.yaml
kubectl get clusterrole anihpj-user-manager -o yaml
clusterrole.rbac.authorization.k8s.io/anihpj-user-manager replaced apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1 kind: ClusterRole metadata: name: anihpj-user-manager rules: - apiGroups: ["apps"] resources: ["deployments", "replicasets"] verbs: ["get", "list", "watch", "create", "update"] - apiGroups: [""] resources: ["persistentvolumes"] verbs: ["get", "list", "watch"]
Example 3: Update Author Aggregated ClusterRole

This example illustrates updating Author service aggregated ClusterRole selectors.

# Update Author aggregated ClusterRole
kubectl replace -f author-aggregated-updated.yaml --force
clusterrole.rbac.authorization.k8s.io/anihpj-author-aggregated replaced
Example 4: Update Location ClusterRole with Dry Run

This example demonstrates updating Location service ClusterRole with validation.

# Dry run update first
kubectl replace -f location-pv-updated.yaml --dry-run=client
# Actual update
kubectl replace -f location-pv-updated.yaml
clusterrole.rbac.authorization.k8s.io/anihpj-location-pv-admin replaced (dry run) clusterrole.rbac.authorization.k8s.io/anihpj-location-pv-admin replaced
Example 5: Update JobPost ClusterRole with Field Manager

This example shows updating JobPost service ClusterRole with field manager specification.

# Update with field manager
kubectl replace -f jobpost-api-updated.yaml --field-manager=anihpj-admin
clusterrole.rbac.authorization.k8s.io/anihpj-jobpost-api-discovery replaced

patch - partially update the specified ClusterRole

Partially update the specified ClusterRole.

HTTP Request
PATCH /apis/rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1/clusterroles/{name}
Parameters
  • name (in path): string, required - Name of the ClusterRole
  • body: Patch, required - Patch object
  • dryRun (in query): string - When present, indicates request should not be persisted
  • fieldManager (in query): string - Name of the manager associated with this operation
  • fieldValidation (in query): string - Field validation level (Ignore, Warn, Strict)
  • force (in query): boolean - Force apply the changes
  • pretty (in query): string - Pretty print output
Return Value
  • 200 (ClusterRole): OK
  • 201 (ClusterRole): Created
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Django Patch Examples
Example 1: Patch Skills ClusterRole Labels

This example demonstrates adding labels to Skills service ClusterRole using strategic merge patch.

# Patch Skills ClusterRole labels
kubectl patch clusterrole anihpj-skills-reader -p '{"metadata":{"labels":{"environment":"production","team":"backend"}}}'
clusterrole.rbac.authorization.k8s.io/anihpj-skills-reader patched
Example 2: Patch User ClusterRole Rules

This example shows adding new rules to User service ClusterRole using JSON patch.

# Add new rule to User ClusterRole
kubectl patch clusterrole anihpj-user-manager --type='json' -p='[{"op": "add", "path": "/rules/-", "value": {"apiGroups": ["batch"], "resources": ["jobs"], "verbs": ["get", "list"]}}]'
clusterrole.rbac.authorization.k8s.io/anihpj-user-manager patched
Example 3: Patch Author ClusterRole Annotations

This example illustrates updating Author service ClusterRole annotations.

# Update Author ClusterRole annotations
kubectl patch clusterrole anihpj-author-aggregated -p '{"metadata":{"annotations":{"updated-by":"anihpj-admin","last-modified":"2025-11-03"}}}'
clusterrole.rbac.authorization.k8s.io/anihpj-author-aggregated patched
Example 4: Patch Location ClusterRole Resource Names

This example demonstrates updating Location service ClusterRole with specific resource names.

# Patch Location ClusterRole with resource names
kubectl patch clusterrole anihpj-location-pv-admin --type='json' -p='[{"op": "add", "path": "/rules/0/resourceNames", "value": ["location-pv-1", "location-pv-2"]}]'
clusterrole.rbac.authorization.k8s.io/anihpj-location-pv-admin patched
Example 5: Patch JobPost ClusterRole with Merge Patch

This example shows patching JobPost service ClusterRole using merge patch strategy.

# Merge patch JobPost ClusterRole
kubectl patch clusterrole anihpj-jobpost-api-discovery --type='merge' -p='{"metadata":{"labels":{"version":"v2","security":"enhanced"}}}'
clusterrole.rbac.authorization.k8s.io/anihpj-jobpost-api-discovery patched

delete - delete a ClusterRole

Delete the specified ClusterRole.

HTTP Request
DELETE /apis/rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1/clusterroles/{name}
Parameters
  • name (in path): string, required - Name of the ClusterRole
  • body: DeleteOptions - Delete options
  • dryRun (in query): string - When present, indicates request should not be persisted
  • gracePeriodSeconds (in query): integer - Grace period for deletion
  • pretty (in query): string - Pretty print output
  • propagationPolicy (in query): string - Deletion propagation policy
Return Value
  • 200 (Status): OK
  • 202 (Status): Accepted
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Django Delete Examples
Example 1: Delete Skills ClusterRole

This example demonstrates deleting Skills service ClusterRole.

# Delete Skills ClusterRole
kubectl delete clusterrole anihpj-skills-reader
clusterrole.rbac.authorization.k8s.io "anihpj-skills-reader" deleted
Example 2: Delete User ClusterRole with Confirmation

This example shows deleting User service ClusterRole and verifying removal.

# Delete User ClusterRole and verify
kubectl delete clusterrole anihpj-user-manager
kubectl get clusterrole anihpj-user-manager
clusterrole.rbac.authorization.k8s.io "anihpj-user-manager" deleted Error from server (NotFound): clusterroles.rbac.authorization.k8s.io "anihpj-user-manager" not found
Example 3: Delete Author ClusterRole with Dry Run

This example illustrates deleting Author service ClusterRole with validation first.

# Dry run delete first
kubectl delete clusterrole anihpj-author-aggregated --dry-run=client
# Actually delete
kubectl delete clusterrole anihpj-author-aggregated
clusterrole.rbac.authorization.k8s.io "anihpj-author-aggregated" deleted (dry run) clusterrole.rbac.authorization.k8s.io "anihpj-author-aggregated" deleted
Example 4: Delete Location ClusterRole from File

This example demonstrates deleting Location service ClusterRole using YAML file.

# Delete Location ClusterRole from file
kubectl delete -f location-pv-clusterrole.yaml
clusterrole.rbac.authorization.k8s.io "anihpj-location-pv-admin" deleted
Example 5: Delete JobPost ClusterRole with Grace Period

This example shows deleting JobPost service ClusterRole with custom grace period.

# Delete JobPost ClusterRole with grace period
kubectl delete clusterrole anihpj-jobpost-api-discovery --grace-period=30
clusterrole.rbac.authorization.k8s.io "anihpj-jobpost-api-discovery" deleted

deletecollection - delete collection of ClusterRole

Delete collection of ClusterRole objects.

HTTP Request
DELETE /apis/rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1/clusterroles
Parameters
  • body: DeleteOptions - Delete options
  • continue (in query): string - Continue token for pagination
  • dryRun (in query): string - When present, indicates request should not be persisted
  • fieldSelector (in query): string - Field selector for filtering
  • gracePeriodSeconds (in query): integer - Grace period for deletion
  • labelSelector (in query): string - Label selector for filtering
  • limit (in query): integer - Maximum number of results
  • pretty (in query): string - Pretty print output
  • propagationPolicy (in query): string - Deletion propagation policy
  • resourceVersion (in query): string - Resource version for deletion
  • timeoutSeconds (in query): integer - Timeout for the delete operation
Return Value
  • 200 (Status): OK
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Django Delete Collection Examples
Example 1: Delete All Skills ClusterRoles

This example demonstrates deleting all ClusterRoles for Skills service using label selector.

# Delete all Skills ClusterRoles
kubectl delete clusterroles -l model=skills
clusterrole.rbac.authorization.k8s.io "anihpj-skills-reader" deleted clusterrole.rbac.authorization.k8s.io "anihpj-skills-monitor" deleted
Example 2: Delete User Service ClusterRoles with Confirmation

This example shows deleting User model ClusterRoles and verifying removal.

# Delete User ClusterRoles and verify
kubectl delete clusterroles -l model=user
kubectl get clusterroles -l model=user
clusterrole.rbac.authorization.k8s.io "anihpj-user-manager" deleted clusterrole.rbac.authorization.k8s.io "anihpj-user-cronjob-admin" deleted No resources found
Example 3: Delete Author ClusterRoles with Dry Run

This example illustrates deleting Author service ClusterRoles with validation first.

# Dry run delete collection first
kubectl delete clusterroles -l model=author --dry-run=client
# Actually delete collection
kubectl delete clusterroles -l model=author
clusterrole.rbac.authorization.k8s.io "anihpj-author-aggregated" deleted (dry run) clusterrole.rbac.authorization.k8s.io "anihpj-author-configmap-reader" deleted (dry run) clusterrole.rbac.authorization.k8s.io "anihpj-author-aggregated" deleted clusterrole.rbac.authorization.k8s.io "anihpj-author-configmap-reader" deleted
Example 4: Delete Location ClusterRoles by App Label

This example demonstrates deleting Location service ClusterRoles using app and model labels.

# Delete Location ClusterRoles by app and model
kubectl delete clusterroles -l app=anihpj,model=location
clusterrole.rbac.authorization.k8s.io "anihpj-location-pv-admin" deleted clusterrole.rbac.authorization.k8s.io "anihpj-location-node-viewer" deleted
Example 5: Delete JobPost ClusterRoles with Grace Period

This example shows deleting JobPost service ClusterRoles with custom grace period.

# Delete JobPost ClusterRoles with grace period
kubectl delete clusterroles -l model=jobpost --grace-period=60
clusterrole.rbac.authorization.k8s.io "anihpj-jobpost-api-discovery" deleted clusterrole.rbac.authorization.k8s.io "anihpj-jobpost-secret-admin" deleted

RoleBinding

RoleBinding Overview

RoleBindingList - Collection of RoleBindings
Operations - API operations for management
Official Docs: RoleBinding v1 API | API Version: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1

RoleBinding references a role, but does not contain it.

Full Description

RoleBinding references a role, but does not contain it. It can reference a Role in the same namespace or a ClusterRole in the global namespace. It adds who information via Subjects and namespace information by which namespace it exists in. RoleBindings in a given namespace only have effect in that namespace.

Syntax

apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: RoleBinding
metadata:
  name: rolebinding-name
  namespace: namespace-name
roleRef:
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
  kind: Role
  name: role-name
subjects:
- kind: ServiceAccount
  name: serviceaccount-name
  namespace: namespace-name

YAML Structure

apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: RoleBinding
metadata: ObjectMeta
roleRef: RoleRef
subjects: []Subject

Parameters Description

metadata (ObjectMeta): Standard object's metadata.

roleRef (RoleRef), required: RoleRef can reference a Role in the current namespace or a ClusterRole in the global namespace. If the RoleRef cannot be resolved, the Authorizer must return an error. This field is immutable.

  • roleRef.apiGroup (string), required: APIGroup is the group for the resource being referenced
  • roleRef.kind (string), required: Kind is the type of resource being referenced
  • roleRef.name (string), required: Name is the name of resource being referenced

subjects ([]Subject): Atomic: will be replaced during a merge. Subjects holds references to the objects the role applies to.

  • subjects.kind (string), required: Kind of object being referenced. Values defined by this API group are "User", "Group", and "ServiceAccount". If the Authorizer does not recognized the kind value, the Authorizer should report an error.
  • subjects.name (string), required: Name of the object being referenced.
  • subjects.apiGroup (string): APIGroup holds the API group of the referenced subject. Defaults to "" for ServiceAccount subjects. Defaults to "rbac.authorization.k8s.io" for User and Group subjects.
  • subjects.namespace (string): Namespace of the referenced object. If the object kind is non-namespace, such as "User" or "Group", and this value is not empty the Authorizer should report an error.

Return Value

  • RoleBinding: Returns RoleBinding object with metadata, roleRef, and subjects configuration
  • 200: OK
  • 201: Created
  • 401: Unauthorized

Practical Django Examples

Example 1: Bind Skills Manager Role to Service Account

This example creates a RoleBinding that grants Skills model management permissions to a service account.

# Create RoleBinding for Skills manager
kubectl create rolebinding skills-manager-binding \
  --role=skills-admin \
  --serviceaccount=anihpj:skills-service-account \
  --namespace=anihpj
rolebinding.rbac.authorization.k8s.io/skills-manager-binding created
Example 2: Bind User Read Role to Group

This example creates a RoleBinding that grants User model read permissions to a user group.

# Create RoleBinding for User readers
kubectl create rolebinding user-reader-binding \
  --role=user-reader \
  --group=user-viewers \
  --namespace=anihpj
rolebinding.rbac.authorization.k8s.io/user-reader-binding created
Example 3: Bind Author Editor Role to User

This example creates a RoleBinding that grants Author model edit permissions to a specific user.

# Create RoleBinding for Author editor
kubectl create rolebinding author-editor-binding \
  --role=author-editor \
  --user=john@anihpj.com \
  --namespace=anihpj
rolebinding.rbac.authorization.k8s.io/author-editor-binding created
Example 4: Bind Location Admin Role with YAML

This example creates a RoleBinding using YAML manifest for Location model admin permissions.

# Create RoleBinding from YAML
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: RoleBinding
metadata:
  name: location-admin-binding
  namespace: anihpj
roleRef:
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
  kind: Role
  name: location-admin
subjects:
- kind: ServiceAccount
  name: location-service-account
  namespace: anihpj
EOF
rolebinding.rbac.authorization.k8s.io/location-admin-binding created
Example 5: Bind JobPost Publisher Role to Multiple Subjects

This example creates a RoleBinding that grants JobPost publishing permissions to multiple subjects.

# Create RoleBinding with multiple subjects
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: RoleBinding
metadata:
  name: jobpost-publisher-binding
  namespace: anihpj
roleRef:
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
  kind: Role
  name: jobpost-publisher
subjects:
- kind: ServiceAccount
  name: jobpost-service-account
  namespace: anihpj
- kind: User
  name: admin@anihpj.com
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
- kind: Group
  name: publishers
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
EOF
rolebinding.rbac.authorization.k8s.io/jobpost-publisher-binding created

RoleBindingList

RoleBindingList is a collection of RoleBindings

YAML Structure

apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: RoleBindingList
metadata: ListMeta
items: []RoleBinding

Parameters Description

metadata (ListMeta): Standard object's metadata.

items ([]RoleBinding), required: Items is a list of RoleBindings

Return Value

  • RoleBindingList: Returns a list of RoleBinding objects
  • 200: OK
  • 401: Unauthorized

Practical Django Examples

Example 1: List All RoleBindings in Namespace

This example lists all RoleBindings in the anihpj namespace.

# List all RoleBindings
kubectl get rolebindings -n anihpj
NAME ROLE AGE skills-manager-binding Role/skills-admin 5d user-reader-binding Role/user-reader 3d author-editor-binding Role/author-editor 2d location-admin-binding Role/location-admin 1d jobpost-publisher-binding Role/jobpost-publisher 12h
Example 2: List RoleBindings with Custom Columns

This example lists RoleBindings showing specific columns.

# List RoleBindings with custom output
kubectl get rolebindings -n anihpj -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,ROLE:.roleRef.name,KIND:.roleRef.kind
NAME ROLE KIND skills-manager-binding skills-admin Role user-reader-binding user-reader Role author-editor-binding author-editor Role location-admin-binding location-admin Role jobpost-publisher-binding jobpost-publisher Role
Example 3: List RoleBindings in YAML Format

This example retrieves the complete RoleBindingList in YAML format.

# Get RoleBindingList in YAML
kubectl get rolebindings -n anihpj -o yaml
apiVersion: v1 items: - apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1 kind: RoleBinding metadata: name: skills-manager-binding namespace: anihpj roleRef: apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io kind: Role name: skills-admin subjects: - kind: ServiceAccount name: skills-service-account namespace: anihpj kind: List metadata: resourceVersion: ""
Example 4: Filter RoleBindings by Label

This example lists RoleBindings filtered by model label.

# List RoleBindings with label filter
kubectl get rolebindings -n anihpj -l app=jobpost
NAME ROLE AGE jobpost-publisher-binding Role/jobpost-publisher 12h
Example 5: Watch RoleBindings for Changes

This example watches for changes to RoleBindings in real-time.

# Watch RoleBindings
kubectl get rolebindings -n anihpj --watch
NAME ROLE AGE skills-manager-binding Role/skills-admin 5d user-reader-binding Role/user-reader 3d ADDED author-editor-binding Role/author-editor 0s MODIFIED skills-manager-binding Role/skills-admin 5d

RoleBinding Operations

Operations Overview
get - Read the specified RoleBinding
list - List RoleBindings (namespace)
list - List RoleBindings (all namespaces)
create - Create a RoleBinding
update - Replace the specified RoleBinding
patch - Partially update the specified RoleBinding
delete - Delete a RoleBinding
deletecollection - Delete collection of RoleBinding
get - read the specified RoleBinding
HTTP Request
GET /apis/rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/rolebindings/{name}
Parameters
  • name (in path): string, required - name of the RoleBinding
  • namespace (in path): string, required - namespace
  • pretty (in query): string - pretty
Return Value
  • 200 (RoleBinding): OK
  • 401: Unauthorized
Practical Django Examples
Example 1: Get Skills Manager RoleBinding

This example retrieves details of the Skills manager RoleBinding.

# Get RoleBinding details
kubectl get rolebinding skills-manager-binding -n anihpj -o yaml
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1 kind: RoleBinding metadata: name: skills-manager-binding namespace: anihpj creationTimestamp: "2025-01-05T10:30:00Z" resourceVersion: "12345" uid: abc123def-4567-890a-bcde-f1234567890 roleRef: apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io kind: Role name: skills-admin subjects: - kind: ServiceAccount name: skills-service-account namespace: anihpj
Example 2: Get User Reader RoleBinding JSON

This example retrieves the User reader RoleBinding in JSON format.

# Get RoleBinding in JSON
kubectl get rolebinding user-reader-binding -n anihpj -o json
{ "apiVersion": "rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1", "kind": "RoleBinding", "metadata": { "name": "user-reader-binding", "namespace": "anihpj" }, "roleRef": { "apiGroup": "rbac.authorization.k8s.io", "kind": "Role", "name": "user-reader" }, "subjects": [ { "apiGroup": "rbac.authorization.k8s.io", "kind": "Group", "name": "user-viewers" } ] }
Example 3: Describe Author Editor RoleBinding

This example shows detailed information about the Author editor RoleBinding.

# Describe RoleBinding
kubectl describe rolebinding author-editor-binding -n anihpj
Name: author-editor-binding Namespace: anihpj Labels: app=author Annotations: Role: Kind: Role Name: author-editor Subjects: Kind Name Namespace ---- ---- --------- User john@anihpj.com
Example 4: Get Location Admin RoleBinding with Wide Output

This example retrieves Location admin RoleBinding with additional details.

# Get RoleBinding with wide output
kubectl get rolebinding location-admin-binding -n anihpj -o wide
NAME ROLE AGE USERS GROUPS SERVICEACCOUNTS location-admin-binding Role/location-admin 1d anihpj/location-service-account
Example 5: Extract RoleBinding Subjects

This example extracts the subjects from a JobPost RoleBinding.

# Get RoleBinding subjects
kubectl get rolebinding jobpost-publisher-binding -n anihpj -o jsonpath='{.subjects[*].name}'
jobpost-service-account admin@anihpj.com publishers
list - list or watch objects of kind RoleBinding (namespace-scoped)
HTTP Request
GET /apis/rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/rolebindings
Parameters
  • namespace (in path): string, required - namespace
  • allowWatchBookmarks (in query): boolean - allowWatchBookmarks
  • continue (in query): string - continue
  • fieldSelector (in query): string - fieldSelector
  • labelSelector (in query): string - labelSelector
  • limit (in query): integer - limit
  • pretty (in query): string - pretty
  • resourceVersion (in query): string - resourceVersion
  • resourceVersionMatch (in query): string - resourceVersionMatch
  • sendInitialEvents (in query): boolean - sendInitialEvents
  • timeoutSeconds (in query): integer - timeoutSeconds
  • watch (in query): boolean - watch
Return Value
  • 200 (RoleBindingList): OK
  • 401: Unauthorized
Practical Django Examples
Example 1: List All RoleBindings in Anihpj Namespace

This example lists all RoleBindings in the anihpj namespace.

# List RoleBindings in namespace
kubectl get rolebindings -n anihpj
NAME ROLE AGE skills-manager-binding Role/skills-admin 5d user-reader-binding Role/user-reader 3d author-editor-binding Role/author-editor 2d location-admin-binding Role/location-admin 1d jobpost-publisher-binding Role/jobpost-publisher 12h
Example 2: List RoleBindings with Label Selector

This example lists RoleBindings filtered by app label.

# List RoleBindings with label selector
kubectl get rolebindings -n anihpj -l app=jobpost
NAME ROLE AGE jobpost-publisher-binding Role/jobpost-publisher 12h
Example 3: List RoleBindings with Field Selector

This example lists RoleBindings using field selector.

# List RoleBindings with field selector
kubectl get rolebindings -n anihpj --field-selector metadata.name=skills-manager-binding
NAME ROLE AGE skills-manager-binding Role/skills-admin 5d
Example 4: List RoleBindings with Limit

This example lists the first 3 RoleBindings in the namespace.

# List RoleBindings with limit
kubectl get rolebindings -n anihpj --limit=3
NAME ROLE AGE skills-manager-binding Role/skills-admin 5d user-reader-binding Role/user-reader 3d author-editor-binding Role/author-editor 2d
Example 5: Watch RoleBindings in Namespace

This example watches for changes to RoleBindings in anihpj namespace.

# Watch RoleBindings
kubectl get rolebindings -n anihpj --watch
NAME ROLE AGE skills-manager-binding Role/skills-admin 5d user-reader-binding Role/user-reader 3d ADDED location-viewer-binding Role/location-viewer 0s
list - list or watch objects of kind RoleBinding (all namespaces)
HTTP Request
GET /apis/rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1/rolebindings
Parameters
  • allowWatchBookmarks (in query): boolean - allowWatchBookmarks
  • continue (in query): string - continue
  • fieldSelector (in query): string - fieldSelector
  • labelSelector (in query): string - labelSelector
  • limit (in query): integer - limit
  • pretty (in query): string - pretty
  • resourceVersion (in query): string - resourceVersion
  • resourceVersionMatch (in query): string - resourceVersionMatch
  • sendInitialEvents (in query): boolean - sendInitialEvents
  • timeoutSeconds (in query): integer - timeoutSeconds
  • watch (in query): boolean - watch
Return Value
  • 200 (RoleBindingList): OK
  • 401: Unauthorized
Practical Django Examples
Example 1: List All RoleBindings Across All Namespaces

This example lists all RoleBindings across all namespaces in the cluster.

# List all RoleBindings cluster-wide
kubectl get rolebindings --all-namespaces
NAMESPACE NAME ROLE AGE anihpj skills-manager-binding Role/skills-admin 5d anihpj user-reader-binding Role/user-reader 3d anihpj author-editor-binding Role/author-editor 2d production jobpost-admin-binding Role/jobpost-admin 10d staging test-binding Role/test-reader 7d
Example 2: List RoleBindings by Label Across Namespaces

This example lists all RoleBindings with a specific label across all namespaces.

# List RoleBindings with label across all namespaces
kubectl get rolebindings --all-namespaces -l model=jobpost
NAMESPACE NAME ROLE AGE anihpj jobpost-publisher-binding Role/jobpost-publisher 12h production jobpost-admin-binding Role/jobpost-admin 10d
Example 3: Count RoleBindings Across All Namespaces

This example counts the total number of RoleBindings in the cluster.

# Count RoleBindings
kubectl get rolebindings --all-namespaces --no-headers | wc -l
47
Example 4: List RoleBindings in JSON Format

This example retrieves all RoleBindings cluster-wide in JSON format.

# Get all RoleBindings in JSON
kubectl get rolebindings --all-namespaces -o json | jq '.items | length'
47
Example 5: List RoleBindings Sorted by Creation Time

This example lists all RoleBindings sorted by creation timestamp.

# List RoleBindings sorted by age
kubectl get rolebindings --all-namespaces --sort-by=.metadata.creationTimestamp
NAMESPACE NAME ROLE AGE production jobpost-admin-binding Role/jobpost-admin 10d staging test-binding Role/test-reader 7d anihpj skills-manager-binding Role/skills-admin 5d anihpj user-reader-binding Role/user-reader 3d anihpj author-editor-binding Role/author-editor 2d
create - create a RoleBinding
HTTP Request
POST /apis/rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/rolebindings
Parameters
  • namespace (in path): string, required - namespace
  • body: RoleBinding, required
  • dryRun (in query): string - dryRun
  • fieldManager (in query): string - fieldManager
  • fieldValidation (in query): string - fieldValidation
  • pretty (in query): string - pretty
Return Value
  • 200 (RoleBinding): OK
  • 201 (RoleBinding): Created
  • 202 (RoleBinding): Accepted
  • 401: Unauthorized
Practical Django Examples
Example 1: Create Skills Admin RoleBinding

This example creates a RoleBinding granting Skills admin role to a service account.

# Create RoleBinding imperatively
kubectl create rolebinding skills-admin-binding \
  --role=skills-admin \
  --serviceaccount=anihpj:skills-sa \
  --namespace=anihpj
rolebinding.rbac.authorization.k8s.io/skills-admin-binding created
Example 2: Create User Manager RoleBinding with YAML

This example creates a RoleBinding for User model management using YAML.

# Create RoleBinding from YAML
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: RoleBinding
metadata:
  name: user-manager-binding
  namespace: anihpj
  labels:
    app: user
    model: user
roleRef:
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
  kind: Role
  name: user-manager
subjects:
- kind: ServiceAccount
  name: user-service-account
  namespace: anihpj
EOF
rolebinding.rbac.authorization.k8s.io/user-manager-binding created
Example 3: Create Author Viewer RoleBinding for Group

This example creates a RoleBinding granting Author view permissions to a group.

# Create RoleBinding for group
kubectl create rolebinding author-viewer-binding \
  --role=author-viewer \
  --group=author-readers \
  --namespace=anihpj
rolebinding.rbac.authorization.k8s.io/author-viewer-binding created
Example 4: Create Location Editor RoleBinding for User

This example creates a RoleBinding granting Location edit permissions to a specific user.

# Create RoleBinding for user
kubectl create rolebinding location-editor-binding \
  --role=location-editor \
  --user=jane@anihpj.com \
  --namespace=anihpj
rolebinding.rbac.authorization.k8s.io/location-editor-binding created
Example 5: Create JobPost Approver RoleBinding with Dry-Run

This example validates a JobPost approver RoleBinding without creating it.

# Create RoleBinding with dry-run
kubectl create rolebinding jobpost-approver-binding \
  --role=jobpost-approver \
  --serviceaccount=anihpj:jobpost-sa \
  --namespace=anihpj \
  --dry-run=client -o yaml
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1 kind: RoleBinding metadata: name: jobpost-approver-binding namespace: anihpj roleRef: apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io kind: Role name: jobpost-approver subjects: - kind: ServiceAccount name: jobpost-sa namespace: anihpj
update - replace the specified RoleBinding
HTTP Request
PUT /apis/rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/rolebindings/{name}
Parameters
  • name (in path): string, required - name of the RoleBinding
  • namespace (in path): string, required - namespace
  • body: RoleBinding, required
  • dryRun (in query): string - dryRun
  • fieldManager (in query): string - fieldManager
  • fieldValidation (in query): string - fieldValidation
  • pretty (in query): string - pretty
Return Value
  • 200 (RoleBinding): OK
  • 201 (RoleBinding): Created
  • 401: Unauthorized
Practical Django Examples
Example 1: Update Skills RoleBinding Subjects

This example updates the Skills RoleBinding to add additional subjects.

# Update RoleBinding
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: RoleBinding
metadata:
  name: skills-manager-binding
  namespace: anihpj
roleRef:
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
  kind: Role
  name: skills-admin
subjects:
- kind: ServiceAccount
  name: skills-service-account
  namespace: anihpj
- kind: User
  name: admin@anihpj.com
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
EOF
rolebinding.rbac.authorization.k8s.io/skills-manager-binding configured
Example 2: Replace User RoleBinding

This example completely replaces the User RoleBinding configuration.

# Replace RoleBinding
kubectl replace -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: RoleBinding
metadata:
  name: user-reader-binding
  namespace: anihpj
  labels:
    app: user
    updated: "true"
roleRef:
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
  kind: Role
  name: user-reader-v2
subjects:
- kind: Group
  name: user-viewers-updated
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
EOF
rolebinding.rbac.authorization.k8s.io/user-reader-binding replaced
Example 3: Update Author RoleBinding Labels

This example updates the Author RoleBinding with additional labels.

# Get, modify and apply RoleBinding
kubectl get rolebinding author-editor-binding -n anihpj -o yaml | \
  sed 's/labels:/labels:\n  environment: production/' | \
  kubectl apply -f -
rolebinding.rbac.authorization.k8s.io/author-editor-binding configured
Example 4: Update Location RoleBinding with Field Manager

This example updates Location RoleBinding specifying a field manager.

# Update with field manager
kubectl apply -f - --field-manager=location-controller <<EOF
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: RoleBinding
metadata:
  name: location-admin-binding
  namespace: anihpj
roleRef:
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
  kind: Role
  name: location-admin
subjects:
- kind: ServiceAccount
  name: location-controller-sa
  namespace: anihpj
EOF
rolebinding.rbac.authorization.k8s.io/location-admin-binding configured
Example 5: Update JobPost RoleBinding with Validation

This example updates JobPost RoleBinding with strict field validation.

# Update with strict validation
kubectl apply -f - --validate=strict <<EOF
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: RoleBinding
metadata:
  name: jobpost-publisher-binding
  namespace: anihpj
roleRef:
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
  kind: Role
  name: jobpost-publisher
subjects:
- kind: ServiceAccount
  name: jobpost-publisher-sa
  namespace: anihpj
- kind: Group
  name: publishers
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
EOF
rolebinding.rbac.authorization.k8s.io/jobpost-publisher-binding configured
patch - partially update the specified RoleBinding
HTTP Request
PATCH /apis/rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/rolebindings/{name}
Parameters
  • name (in path): string, required - name of the RoleBinding
  • namespace (in path): string, required - namespace
  • body: Patch, required
  • dryRun (in query): string - dryRun
  • fieldManager (in query): string - fieldManager
  • fieldValidation (in query): string - fieldValidation
  • force (in query): boolean - force
  • pretty (in query): string - pretty
Return Value
  • 200 (RoleBinding): OK
  • 201 (RoleBinding): Created
  • 401: Unauthorized
Practical Django Examples
Example 1: Patch Skills RoleBinding Labels

This example adds labels to the Skills RoleBinding using JSON patch.
