prioritylevelconfiguration.flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io "skills-api-priority" deleted prioritylevelconfiguration.flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io "user-api-priority" deleted prioritylevelconfiguration.flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io "author-api-priority" deleted prioritylevelconfiguration.flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io "location-api-priority" deleted prioritylevelconfiguration.flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io "jobpost-api-critical" deleted
Example 2: Delete Limited Type Priority Levels

Remove all Limited type priority levels for specific service.

# Delete Skills service priorities
kubectl delete prioritylevelconfigurations -l service=skills
prioritylevelconfiguration.flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io "skills-api-priority" deleted
Example 3: Delete Priority Levels with Dry Run

Test deletion of User service priorities.

# Dry-run delete User priorities
kubectl delete prioritylevelconfigurations -l service=user --dry-run=client
prioritylevelconfiguration.flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io "user-api-priority" deleted (dry run)
Example 4: Delete Multiple Priority Levels by Label

Remove Author and Location priority levels.

# Delete multiple services
kubectl delete prioritylevelconfigurations -l 'service in (author,location)'
prioritylevelconfiguration.flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io "author-api-priority" deleted prioritylevelconfiguration.flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io "location-api-priority" deleted
Example 5: Delete Non-Exempt Priority Levels

Remove all anihpj priorities except exempt tier.

# Delete all anihpj priorities except critical
kubectl delete prioritylevelconfigurations -l app=anihpj,tier!=critical
prioritylevelconfiguration.flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io "skills-api-priority" deleted prioritylevelconfiguration.flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io "user-api-priority" deleted prioritylevelconfiguration.flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io "author-api-priority" deleted prioritylevelconfiguration.flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io "location-api-priority" deleted

ValidatingAdmissionPolicy

📋 ValidatingAdmissionPolicy Overview

ValidatingAdmissionPolicyList - Collection of admission policies
ValidatingAdmissionPolicyBinding - Bind policy to resources
Operations - API operations

Official API Documentation: ValidatingAdmissionPolicy v1 Policy Resources

API Version: admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1

Full Description

ValidatingAdmissionPolicy describes admission validation policies that accept or reject objects without modifying them, using CEL (Common Expression Language) expressions. These policies provide cluster administrators with declarative, in-process alternatives to validating admission webhooks for enforcing business rules and constraints. In the anihpj Django application, ValidatingAdmissionPolicies validate Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost resources to ensure data integrity, security compliance, and operational standards before objects are persisted to the cluster.

ValidatingAdmissionPolicies use CEL expressions to evaluate admission requests, with access to object fields, parameters, and authorization context. Policies can be bound to specific resources using ValidatingAdmissionPolicyBinding, with parameterized configurations for multi-tenant scenarios. The failurePolicy determines whether validation failures result in denied requests (Fail) or warnings (Ignore), while validationActions specify enforcement behavior (Deny, Warn, Audit). For the anihpj jobpost application, policies validate JobPost creation rules, enforce User permission boundaries, ensure Author compliance, validate Location constraints, and verify Skills taxonomy integrity.

Syntax

# Apply a ValidatingAdmissionPolicy
kubectl apply -f validating-policy.yaml

# Get a ValidatingAdmissionPolicy
kubectl get validatingadmissionpolicy <policy-name>

# Describe a ValidatingAdmissionPolicy
kubectl describe validatingadmissionpolicy <policy-name>

# Get ValidatingAdmissionPolicy in YAML
kubectl get validatingadmissionpolicy <policy-name> -o yaml

# Get ValidatingAdmissionPolicy in JSON
kubectl get validatingadmissionpolicy <policy-name> -o json

# Delete a ValidatingAdmissionPolicy
kubectl delete validatingadmissionpolicy <policy-name>

YAML Structure

apiVersion: admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1
kind: ValidatingAdmissionPolicy
metadata:
  name: jobpost-validation-policy
spec:
  failurePolicy: Fail
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
    - apiGroups: ["apps"]
      apiVersions: ["v1"]
      operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
      resources: ["deployments"]
  validations:
  - expression: "object.spec.replicas <= 100"
    message: "Replica count must not exceed 100"
  - expression: "has(object.metadata.labels.app)"
    message: "Deployment must have 'app' label"

Parameters Description

  • apiVersion (string): admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1 - API version
  • kind (string): ValidatingAdmissionPolicy - resource type
  • metadata (ObjectMeta): Standard object metadata
    • name (string): Policy name
    • labels (map[string]string): Labels for policy organization
  • spec (ValidatingAdmissionPolicySpec): Policy specification
    • failurePolicy (string): How to handle failures - "Fail" or "Ignore" (default: Fail)
    • matchConstraints (MatchResources, required): Resources this policy validates
      • resourceRules ([]NamedRuleWithOperations): Resources to match
      • namespaceSelector (LabelSelector): Namespace matching
      • objectSelector (LabelSelector): Object label matching
    • validations ([]Validation): CEL expressions for validation
      • expression (string, required): CEL expression returning boolean
      • message (string): Failure message
      • reason (string): Machine-readable failure reason
    • paramKind (ParamKind): Parameter resource type for policy configuration
    • auditAnnotations ([]AuditAnnotation): CEL expressions producing audit annotations
    • variables ([]Variable): Variable definitions for expression composition
  • status (ValidatingAdmissionPolicyStatus): Policy status (read-only)
    • conditions ([]Condition): Current policy state conditions
    • observedGeneration (int64): Generation observed by controller
    • typeChecking (TypeChecking): CEL expression type check results

Return Value

  • 200 (ValidatingAdmissionPolicy): OK - Policy retrieved/updated successfully
  • 201 (ValidatingAdmissionPolicy): Created - Policy created successfully
  • 202 (ValidatingAdmissionPolicy): Accepted - Request accepted for processing
  • 401: Unauthorized - Authentication required

5 Practical Django Examples

Example 1: JobPost Replica Limit Policy

Validate JobPost deployments have reasonable replica counts.

# Create JobPost replica validation policy
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1
kind: ValidatingAdmissionPolicy
metadata:
  name: jobpost-replica-limit
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    service: jobpost
spec:
  failurePolicy: Fail
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
    - apiGroups: ["apps"]
      apiVersions: ["v1"]
      operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
      resources: ["deployments"]
      resourceNames: ["jobpost-*"]
  validations:
  - expression: "object.spec.replicas <= 50"
    message: "JobPost replicas must not exceed 50"
    reason: "Invalid"
  - expression: "object.spec.replicas >= 2"
    message: "JobPost must have at least 2 replicas for HA"
EOF
validatingadmissionpolicy.admissionregistration.k8s.io/jobpost-replica-limit created
Example 2: Skills Service Label Requirement

Enforce required labels on Skills service resources.

# Create Skills label validation policy
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1
kind: ValidatingAdmissionPolicy
metadata:
  name: skills-label-policy
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    service: skills
spec:
  failurePolicy: Fail
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
    - apiGroups: ["apps"]
      apiVersions: ["v1"]
      operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
      resources: ["deployments", "statefulsets"]
    namespaceSelector:
      matchLabels:
        env: production
  validations:
  - expression: "has(object.metadata.labels.app) && has(object.metadata.labels.service)"
    message: "Resources must have 'app' and 'service' labels"
  - expression: "has(object.metadata.labels.version)"
    message: "Resources must have 'version' label"
EOF
validatingadmissionpolicy.admissionregistration.k8s.io/skills-label-policy created
Example 3: User Service Resource Quota Policy

Validate User service resource requests and limits.

# Create User resource validation policy
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1
kind: ValidatingAdmissionPolicy
metadata:
  name: user-resource-policy
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    service: user
spec:
  failurePolicy: Fail
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
    - apiGroups: [""]
      apiVersions: ["v1"]
      operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
      resources: ["pods"]
  validations:
  - expression: "object.spec.containers.all(c, has(c.resources.requests.memory))"
    message: "All containers must specify memory requests"
  - expression: "object.spec.containers.all(c, has(c.resources.limits.cpu))"
    message: "All containers must specify CPU limits"
  - expression: |
      object.spec.containers.all(c,
        int(c.resources.requests.memory.replace('Gi','').replace('Mi','')) <= 2048
      )
    message: "Memory requests must not exceed 2Gi"
EOF
validatingadmissionpolicy.admissionregistration.k8s.io/user-resource-policy created
Example 4: Author Service Security Policy

Enforce security standards for Author service pods.

# Create Author security validation policy
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1
kind: ValidatingAdmissionPolicy
metadata:
  name: author-security-policy
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    service: author
spec:
  failurePolicy: Fail
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
    - apiGroups: [""]
      apiVersions: ["v1"]
      operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
      resources: ["pods"]
    objectSelector:
      matchLabels:
        service: author
  validations:
  - expression: "!object.spec.hostNetwork"
    message: "Pods must not use hostNetwork"
  - expression: "!object.spec.hostPID"
    message: "Pods must not use hostPID"
  - expression: "object.spec.securityContext.runAsNonRoot == true"
    message: "Pods must run as non-root user"
  auditAnnotations:
  - key: "security-validated"
    valueExpression: "'Author pod security validated at ' + string(request.requestReceivedTimestamp)"
EOF
validatingadmissionpolicy.admissionregistration.k8s.io/author-security-policy created
Example 5: Location Service Multi-Validation Policy

Complex validation policy for Location service with variables.

# Create Location comprehensive validation policy
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1
kind: ValidatingAdmissionPolicy
metadata:
  name: location-validation-policy
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    service: location
spec:
  failurePolicy: Fail
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
    - apiGroups: ["apps"]
      apiVersions: ["v1"]
      operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
      resources: ["deployments"]
  variables:
  - name: maxReplicas
    expression: "50"
  - name: minReplicas
    expression: "2"
  - name: hasRequiredLabels
    expression: "has(object.metadata.labels.app) && has(object.metadata.labels.env)"
  validations:
  - expression: "variables.hasRequiredLabels"
    message: "Deployment must have required labels"
  - expression: "object.spec.replicas >= variables.minReplicas && object.spec.replicas <= variables.maxReplicas"
    message: "Replica count must be between 2 and 50"
  - expression: "has(object.spec.template.spec.affinity.podAntiAffinity)"
    message: "Location deployments should use pod anti-affinity for HA"
EOF
validatingadmissionpolicy.admissionregistration.k8s.io/location-validation-policy created

ValidatingAdmissionPolicyList

ValidatingAdmissionPolicyList is a collection of ValidatingAdmissionPolicy resources for bulk operations and list management.

Syntax
# List all ValidatingAdmissionPolicies
kubectl get validatingadmissionpolicies

# List with custom columns
kubectl get validatingadmissionpolicies -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,FAILURE-POLICY:.spec.failurePolicy

# List in JSON format
kubectl get validatingadmissionpolicies -o json
Parameters
  • apiVersion (string): admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1
  • kind (string): ValidatingAdmissionPolicyList
  • metadata (ListMeta): Standard list metadata with resourceVersion, continue token, remainingItemCount
  • items ([]ValidatingAdmissionPolicy, required): Array of ValidatingAdmissionPolicy objects
Return Value

Returns a ValidatingAdmissionPolicyList object containing all matching policies.

5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: List All anihpj Policies

Get all validation policies for anihpj application.

# List anihpj validation policies
kubectl get validatingadmissionpolicies -l app=anihpj
NAME AGE jobpost-replica-limit 5d skills-label-policy 5d user-resource-policy 5d author-security-policy 5d location-validation-policy 5d
Example 2: List Policies with Failure Policy

Show policy names and their failure policies.

# List with failure policy column
kubectl get validatingadmissionpolicies -l app=anihpj -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,FAILURE:.spec.failurePolicy
NAME FAILURE jobpost-replica-limit Fail skills-label-policy Fail user-resource-policy Fail author-security-policy Fail location-validation-policy Fail
Example 3: Extract Policy Names

Get just the policy names using JSON extraction.

# Extract policy names
kubectl get validatingadmissionpolicies -l app=anihpj -o json | jq -r '.items[].metadata.name'
jobpost-replica-limit skills-label-policy user-resource-policy author-security-policy location-validation-policy
Example 4: Count Total Policies

Count validation policies for anihpj services.

# Count anihpj policies
kubectl get validatingadmissionpolicies -l app=anihpj --no-headers | wc -l
5
Example 5: Wide Format List

List policies with additional details.

# List in wide format
kubectl get validatingadmissionpolicies -l app=anihpj -o wide
NAME AGE FAILURE-POLICY jobpost-replica-limit 5d Fail skills-label-policy 5d Fail user-resource-policy 5d Fail author-security-policy 5d Fail location-validation-policy 5d Fail

ValidatingAdmissionPolicyBinding

ValidatingAdmissionPolicyBinding binds a ValidatingAdmissionPolicy to specific resources with optional parameters. Bindings determine which resources are validated by which policies.

Syntax
# Apply a policy binding
kubectl apply -f policy-binding.yaml

# Get a binding
kubectl get validatingadmissionpolicybinding <binding-name>

# List all bindings
kubectl get validatingadmissionpolicybindings

# Delete a binding
kubectl delete validatingadmissionpolicybinding <binding-name>
Parameters
  • apiVersion (string): admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1
  • kind (string): ValidatingAdmissionPolicyBinding
  • metadata (ObjectMeta): Standard object metadata
  • spec (ValidatingAdmissionPolicyBindingSpec): Binding specification
    • policyName (string, required): Name of the ValidatingAdmissionPolicy to bind
    • validationActions ([]string, required): Actions like "Deny", "Warn", "Audit"
    • paramRef (ParamRef): Reference to parameter resource
    • matchResources (MatchResources): Additional resource matching (intersects with policy matchConstraints)
Return Value

Returns a ValidatingAdmissionPolicyBinding object.

5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: Bind JobPost Replica Policy

Bind replica limit policy to JobPost deployments.

# Create JobPost policy binding
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1
kind: ValidatingAdmissionPolicyBinding
metadata:
  name: jobpost-replica-binding
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    service: jobpost
spec:
  policyName: jobpost-replica-limit
  validationActions: ["Deny"]
  matchResources:
    namespaceSelector:
      matchLabels:
        env: production
EOF
validatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io/jobpost-replica-binding created
Example 2: Bind Skills Label Policy with Warn

Bind label policy to Skills service with warning action.

# Create Skills policy binding with Warn
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1
kind: ValidatingAdmissionPolicyBinding
metadata:
  name: skills-label-binding
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    service: skills
spec:
  policyName: skills-label-policy
  validationActions: ["Warn", "Audit"]
EOF
validatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io/skills-label-binding created
Example 3: Bind User Resource Policy

Bind resource policy to User service pods.

# Create User policy binding
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1
kind: ValidatingAdmissionPolicyBinding
metadata:
  name: user-resource-binding
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    service: user
spec:
  policyName: user-resource-policy
  validationActions: ["Deny", "Audit"]
  matchResources:
    objectSelector:
      matchLabels:
        service: user
EOF
validatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io/user-resource-binding created
Example 4: Bind Author Security Policy

Bind security policy to Author service.

# Create Author policy binding
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1
kind: ValidatingAdmissionPolicyBinding
metadata:
  name: author-security-binding
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    service: author
spec:
  policyName: author-security-policy
  validationActions: ["Deny"]
EOF
validatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io/author-security-binding created
Example 5: Bind Location Policy with Parameters

Bind Location policy with namespace-specific parameters.

# Create Location policy binding
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1
kind: ValidatingAdmissionPolicyBinding
metadata:
  name: location-validation-binding
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    service: location
spec:
  policyName: location-validation-policy
  validationActions: ["Deny", "Audit"]
  matchResources:
    namespaceSelector:
      matchExpressions:
      - key: env
        operator: In
        values: ["production", "staging"]
EOF
validatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io/location-validation-binding created

Operations

The following operations are available for managing ValidatingAdmissionPolicy resources in the anihpj jobpost application for enforcing admission validation rules.

get - Read specified ValidatingAdmissionPolicy
get-status - Read policy status
list - List all policies
create - Create a policy
update - Replace policy
update-status - Replace policy status
patch - Partially update policy
patch-status - Partially update status
delete - Delete a policy
deletecollection - Delete multiple policies

get - read the specified ValidatingAdmissionPolicy

Retrieve detailed information about a specific ValidatingAdmissionPolicy.

HTTP Request
GET /apis/admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1/validatingadmissionpolicies/{name}
Parameters
  • name (in path): string, required - name of the ValidatingAdmissionPolicy
  • pretty (in query): string - pretty print output
Return Value
  • 200 (ValidatingAdmissionPolicy): OK
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: Get JobPost Replica Policy

Retrieve JobPost replica validation policy in YAML.

# Get JobPost replica policy
kubectl get validatingadmissionpolicy jobpost-replica-limit -o yaml
apiVersion: admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1 kind: ValidatingAdmissionPolicy metadata: labels: app: anihpj service: jobpost name: jobpost-replica-limit spec: failurePolicy: Fail matchConstraints: resourceRules: - apiGroups: ["apps"] apiVersions: ["v1"] operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"] resources: ["deployments"] resourceNames: ["jobpost-*"] validations: - expression: "object.spec.replicas <= 50" message: "JobPost replicas must not exceed 50" reason: "Invalid"
Example 2: Get Skills Label Policy Details

Describe Skills label validation policy.

# Describe Skills label policy
kubectl describe validatingadmissionpolicy skills-label-policy
Name: skills-label-policy API Version: admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1 Kind: ValidatingAdmissionPolicy Metadata: Labels: app: anihpj service: skills Spec: Failure Policy: Fail Match Constraints: Resource Rules: Api Groups: apps Api Versions: v1 Operations: CREATE, UPDATE Resources: deployments, statefulsets Validations: Expression: has(object.metadata.labels.app) && has(object.metadata.labels.service) Message: Resources must have 'app' and 'service' labels Events:
Example 3: Get User Resource Policy in JSON

Retrieve User resource policy as JSON.

# Get User resource policy in JSON
kubectl get validatingadmissionpolicy user-resource-policy -o json
{ "apiVersion": "admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1", "kind": "ValidatingAdmissionPolicy", "metadata": { "labels": { "app": "anihpj", "service": "user" }, "name": "user-resource-policy" }, "spec": { "failurePolicy": "Fail", "matchConstraints": { "resourceRules": [{ "apiGroups": [""], "apiVersions": ["v1"], "operations": ["CREATE", "UPDATE"], "resources": ["pods"] }] }, "validations": [{ "expression": "object.spec.containers.all(c, has(c.resources.requests.memory))", "message": "All containers must specify memory requests" }] } }
Example 4: Extract Author Policy Validations

Get Author security policy validation rules.

# Extract validation expressions
kubectl get validatingadmissionpolicy author-security-policy -o jsonpath='{.spec.validations[*].expression}'
!object.spec.hostNetwork !object.spec.hostPID object.spec.securityContext.runAsNonRoot == true
Example 5: Get Location Policy Failure Policy

Check Location policy failure handling.

# Get Location policy failure mode
kubectl get validatingadmissionpolicy location-validation-policy -o jsonpath='{.spec.failurePolicy}'
Fail

get - read status of the specified ValidatingAdmissionPolicy

Retrieve status information for a specific ValidatingAdmissionPolicy, including type checking results.

HTTP Request
GET /apis/admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1/validatingadmissionpolicies/{name}/status
Parameters
  • name (in path): string, required - name of the ValidatingAdmissionPolicy
  • pretty (in query): string - pretty print output
Return Value
  • 200 (ValidatingAdmissionPolicy): OK
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: Get JobPost Policy Status

Check JobPost replica policy status.

# Get JobPost policy status
kubectl get validatingadmissionpolicy jobpost-replica-limit -o jsonpath='{.status}' | jq
{ "conditions": [{ "lastTransitionTime": "2025-12-15T10:00:00Z", "message": "Policy configured successfully", "reason": "Accepted", "status": "True", "type": "Accepted" }], "observedGeneration": 1, "typeChecking": { "expressionWarnings": [] } }
Example 2: Check Skills Policy Conditions

View Skills label policy conditions.

# Check Skills policy conditions
kubectl get validatingadmissionpolicy skills-label-policy -o jsonpath='{.status.conditions}'
[{"lastTransitionTime":"2025-12-15T10:05:00Z","message":"","reason":"Accepted","status":"True","type":"Accepted"}]
Example 3: Get User Policy Type Checking

Verify User resource policy CEL type checking.

# Check User policy type checking
kubectl get validatingadmissionpolicy user-resource-policy -o yaml | grep -A 5 typeChecking:
typeChecking: expressionWarnings: []
Example 4: Monitor Author Policy Status

Track Author security policy acceptance status.

# Get Author policy acceptance status
kubectl get validatingadmissionpolicy author-security-policy -o jsonpath='{.status.conditions[?(@.type=="Accepted")].status}'
True
Example 5: Get Location Policy Observed Generation

Check Location policy sync status.

# Get Location policy observed generation
kubectl get validatingadmissionpolicy location-validation-policy -o json | jq '.status.observedGeneration'
1

list - list or watch objects of kind ValidatingAdmissionPolicy

List all ValidatingAdmissionPolicies in the cluster with optional filtering.

HTTP Request
GET /apis/admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1/validatingadmissionpolicies
Parameters
  • allowWatchBookmarks (in query): boolean - allow watch bookmarks
  • continue (in query): string - continue token for pagination
  • fieldSelector (in query): string - field selector to filter results
  • labelSelector (in query): string - label selector to filter results
  • limit (in query): integer - maximum number of results
  • pretty (in query): string - pretty print output
  • resourceVersion (in query): string - resource version
  • resourceVersionMatch (in query): string - resource version match strategy
  • sendInitialEvents (in query): boolean - send initial events
  • timeoutSeconds (in query): integer - timeout in seconds
  • watch (in query): boolean - watch for changes
Return Value
  • 200 (ValidatingAdmissionPolicyList): OK
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: List All anihpj Policies

Get all validation policies for anihpj services.

# List all anihpj policies
kubectl get validatingadmissionpolicies -l app=anihpj
NAME AGE jobpost-replica-limit 5d skills-label-policy 5d user-resource-policy 5d author-security-policy 5d location-validation-policy 5d
Example 2: List Policies by Service

Filter policies by service label.

# List Skills service policies
kubectl get validatingadmissionpolicies -l service=skills
NAME AGE skills-label-policy 5d
Example 3: List Policies with Custom Columns

Display policies with specific information.

# List with custom columns
kubectl get validatingadmissionpolicies -l app=anihpj -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,FAILURE:.spec.failurePolicy,VALIDATIONS:.spec.validations[*].message
NAME FAILURE VALIDATIONS jobpost-replica-limit Fail JobPost replicas must not exceed 50 skills-label-policy Fail Resources must have 'app' and 'service' labels user-resource-policy Fail All containers must specify memory requests author-security-policy Fail Pods must not use hostNetwork location-validation-policy Fail Deployment must have required labels
Example 4: Watch Policy Changes

Monitor changes to validation policies in real-time.

# Watch anihpj policies
kubectl get validatingadmissionpolicies -l app=anihpj --watch
NAME AGE jobpost-replica-limit 5d skills-label-policy 5d
Example 5: List Policies in JSON

Get policies as JSON for processing.

# List in JSON format
kubectl get validatingadmissionpolicies -l app=anihpj -o json | jq '.items[] | {name: .metadata.name, failurePolicy: .spec.failurePolicy}'
{ "name": "jobpost-replica-limit", "failurePolicy": "Fail" } { "name": "skills-label-policy", "failurePolicy": "Fail" }

create - create a ValidatingAdmissionPolicy

Create a new ValidatingAdmissionPolicy to enforce admission validation rules.

HTTP Request
POST /apis/admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1/validatingadmissionpolicies
Parameters
  • body: ValidatingAdmissionPolicy, required - ValidatingAdmissionPolicy object to create
  • dryRun (in query): string - simulate the operation without persisting
  • fieldManager (in query): string - name of the manager creating this resource
  • fieldValidation (in query): string - validation level (Strict, Warn, Ignore)
  • pretty (in query): string - pretty print output
Return Value
  • 200 (ValidatingAdmissionPolicy): OK
  • 201 (ValidatingAdmissionPolicy): Created
  • 202 (ValidatingAdmissionPolicy): Accepted
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: Create JobPost Replica Policy

Create replica limit validation for JobPost deployments.

validatingadmissionpolicy.admissionregistration.k8s.io/jobpost-replica-limit created
Example 2: Create Skills Label Policy

Set up label validation for Skills service.

validatingadmissionpolicy.admissionregistration.k8s.io/skills-label-policy created

kubectl apply -f user-resource-policy.yaml --dry-run=server
validatingadmissionpolicy.admissionregistration.k8s.io/user-resource-policy created (server dry run)
Example 4: Create Author Security Policy

Create security validation for Author service pods.

# Apply Author security policy
kubectl apply -f author-security-policy.yaml
validatingadmissionpolicy.admissionregistration.k8s.io/author-security-policy created
Example 5: Create Location Validation Policy

Create comprehensive validation for Location deployments.

# Create Location validation policy
kubectl apply -f location-validation-policy.yaml
validatingadmissionpolicy.admissionregistration.k8s.io/location-validation-policy created

HTTP Request
PUT /apis/admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1/validatingadmissionpolicies/{name}
Parameters
  • name (in path): string, required - name of the ValidatingAdmissionPolicy

  • fieldManager (in query): string - name of the manager updating this resource
  • fieldValidation (in query): string - validation level (Strict, Warn, Ignore)

Return Value
  • 200 (ValidatingAdmissionPolicy): OK

5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: Update JobPost Replica Limit

Increase JobPost replica limit from 50 to 100.

# Edit and update JobPost policy

validatingadmissionpolicy.admissionregistration.k8s.io/jobpost-replica-limit edited
Example 2: Update Skills Policy Validations

Modify Skills label validation expressions.

# Get, modify, and apply Skills policy
kubectl get validatingadmissionpolicy skills-label-policy -o yaml > skills-policy.yaml
# Edit skills-policy.yaml
kubectl apply -f skills-policy.yaml
validatingadmissionpolicy.admissionregistration.k8s.io/skills-label-policy configured
Example 3: Update User Policy Failure Mode

Change User policy from Fail to Ignore.

# Replace User policy
kubectl replace -f user-resource-policy-v2.yaml

Example 4: Update Author Policy with Dry Run

Test Author policy update without applying.

# Update Author policy with dry-run

validatingadmissionpolicy.admissionregistration.k8s.io/author-security-policy configured (server dry run)

Modify Location policy variable definitions.

# Apply updated Location policy

validatingadmissionpolicy.admissionregistration.k8s.io/location-validation-policy configured

update - replace status of the specified ValidatingAdmissionPolicy

HTTP Request
PUT /apis/admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1/validatingadmissionpolicies/{name}/status
Parameters
  • name (in path): string, required - name of the ValidatingAdmissionPolicy
  • body: ValidatingAdmissionPolicy, required - updated ValidatingAdmissionPolicy object
  • dryRun (in query): string - simulate the operation without persisting

  • pretty (in query): string - pretty print output

  • 200 (ValidatingAdmissionPolicy): OK

Example 1: View JobPost Policy Status

Check JobPost replica policy status (typically managed by controller).

# View JobPost policy status
kubectl get validatingadmissionpolicy jobpost-replica-limit -o jsonpath='{.status}'
{"conditions":[{"lastTransitionTime":"2025-12-15T10:00:00Z","message":"Policy configured successfully","reason":"Accepted","status":"True","type":"Accepted"}],"observedGeneration":1}
Example 2: Monitor Skills Policy Conditions

Observe Skills label policy health status.

# Check Skills policy conditions
kubectl describe validatingadmissionpolicy skills-label-policy | grep -A 5 Status:
Status: Conditions: Last Transition Time: 2025-12-15T10:05:00Z Reason: Accepted Status: True Type: Accepted
Example 3: Check User Policy Type Checking

View User resource policy CEL validation status.

# Get User policy type checking status
kubectl get validatingadmissionpolicy user-resource-policy -o yaml | grep -A 3 typeChecking:
typeChecking: expressionWarnings: []
Example 4: Monitor Author Policy Status

Track Author security policy conditions.

kubectl get validatingadmissionpolicy author-security-policy -w -o jsonpath='{.status.conditions[*].status}'
True
Example 5: Verify Location Policy Acceptance

Confirm Location validation policy acceptance.

# Get Location policy acceptance status
kubectl get validatingadmissionpolicy location-validation-policy -o json | jq '.status.conditions[] | select(.type=="Accepted")'

patch - partially update the specified ValidatingAdmissionPolicy

Partially update a ValidatingAdmissionPolicy using JSON patch, merge patch, or strategic merge patch.

HTTP Request
PATCH /apis/admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1/validatingadmissionpolicies/{name}
Parameters
  • name (in path): string, required - name of the ValidatingAdmissionPolicy
  • body: Patch, required - patch to apply
  • dryRun (in query): string - simulate the operation without persisting
  • fieldManager (in query): string - name of the manager patching this resource
  • fieldValidation (in query): string - validation level (Strict, Warn, Ignore)
  • force (in query): boolean - force apply the patch
  • pretty (in query): string - pretty print output
Return Value
  • 200 (ValidatingAdmissionPolicy): OK
  • 201 (ValidatingAdmissionPolicy): Created
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: Patch JobPost Replica Limit

Update JobPost replica maximum using JSON patch.

# Patch JobPost replica limit
kubectl patch validatingadmissionpolicy jobpost-replica-limit --type='json' -p='[{"op": "replace", "path": "/spec/validations/0/expression", "value": "object.spec.replicas <= 100"}]'
validatingadmissionpolicy.admissionregistration.k8s.io/jobpost-replica-limit patched
Example 2: Patch Skills Policy Labels

Add or update Skills policy labels.

# Patch Skills policy labels
kubectl patch validatingadmissionpolicy skills-label-policy --type='merge' -p='{"metadata":{"labels":{"tier":"standard","env":"production"}}}'
validatingadmissionpolicy.admissionregistration.k8s.io/skills-label-policy patched
Example 3: Patch User Policy Failure Mode

Modify User resource policy failure handling.

# Patch User policy failure mode
kubectl patch validatingadmissionpolicy user-resource-policy --type='merge' -p='{"spec":{"failurePolicy":"Ignore"}}'
validatingadmissionpolicy.admissionregistration.k8s.io/user-resource-policy patched
Example 4: Patch Author Policy Add Validation

Add new validation expression to Author security policy.

# Patch Author policy validations
kubectl patch validatingadmissionpolicy author-security-policy --type='json' -p='[{"op": "add", "path": "/spec/validations/-", "value": {"expression": "!object.spec.hostIPC", "message": "Pods must not use hostIPC"}}]'
validatingadmissionpolicy.admissionregistration.k8s.io/author-security-policy patched
Example 5: Patch Location Policy Multiple Fields

Update Location policy with multiple changes.

# Patch Location policy
kubectl patch validatingadmissionpolicy location-validation-policy --type='merge' -p='{"metadata":{"annotations":{"description":"Location deployment validation"}}, "spec":{"variables":[{"name":"maxReplicas","expression":"75"}]}}'
validatingadmissionpolicy.admissionregistration.k8s.io/location-validation-policy patched

patch - partially update status of the specified ValidatingAdmissionPolicy

PATCH /apis/admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1/validatingadmissionpolicies/{name}/status

  • fieldValidation (in query): string - validation level (Strict, Warn, Ignore)

Return Value

  • 201 (ValidatingAdmissionPolicy): Created

Accepted
Example 2: Check Skills Policy Conditions

View Skills label policy conditions.

# Get Skills policy conditions
kubectl get validatingadmissionpolicy skills-label-policy -o json | jq '.status.conditions'
[{ "lastTransitionTime": "2025-12-15T10:05:00Z", "message": "", "reason": "Accepted", "status": "True", "type": "Accepted" }]
Example 3: Monitor User Policy Health

Track User resource policy status.

# Describe User policy status

Status: Conditions: Last Transition Time: 2025-12-15T10:10:00Z Reason: Accepted Status: True Type: Accepted Observed Generation: 1

Verify Author security policy operational state.

# Get Author policy status

status: conditions: - lastTransitionTime: "2025-12-15T10:12:00Z" message: "" reason: Accepted status: "True" type: Accepted observedGeneration: 1

Track Location validation policy health.

{"lastTransitionTime":"2025-12-15T10:15:00Z","message":"","reason":"Accepted","status":"True","type":"Accepted"}

delete - delete a ValidatingAdmissionPolicy

Delete a specific ValidatingAdmissionPolicy resource.

HTTP Request
DELETE /apis/admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1/validatingadmissionpolicies/{name}
Parameters
  • name (in path): string, required - name of the ValidatingAdmissionPolicy
  • body: DeleteOptions - delete options
  • dryRun (in query): string - simulate the operation without persisting
  • gracePeriodSeconds (in query): integer - grace period before deletion
  • ignoreStoreReadErrorWithClusterBreakingPotential (in query): boolean - ignore store read errors
  • pretty (in query): string - pretty print output
  • propagationPolicy (in query): string - propagation policy (Orphan, Background, Foreground)
Return Value
  • 200 (Status): OK
  • 202 (Status): Accepted
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: Delete JobPost Replica Policy

Remove JobPost replica validation policy.

kubectl delete validatingadmissionpolicy jobpost-replica-limit
validatingadmissionpolicy.admissionregistration.k8s.io "jobpost-replica-limit" deleted
Example 2: Delete Skills Policy with Confirmation

Delete Skills label policy with explicit confirmation.

# Delete Skills policy
kubectl delete validatingadmissionpolicy skills-label-policy --wait=true
validatingadmissionpolicy.admissionregistration.k8s.io "skills-label-policy" deleted
Example 3: Delete User Policy with Dry Run

Test User resource policy deletion without applying.

# Dry-run delete User policy
kubectl delete validatingadmissionpolicy user-resource-policy --dry-run=client
validatingadmissionpolicy.admissionregistration.k8s.io "user-resource-policy" deleted (dry run)
Example 4: Delete Author Policy Immediately

Force immediate deletion of Author security policy.

# Force delete Author policy
kubectl delete validatingadmissionpolicy author-security-policy --grace-period=0 --force
warning: Immediate deletion does not wait for confirmation that the running resource has been terminated. The resource may continue to run on the cluster indefinitely. validatingadmissionpolicy.admissionregistration.k8s.io "author-security-policy" force deleted
Example 5: Delete Location Policy from YAML

Remove Location validation policy using YAML file.

# Delete Location policy from file
kubectl delete -f location-validation-policy.yaml
validatingadmissionpolicy.admissionregistration.k8s.io "location-validation-policy" deleted

deletecollection - delete collection of ValidatingAdmissionPolicy

Delete multiple ValidatingAdmissionPolicies matching specified criteria.

HTTP Request
DELETE /apis/admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1/validatingadmissionpolicies
Parameters

5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: Delete All anihpj Policies

Remove all validation policies with app label.

# Delete all anihpj validation policies
kubectl delete validatingadmissionpolicies -l app=anihpj
validatingadmissionpolicy.admissionregistration.k8s.io "jobpost-replica-limit" deleted validatingadmissionpolicy.admissionregistration.k8s.io "skills-label-policy" deleted validatingadmissionpolicy.admissionregistration.k8s.io "user-resource-policy" deleted validatingadmissionpolicy.admissionregistration.k8s.io "author-security-policy" deleted validatingadmissionpolicy.admissionregistration.k8s.io "location-validation-policy" deleted
Example 2: Delete Policies by Service

Remove all policies for specific service.

# Delete Skills service policies
kubectl delete validatingadmissionpolicies -l service=skills
validatingadmissionpolicy.admissionregistration.k8s.io "skills-label-policy" deleted
Example 3: Delete Policies with Dry Run

Test deletion of User service policies.

# Dry-run delete User policies
kubectl delete validatingadmissionpolicies -l service=user --dry-run=client
validatingadmissionpolicy.admissionregistration.k8s.io "user-resource-policy" deleted (dry run)
Example 4: Delete Multiple Policies by Label

Remove Author and Location policies.

# Delete multiple services
kubectl delete validatingadmissionpolicies -l 'service in (author,location)'
validatingadmissionpolicy.admissionregistration.k8s.io "author-security-policy" deleted validatingadmissionpolicy.admissionregistration.k8s.io "location-validation-policy" deleted
Example 5: Delete Non-Production Policies

Remove all anihpj policies except production tier.

# Delete all anihpj policies except production
kubectl delete validatingadmissionpolicies -l app=anihpj,tier!=production
validatingadmissionpolicy.admissionregistration.k8s.io "jobpost-replica-limit" deleted validatingadmissionpolicy.admissionregistration.k8s.io "skills-label-policy" deleted

ValidatingAdmissionPolicyBinding

📋 ValidatingAdmissionPolicyBinding Overview

ValidatingAdmissionPolicyBindingList - Collection for bulk operations
Operations - 7 operations available

API Group: admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1

📖 Official Kubernetes Documentation

Full Description

ValidatingAdmissionPolicyBinding binds a ValidatingAdmissionPolicy to parameterized resources, creating the association between admission validation policies and the resources they govern. In the anihpj Django project's jobpost application, bindings control which Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost deployments are validated by which policies, enabling granular control over admission validation enforcement.

For a given admission request, each binding causes its policy to be evaluated N times, where N is 1 for policies/bindings that don't use parameters, otherwise N is the number of parameters selected by the binding. The binding specifies validationActions (Deny, Warn, Audit) that determine how validation failures are enforced. Bindings use matchResources to intersect with the policy's matchConstraints, selecting which resources are validated. In anihpj, this allows different enforcement levels for different microservices—JobPost deployments might use Deny, Skills might use Warn, and development namespaces might use Audit.

Syntax

# Apply binding from YAML
kubectl apply -f binding.yaml

# Get binding details
kubectl get validatingadmissionpolicybinding <name> -o yaml

# Describe binding
kubectl describe validatingadmissionpolicybinding <name>

# List all bindings
kubectl get validatingadmissionpolicybindings

# Delete binding
kubectl delete validatingadmissionpolicybinding <name>

YAML Structure

apiVersion: admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1
kind: ValidatingAdmissionPolicyBinding
metadata:
  name: jobpost-replica-binding
spec:
  policyName: jobpost-replica-limit    # Required: policy to bind
  validationActions: ["Deny"]          # Required: Deny, Warn, or Audit
  matchResources:
    namespaceSelector:
      matchLabels:
        environment: production
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups: ["apps"]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources: ["deployments"]
  paramRef:                             # Optional: parameter resource
    name: jobpost-params
    namespace: anihpj-system
    parameterNotFoundAction: "Deny"

Parameters Description

  • apiVersion: string (admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1) - API version
  • kind: string (ValidatingAdmissionPolicyBinding) - Resource kind
  • metadata: ObjectMeta - Standard object metadata
  • spec: ValidatingAdmissionPolicyBindingSpec - Binding specification
    • policyName: string (required) - Name of ValidatingAdmissionPolicy to bind
    • validationActions: []string (required) - How validation failures are enforced
      • "Deny" - Validation failure denies the request
      • "Warn" - Validation failure adds HTTP warning header
      • "Audit" - Validation failure logged to audit event
    • matchResources: MatchResources - Resource matching criteria (intersects with policy matchConstraints)
      • namespaceSelector: LabelSelector - Namespace label selector
      • objectSelector: LabelSelector - Object label selector
      • resourceRules: []NamedRuleWithOperations - Resources to match
      • excludeResourceRules: []NamedRuleWithOperations - Resources to exclude
      • matchPolicy: string - "Exact" or "Equivalent" (default)
    • paramRef: ParamRef - Parameter resource reference
      • name: string - Parameter resource name
      • namespace: string - Parameter resource namespace
      • selector: LabelSelector - Match multiple params by label
      • parameterNotFoundAction: string - "Allow" or "Deny" when params not found

Return Value

  • 200 (ValidatingAdmissionPolicyBinding): OK - Successful retrieval
  • 201 (ValidatingAdmissionPolicyBinding): Created - Successfully created
  • 202 (ValidatingAdmissionPolicyBinding): Accepted - Request accepted for processing
  • 401: Unauthorized - Authentication required

5 Practical Django Examples

Example 1: Bind JobPost Replica Policy with Deny Action

Bind JobPost replica limit policy to production deployments with strict enforcement.

apiVersion: admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1
kind: ValidatingAdmissionPolicyBinding
metadata:
  name: jobpost-replica-binding
spec:
  policyName: jobpost-replica-limit
  validationActions: ["Deny"]
  matchResources:
    namespaceSelector:
      matchLabels:
        environment: production
        app: anihpj
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups: ["apps"]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources: ["deployments"]
        resourceNames: ["jobpost-*"]
# Apply JobPost binding
kubectl apply -f jobpost-replica-binding.yaml
validatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io/jobpost-replica-binding created
Example 2: Bind Skills Label Policy with Warn Action

Bind Skills label requirements with warning-only enforcement for gradual rollout.

apiVersion: admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1
kind: ValidatingAdmissionPolicyBinding
metadata:
  name: skills-label-binding
spec:
  policyName: skills-label-policy
  validationActions: ["Warn", "Audit"]
  matchResources:
    namespaceSelector:
      matchLabels:
        service: skills
        stage: migration
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups: ["apps"]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources: ["deployments", "statefulsets"]
# Apply Skills binding
kubectl apply -f skills-label-binding.yaml
validatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io/skills-label-binding created
Example 3: Bind User Resource Policy with Parameters

Bind User resource quota policy with dynamic parameters from ConfigMap.

apiVersion: admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1
kind: ValidatingAdmissionPolicyBinding
metadata:
  name: user-resource-binding
spec:
  policyName: user-resource-policy
  validationActions: ["Deny", "Audit"]
  matchResources:
    namespaceSelector:
      matchExpressions:
        - key: service
          operator: In
          values: ["user", "auth"]
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups: ["apps"]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources: ["deployments"]
  paramRef:
    name: user-resource-limits
    namespace: anihpj-config
    parameterNotFoundAction: "Deny"
# Apply User binding with params
kubectl apply -f user-resource-binding.yaml
validatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io/user-resource-binding created
Example 4: Bind Author Security Policy with Selector

Bind Author security policy using param selector for multi-tenant configuration.

apiVersion: admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1
kind: ValidatingAdmissionPolicyBinding
metadata:
  name: author-security-binding
spec:
  policyName: author-security-policy
  validationActions: ["Deny"]
  matchResources:
    objectSelector:
      matchLabels:
        security-tier: high
    namespaceSelector:
      matchLabels:
        app: anihpj
        service: author
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups: ["apps"]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources: ["deployments", "daemonsets"]
  paramRef:
    selector:
      matchLabels:
        config-type: security
        service: author
    parameterNotFoundAction: "Deny"
# Apply Author binding with selector
kubectl apply -f author-security-binding.yaml
validatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io/author-security-binding created
Example 5: Bind Location Policy with Exclude Rules

Bind Location validation policy excluding system namespaces.

apiVersion: admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1
kind: ValidatingAdmissionPolicyBinding
metadata:
  name: location-validation-binding
spec:
  policyName: location-validation-policy
  validationActions: ["Deny", "Audit"]
  matchResources:
    namespaceSelector:
      matchLabels:
        app: anihpj
        service: location
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups: ["apps"]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources: ["deployments"]
    excludeResourceRules:
      - apiGroups: ["apps"]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources: ["deployments"]
        resourceNames: ["location-canary-*"]
    matchPolicy: "Equivalent"
# Apply Location binding with excludes
kubectl apply -f location-validation-binding.yaml
validatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io/location-validation-binding created

ValidatingAdmissionPolicyBindingList

Full Description

ValidatingAdmissionPolicyBindingList is a collection of ValidatingAdmissionPolicyBinding resources, used for bulk operations and listing all policy bindings in the cluster. In the anihpj Django project, listing bindings helps understand which policies are bound to which resources across all microservices (Skills, User, Author, Location, JobPost).

Syntax
# List all bindings
kubectl get validatingadmissionpolicybindings

# List with custom columns
kubectl get validatingadmissionpolicybindings -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,POLICY:.spec.policyName,ACTIONS:.spec.validationActions

# Export all to JSON
kubectl get validatingadmissionpolicybindings -o json
Parameters
  • apiVersion: string (admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1) - API version
  • kind: string (ValidatingAdmissionPolicyBindingList) - Resource kind
  • metadata: ListMeta - List metadata (resourceVersion, continue, remainingItemCount)
  • items: []ValidatingAdmissionPolicyBinding (required) - Array of ValidatingAdmissionPolicyBinding objects
Return Value

Returns a ValidatingAdmissionPolicyBindingList object containing all matching bindings.

5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: List All anihpj Policy Bindings

View all bindings for anihpj microservices.

# List all anihpj bindings
kubectl get validatingadmissionpolicybindings
NAME POLICYNAME AGE jobpost-replica-binding jobpost-replica-limit 5d skills-label-binding skills-label-policy 5d user-resource-binding user-resource-policy 5d author-security-binding author-security-policy 5d location-validation-binding location-validation-policy 5d
Example 2: List Bindings with Actions

Show binding names with their validation actions.

# List bindings showing validation actions
kubectl get validatingadmissionpolicybindings -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,POLICY:.spec.policyName,ACTIONS:.spec.validationActions
NAME POLICY ACTIONS jobpost-replica-binding jobpost-replica-limit [Deny] skills-label-binding skills-label-policy [Warn Audit] user-resource-binding user-resource-policy [Deny Audit] author-security-binding author-security-policy [Deny] location-validation-binding location-validation-policy [Deny Audit]
Example 3: Extract Binding Names

Get JSON array of binding names.

# Extract binding names
kubectl get validatingadmissionpolicybindings -o json | jq '.items[].metadata.name'
"jobpost-replica-binding" "skills-label-binding" "user-resource-binding" "author-security-binding" "location-validation-binding"
Example 4: Count anihpj Bindings

Count total bindings in cluster.

# Count bindings
kubectl get validatingadmissionpolicybindings --no-headers | wc -l
5
Example 5: List Bindings in Wide Format

Show bindings with additional details.

# Wide format listing
kubectl get validatingadmissionpolicybindings -o wide
NAME POLICYNAME AGE jobpost-replica-binding jobpost-replica-limit 5d skills-label-binding skills-label-policy 5d user-resource-binding user-resource-policy 5d author-security-binding author-security-policy 5d location-validation-binding location-validation-policy 5d

Operations

The following operations are available for managing ValidatingAdmissionPolicyBinding resources in the anihpj jobpost application for binding admission validation policies to resources.

get - Read specified ValidatingAdmissionPolicyBinding
list - List all bindings
create - Create a binding
update - Replace binding
patch - Partially update binding
delete - Delete a binding
deletecollection - Delete multiple bindings

get - read the specified ValidatingAdmissionPolicyBinding

Read details of a specific ValidatingAdmissionPolicyBinding resource.

HTTP Request
GET /apis/admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1/validatingadmissionpolicybindings/{name}
Parameters
  • name (in path): string, required - name of the ValidatingAdmissionPolicyBinding
  • pretty (in query): string - pretty print output
Return Value
  • 200 (ValidatingAdmissionPolicyBinding): OK
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: Get JobPost Binding YAML

Retrieve JobPost replica binding configuration.

# Get JobPost binding
kubectl get validatingadmissionpolicybinding jobpost-replica-binding -o yaml
apiVersion: admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1 kind: ValidatingAdmissionPolicyBinding metadata: name: jobpost-replica-binding creationTimestamp: "2025-12-10T10:00:00Z" spec: policyName: jobpost-replica-limit validationActions: - Deny matchResources: namespaceSelector: matchLabels: app: anihpj environment: production
Example 2: Describe Skills Binding

View Skills label binding details.

# Describe Skills binding
kubectl describe validatingadmissionpolicybinding skills-label-binding
Name: skills-label-binding API Version: admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1 Kind: ValidatingAdmissionPolicyBinding Metadata: Name: skills-label-binding Spec: Policy Name: skills-label-policy Validation Actions: Warn Audit
Example 3: Get User Binding JSON

Retrieve User resource binding as JSON.

# Get User binding JSON
kubectl get validatingadmissionpolicybinding user-resource-binding -o json
{ "apiVersion": "admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1", "kind": "ValidatingAdmissionPolicyBinding", "metadata": { "name": "user-resource-binding" }, "spec": { "policyName": "user-resource-policy", "validationActions": ["Deny", "Audit"], "paramRef": { "name": "user-resource-limits", "namespace": "anihpj-config" } } }
Example 4: Extract Author Binding Actions

Get validation actions from Author binding.

# Extract validation actions
kubectl get validatingadmissionpolicybinding author-security-binding -o jsonpath='{.spec.validationActions}'
["Deny"]
Example 5: Get Location Binding Policy Name

Extract bound policy name from Location binding.

# Get policy name
kubectl get validatingadmissionpolicybinding location-validation-binding -o jsonpath='{.spec.policyName}'
location-validation-policy

list - list or watch objects of kind ValidatingAdmissionPolicyBinding

List all ValidatingAdmissionPolicyBinding resources in the cluster with optional filtering.

HTTP Request
GET /apis/admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1/validatingadmissionpolicybindings
Parameters
  • allowWatchBookmarks (in query): boolean - watch bookmarks support
  • continue (in query): string - continue token for pagination
  • fieldSelector (in query): string - field selector for filtering
  • labelSelector (in query): string - label selector for filtering
  • limit (in query): integer - maximum number of results
  • pretty (in query): string - pretty print output
  • resourceVersion (in query): string - resource version
  • resourceVersionMatch (in query): string - resource version match strategy
  • sendInitialEvents (in query): boolean - send initial events
  • timeoutSeconds (in query): integer - timeout in seconds
  • watch (in query): boolean - watch for changes
Return Value
  • 200 (ValidatingAdmissionPolicyBindingList): OK
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: List All anihpj Bindings

List all policy bindings for anihpj microservices.

# List all bindings
kubectl get validatingadmissionpolicybindings
NAME POLICYNAME AGE jobpost-replica-binding jobpost-replica-limit 5d skills-label-binding skills-label-policy 5d user-resource-binding user-resource-policy 5d author-security-binding author-security-policy 5d location-validation-binding location-validation-policy 5d
Example 2: List with Custom Columns

Show binding names, policies, and actions.

# List with custom columns
kubectl get validatingadmissionpolicybindings -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,POLICY:.spec.policyName,ACTIONS:.spec.validationActions
NAME POLICY ACTIONS jobpost-replica-binding jobpost-replica-limit [Deny] skills-label-binding skills-label-policy [Warn Audit] user-resource-binding user-resource-policy [Deny Audit] author-security-binding author-security-policy [Deny] location-validation-binding location-validation-policy [Deny Audit]
Example 3: Watch Bindings

Monitor binding changes in real-time.

# Watch bindings
kubectl get validatingadmissionpolicybindings --watch
NAME POLICYNAME AGE jobpost-replica-binding jobpost-replica-limit 5d skills-label-binding skills-label-policy 5d
Example 4: Extract Binding Names

Get JSON array of all binding names.

# Extract names
kubectl get validatingadmissionpolicybindings -o json | jq '.items[].metadata.name'
"jobpost-replica-binding" "skills-label-binding" "user-resource-binding" "author-security-binding" "location-validation-binding"
Example 5: List Bindings with Deny Action

Filter bindings that use Deny action.

# List Deny-action bindings
kubectl get validatingadmissionpolicybindings -o json | jq '.items[] | select(.spec.validationActions[] == "Deny") | .metadata.name'
"jobpost-replica-binding" "user-resource-binding" "author-security-binding" "location-validation-binding"

create - create a ValidatingAdmissionPolicyBinding

Create a new ValidatingAdmissionPolicyBinding resource.

HTTP Request
POST /apis/admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1/validatingadmissionpolicybindings
Parameters
  • body: ValidatingAdmissionPolicyBinding, required - binding to create
  • dryRun (in query): string - simulate the operation without persisting
  • fieldManager (in query): string - name of the manager creating this resource
  • fieldValidation (in query): string - validation level (Strict, Warn, Ignore)
  • pretty (in query): string - pretty print output
Return Value
  • 200 (ValidatingAdmissionPolicyBinding): OK
  • 201 (ValidatingAdmissionPolicyBinding): Created
  • 202 (ValidatingAdmissionPolicyBinding): Accepted
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: Create JobPost Replica Binding

Create binding for JobPost replica limits.

# Create JobPost binding
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1
kind: ValidatingAdmissionPolicyBinding
metadata:
  name: jobpost-replica-binding
spec:
  policyName: jobpost-replica-limit
  validationActions: ["Deny"]
  matchResources:
    namespaceSelector:
      matchLabels:
        app: anihpj
        environment: production
EOF
validatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io/jobpost-replica-binding created
Example 2: Create Skills Label Binding

Create Skills label policy binding with Warn action.

# Create Skills binding
kubectl create -f skills-label-binding.yaml
validatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io/skills-label-binding created
Example 3: Create User Binding with Dry Run

Test User resource binding creation without applying.

# Dry-run create User binding
kubectl apply -f user-resource-binding.yaml --dry-run=client
validatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io/user-resource-binding created (dry run)
Example 4: Create Author Security Binding

Create Author security policy binding.

# Create Author binding
kubectl apply -f author-security-binding.yaml
validatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io/author-security-binding created
Example 5: Create Location Validation Binding

Create Location policy binding with excludes.

# Create Location binding
kubectl apply -f location-validation-binding.yaml
validatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io/location-validation-binding created

update - replace the specified ValidatingAdmissionPolicyBinding

Replace an existing ValidatingAdmissionPolicyBinding resource.

HTTP Request
PUT /apis/admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1/validatingadmissionpolicybindings/{name}
Parameters
  • name (in path): string, required - name of the ValidatingAdmissionPolicyBinding
  • body: ValidatingAdmissionPolicyBinding, required - updated binding object
  • dryRun (in query): string - simulate the operation without persisting
  • fieldManager (in query): string - name of the manager updating this resource
  • fieldValidation (in query): string - validation level (Strict, Warn, Ignore)
  • pretty (in query): string - pretty print output
Return Value
  • 200 (ValidatingAdmissionPolicyBinding): OK
  • 201 (ValidatingAdmissionPolicyBinding): Created
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: Update JobPost Binding Actions

Change JobPost binding from Deny to Warn+Audit.

# Edit and update JobPost binding
kubectl edit validatingadmissionpolicybinding jobpost-replica-binding
# Change validationActions: ["Deny"] to ["Warn", "Audit"]
validatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io/jobpost-replica-binding edited
Example 2: Update Skills Binding Namespace Selector

Modify Skills binding namespace selector.

# Update Skills binding
kubectl apply -f skills-label-binding-updated.yaml
validatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io/skills-label-binding configured
Example 3: Update User Binding ParamRef

Change User binding parameter reference.

# Replace User binding
kubectl replace -f user-resource-binding-v2.yaml
validatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io/user-resource-binding replaced
Example 4: Update Author Binding Dry Run

Test Author binding update without applying.

# Dry-run update
kubectl apply -f author-security-binding-updated.yaml --dry-run=server
validatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io/author-security-binding configured (server dry run)
Example 5: Update Location Binding Match Resources

Update Location binding resource rules.

# Update Location binding
kubectl apply -f location-validation-binding-updated.yaml
validatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io/location-validation-binding configured

patch - partially update the specified ValidatingAdmissionPolicyBinding

Partially update a ValidatingAdmissionPolicyBinding using JSON patch, merge patch, or strategic merge patch.

HTTP Request
PATCH /apis/admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1/validatingadmissionpolicybindings/{name}
Parameters
  • name (in path): string, required - name of the ValidatingAdmissionPolicyBinding
  • body: Patch, required - patch to apply
  • dryRun (in query): string - simulate the operation without persisting
  • fieldManager (in query): string - name of the manager patching this resource
  • fieldValidation (in query): string - validation level (Strict, Warn, Ignore)
  • force (in query): boolean - force apply the patch
  • pretty (in query): string - pretty print output
Return Value
  • 200 (ValidatingAdmissionPolicyBinding): OK
  • 201 (ValidatingAdmissionPolicyBinding): Created
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: Patch JobPost Binding Actions

Add Audit action to JobPost binding.

# Patch JobPost binding validation actions
kubectl patch validatingadmissionpolicybinding jobpost-replica-binding --type='merge' -p='{"spec":{"validationActions":["Deny","Audit"]}}'
validatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io/jobpost-replica-binding patched
Example 2: Patch Skills Binding Selector

Update Skills binding namespace selector.

# Patch Skills binding
kubectl patch validatingadmissionpolicybinding skills-label-binding --type='json' -p='[{"op": "replace", "path": "/spec/matchResources/namespaceSelector/matchLabels/stage", "value": "production"}]'
validatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io/skills-label-binding patched
Example 3: Patch User Binding ParamRef

Change User binding parameter name.

# Patch User binding
kubectl patch validatingadmissionpolicybinding user-resource-binding --type='merge' -p='{"spec":{"paramRef":{"name":"user-resource-limits-v2"}}}'
validatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io/user-resource-binding patched
Example 4: Patch Author Binding Add Label

Add label to Author binding metadata.

# Patch Author binding labels
kubectl patch validatingadmissionpolicybinding author-security-binding --type='merge' -p='{"metadata":{"labels":{"tier":"critical"}}}'
validatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io/author-security-binding patched
Example 5: Patch Location Binding Match Policy

Change Location binding match policy.

# Patch Location binding
kubectl patch validatingadmissionpolicybinding location-validation-binding --type='merge' -p='{"spec":{"matchResources":{"matchPolicy":"Exact"}}}'
validatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io/location-validation-binding patched

delete - delete a ValidatingAdmissionPolicyBinding

Delete a specific ValidatingAdmissionPolicyBinding resource.

HTTP Request
DELETE /apis/admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1/validatingadmissionpolicybindings/{name}
Parameters
  • name (in path): string, required - name of the ValidatingAdmissionPolicyBinding
  • body: DeleteOptions - delete options
  • dryRun (in query): string - simulate the operation without persisting
  • gracePeriodSeconds (in query): integer - grace period before deletion
  • ignoreStoreReadErrorWithClusterBreakingPotential (in query): boolean - ignore store read errors
  • pretty (in query): string - pretty print output
  • propagationPolicy (in query): string - propagation policy (Orphan, Background, Foreground)
Return Value
  • 200 (Status): OK
  • 202 (Status): Accepted
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: Delete JobPost Replica Binding

Remove JobPost replica binding.

# Delete JobPost binding
kubectl delete validatingadmissionpolicybinding jobpost-replica-binding
validatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io "jobpost-replica-binding" deleted
Example 2: Delete Skills Binding with Confirmation

Delete Skills label binding with wait.

# Delete Skills binding
kubectl delete validatingadmissionpolicybinding skills-label-binding --wait=true
validatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io "skills-label-binding" deleted
Example 3: Delete User Binding with Dry Run

Test User resource binding deletion.

# Dry-run delete User binding
kubectl delete validatingadmissionpolicybinding user-resource-binding --dry-run=client
validatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io "user-resource-binding" deleted (dry run)
Example 4: Delete Author Binding Immediately

Force immediate deletion of Author security binding.

# Force delete Author binding
kubectl delete validatingadmissionpolicybinding author-security-binding --grace-period=0 --force
warning: Immediate deletion does not wait for confirmation that the running resource has been terminated. The resource may continue to run on the cluster indefinitely. validatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io "author-security-binding" force deleted
Example 5: Delete Location Binding from YAML

Remove Location validation binding using file.

# Delete Location binding from file
kubectl delete -f location-validation-binding.yaml
validatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io "location-validation-binding" deleted

deletecollection - delete collection of ValidatingAdmissionPolicyBinding

Delete multiple ValidatingAdmissionPolicyBindings matching specified criteria.

HTTP Request
DELETE /apis/admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1/validatingadmissionpolicybindings
Parameters
  • body: DeleteOptions - delete options
  • continue (in query): string - continue token for pagination
  • dryRun (in query): string - simulate the operation without persisting
  • fieldSelector (in query): string - field selector to filter resources
  • gracePeriodSeconds (in query): integer - grace period before deletion
  • ignoreStoreReadErrorWithClusterBreakingPotential (in query): boolean - ignore store read errors
  • labelSelector (in query): string - label selector to filter resources
  • limit (in query): integer - maximum number of resources to delete
  • pretty (in query): string - pretty print output
  • propagationPolicy (in query): string - propagation policy (Orphan, Background, Foreground)
  • resourceVersion (in query): string - resource version
  • resourceVersionMatch (in query): string - resource version match strategy
  • sendInitialEvents (in query): boolean - send initial events
  • timeoutSeconds (in query): integer - timeout in seconds
Return Value
  • 200 (Status): OK
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: Delete All anihpj Bindings

Remove all bindings with anihpj label.

# Delete all anihpj bindings (if labeled)
kubectl delete validatingadmissionpolicybindings --all
validatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io "jobpost-replica-binding" deleted validatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io "skills-label-binding" deleted validatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io "user-resource-binding" deleted validatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io "author-security-binding" deleted validatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io "location-validation-binding" deleted
Example 2: Delete Bindings by Label

Remove bindings matching specific label.

# Delete bindings by label (if labeled with tier=standard)
kubectl delete validatingadmissionpolicybindings -l tier=standard
validatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io "skills-label-binding" deleted
Example 3: Delete Bindings with Dry Run

Test deletion of multiple bindings.

# Dry-run delete all bindings
kubectl delete validatingadmissionpolicybindings --all --dry-run=client
validatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io "jobpost-replica-binding" deleted (dry run) validatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io "skills-label-binding" deleted (dry run) validatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io "user-resource-binding" deleted (dry run) validatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io "author-security-binding" deleted (dry run) validatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io "location-validation-binding" deleted (dry run)
Example 4: Delete Multiple Bindings by Name Pattern

Remove specific bindings using shell patterns.

# Delete multiple bindings
kubectl delete validatingadmissionpolicybindings jobpost-replica-binding skills-label-binding
validatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io "jobpost-replica-binding" deleted validatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io "skills-label-binding" deleted
Example 5: Delete Non-Critical Bindings

Remove all bindings except critical ones (by label).

# Delete non-critical bindings (if labeled with tier!=critical)
kubectl delete validatingadmissionpolicybindings -l tier!=critical
validatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io "jobpost-replica-binding" deleted validatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io "skills-label-binding" deleted

MutatingAdmissionPolicy v1beta1

MutatingAdmissionPolicy Overview

MutatingAdmissionPolicyBinding - Binding policies to resources
MutatingAdmissionPolicyList - Collection of policies
Operations - API operations for management

Official Kubernetes Documentation: MutatingAdmissionPolicy v1beta1

API Version: admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1beta1

Full Description

MutatingAdmissionPolicy describes an admission mutation policy that mutates objects coming into the admission chain. Unlike ValidatingAdmissionPolicy which validates objects, MutatingAdmissionPolicy actively modifies (mutates) objects before they are persisted. Mutations are defined using CEL (Common Expression Language) expressions that can apply configurations or JSON patches to modify object fields. This powerful mechanism allows cluster administrators to automatically inject labels, annotations, environment variables, sidecars, or modify any part of resource specifications based on policy rules.

In the anihpj Django microservices project, MutatingAdmissionPolicy enables automatic configuration of JobPost, Skills, User, Author, and Location deployments. For example, a policy can automatically inject monitoring sidecars, set resource limits, add security contexts, or enforce naming conventions across all deployments. The policy evaluates matchConstraints to determine which resources to mutate, uses paramKind for parameterized mutations, and supports reinvocationPolicy to handle cascading mutations. Each policy defines mutations using either ApplyConfiguration (structured merge) or JSONPatch operations, giving precise control over how objects are modified during admission.

Syntax

# Apply a mutation policy
kubectl apply -f mutatingadmissionpolicy.yaml

# Get a mutation policy
kubectl get mutatingadmissionpolicy <policy-name>

# Describe a mutation policy  
kubectl describe mutatingadmissionpolicy <policy-name>

# List all mutation policies
kubectl get mutatingadmissionpolicies

# Delete a mutation policy
kubectl delete mutatingadmissionpolicy <policy-name>

YAML Structure

apiVersion: admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: MutatingAdmissionPolicy
metadata:
  name: jobpost-sidecar-injector
spec:
  failurePolicy: Fail
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
    - apiGroups: ["apps"]
      apiVersions: ["v1"]
      operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
      resources: ["deployments"]
  matchConditions:
  - name: has-monitoring-label
    expression: "object.metadata.labels.exists(l, l == 'monitoring') && object.metadata.labels['monitoring'] == 'enabled'"
  mutations:
  - patchType: ApplyConfiguration
    applyConfiguration:
      expression: |
        Object{
          spec: Object.spec{
            template: Object.spec.template{
              spec: Object.spec.template.spec{
                containers: object.spec.template.spec.containers + [
                  Object.spec.template.spec.containers{
                    name: "metrics-exporter",
                    image: "prom/statsd-exporter:latest",
                    ports: [{containerPort: 9102}]
                  }
                ]
              }
            }
          }
        }
  reinvocationPolicy: IfNeeded

Parameters Description

  • apiVersion: string - API version (admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1beta1)
  • kind: string - Resource type (MutatingAdmissionPolicy)
  • metadata: ObjectMeta - Standard object metadata
    • name: string (required) - Policy name
    • labels: map[string]string - Labels for organization
  • spec: MutatingAdmissionPolicySpec - Policy specification
    • failurePolicy: string - How to handle failures (Fail or Ignore, defaults to Fail)
    • matchConstraints: MatchResources (required) - Which resources to mutate
      • resourceRules: []NamedRuleWithOperations - Resources to match
      • excludeResourceRules: []NamedRuleWithOperations - Resources to exclude
      • namespaceSelector: LabelSelector - Namespace selection
      • objectSelector: LabelSelector - Object label selection
      • matchPolicy: string - Exact or Equivalent matching
    • matchConditions: []MatchCondition - Additional CEL-based conditions
      • name: string (required) - Condition identifier
      • expression: string (required) - CEL expression (must return bool)
    • mutations: []Mutation (required) - Mutation operations to perform
      • patchType: string (required) - ApplyConfiguration or JSONPatch
      • applyConfiguration: ApplyConfiguration - Structured merge configuration
        • expression: string (required) - CEL expression returning apply configuration
      • jsonPatch: JSONPatch - JSON Patch operations
        • expression: string (required) - CEL expression returning JSON patch array
    • paramKind: ParamKind - Parameter resource type
      • apiVersion: string (required) - Parameter API version
      • kind: string (required) - Parameter kind
    • reinvocationPolicy: string - Never or IfNeeded (required)
    • variables: []Variable - Reusable CEL variables
      • name: string (required) - Variable name
      • expression: string (required) - CEL expression for value

Return Value

  • 200: OK - Policy exists and details returned
  • 201: Created - Policy created successfully
  • 202: Accepted - Policy update accepted
  • 401: Unauthorized - Authentication required

5 Practical Django Examples

Example 1: JobPost Deployment Sidecar Injection

Automatically inject metrics exporter sidecar into JobPost deployments.

# Create sidecar injection policy
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: MutatingAdmissionPolicy
metadata:
  name: jobpost-sidecar-injector
spec:
  failurePolicy: Fail
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
    - apiGroups: ["apps"]
      apiVersions: ["v1"]
      operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
      resources: ["deployments"]
    namespaceSelector:
      matchLabels:
        app: anihpj
        service: jobpost
  mutations:
  - patchType: ApplyConfiguration
    applyConfiguration:
      expression: |
        Object{
          spec: Object.spec{
            template: Object.spec.template{
              spec: Object.spec.template.spec{
                containers: object.spec.template.spec.containers + [
                  Object.spec.template.spec.containers{
                    name: "metrics-exporter",
                    image: "prom/statsd-exporter:v0.24.0",
                    ports: [{containerPort: 9102, protocol: "TCP"}]
                  }
                ]
              }
            }
          }
        }
  reinvocationPolicy: IfNeeded
EOF
mutatingadmissionpolicy.admissionregistration.k8s.io/jobpost-sidecar-injector created
Example 2: Skills Deployment Resource Limits

Automatically set resource limits on Skills deployments using JSON Patch.

# Create resource limits policy
kubectl apply -f skills-resource-limiter.yaml
mutatingadmissionpolicy.admissionregistration.k8s.io/skills-resource-limiter created
Example 3: User Deployment Security Context

Inject security context into User deployments for non-root execution.

# Create security context policy
kubectl apply -f user-security-context.yaml
mutatingadmissionpolicy.admissionregistration.k8s.io/user-security-context created
Example 4: Author Deployment Label Injection

Automatically add standard labels to Author deployments.

# Create label injection policy
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: MutatingAdmissionPolicy
metadata:
  name: author-label-injector
spec:
  failurePolicy: Ignore
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
    - apiGroups: ["apps"]
      apiVersions: ["v1"]
      operations: ["CREATE"]
      resources: ["deployments"]
  mutations:
  - patchType: JSONPatch
    jsonPatch:
      expression: |
        [
          JSONPatch{
            op: "add",
            path: "/metadata/labels/managed-by",
            value: "anihpj-policy"
          },
          JSONPatch{
            op: "add",
            path: "/metadata/labels/service",
            value: "author"
          }
        ]
  reinvocationPolicy: Never
EOF
mutatingadmissionpolicy.admissionregistration.k8s.io/author-label-injector created
Example 5: Location Deployment Volume Mount Injection

Inject configuration volume mounts into Location deployments.

# Create volume mount policy
kubectl apply -f location-volume-mounter.yaml
