mutatingadmissionpolicy.admissionregistration.k8s.io/location-volume-mounter created

MutatingAdmissionPolicyBinding

Full Description: MutatingAdmissionPolicyBinding binds a MutatingAdmissionPolicy to parameterized resources. The binding specifies which policy to use and optionally provides parameters for policy evaluation. For each admission request, the binding causes its policy to be evaluated N times, where N is 1 for non-parameterized policies or the number of selected parameters. Each evaluation is constrained by a CEL runtime cost budget. In the anihpj project, bindings connect mutation policies to specific deployments, enabling targeted automatic modifications of JobPost, Skills, User, Author, and Location resources based on namespace selectors, object selectors, and parameter references.

Syntax

# Apply a policy binding
kubectl apply -f mutatingadmissionpolicybinding.yaml

# Get a binding
kubectl get mutatingadmissionpolicybindings <binding-name>

# List all bindings
kubectl get mutatingadmissionpolicybindings

Parameters

  • apiVersion: string - admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1alpha1
  • kind: string - MutatingAdmissionPolicyBinding
  • metadata: ObjectMeta - Standard metadata
  • spec: MutatingAdmissionPolicyBindingSpec
    • policyName: string (required) - Name of policy to bind
    • matchResources: MatchResources - Limits resource matching
    • paramRef: ParamRef - Parameter resource reference

Return Value

Returns a MutatingAdmissionPolicyBinding object with binding configuration.

5 Practical Django Examples

Example 1: JobPost Sidecar Binding

Bind sidecar injection policy to production JobPost deployments.

# Create JobPost binding
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1alpha1
kind: MutatingAdmissionPolicyBinding
metadata:
  name: jobpost-sidecar-binding
spec:
  policyName: jobpost-sidecar-injector
  matchResources:
    namespaceSelector:
      matchLabels:
        environment: production
        app: anihpj
EOF
mutatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io/jobpost-sidecar-binding created
Example 2: Skills Resource Limit Binding

Bind resource limiter policy to Skills deployments.

# Create Skills binding
kubectl apply -f skills-resource-binding.yaml
mutatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io/skills-resource-binding created
Example 3: User Security Binding with Parameters

Bind security policy with ConfigMap parameters.

# Create User binding with params
kubectl apply -f user-security-binding.yaml
mutatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io/user-security-binding created
Example 4: Author Label Binding

Bind label injection policy to Author deployments.

# Create Author binding
kubectl apply -f author-label-binding.yaml
mutatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io/author-label-binding created
Example 5: Location Volume Binding

Bind volume mount policy to Location deployments.

# Create Location binding
kubectl apply -f location-volume-binding.yaml
mutatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io/location-volume-binding created

MutatingAdmissionPolicyList

Full Description: MutatingAdmissionPolicyList is a collection of MutatingAdmissionPolicy resources. It provides a convenient way to retrieve, list, and iterate through multiple mutation policies in the cluster. The list supports filtering, pagination, and watch operations for monitoring policy changes across the anihpj microservices environment.

Syntax

# List all policies
kubectl get mutatingadmissionpolicies

# List with custom columns
kubectl get mutatingadmissionpolicies -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,FAILURE-POLICY:.spec.failurePolicy

# Get as JSON
kubectl get mutatingadmissionpolicies -o json

Parameters

  • apiVersion: string - admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1beta1
  • kind: string - MutatingAdmissionPolicyList
  • metadata: ListMeta - List metadata (resourceVersion, continue, remainingItemCount)
  • items: []MutatingAdmissionPolicy (required) - Array of policies

Return Value

Returns a MutatingAdmissionPolicyList object containing all mutation policies.

5 Practical Django Examples

Example 1: List All anihpj Policies

Display all mutation policies (5 for anihpj services).

# List all mutation policies
kubectl get mutatingadmissionpolicies
NAME FAILURE-POLICY AGE jobpost-sidecar-injector Fail 5d skills-resource-limiter Fail 5d user-security-context Fail 5d author-label-injector Ignore 5d location-volume-mounter Fail 5d
Example 2: List with Failure Policy

Show policies with their failure policies.

# List with custom columns
kubectl get mutatingadmissionpolicies -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,FAILURE:.spec.failurePolicy,REINVOKE:.spec.reinvocationPolicy
NAME FAILURE REINVOKE jobpost-sidecar-injector Fail IfNeeded skills-resource-limiter Fail Never user-security-context Fail IfNeeded author-label-injector Ignore Never location-volume-mounter Fail IfNeeded
Example 3: Extract Policy Names

Get JSON array of policy names.

# Extract names using jq
kubectl get mutatingadmissionpolicies -o json | jq '.items[].metadata.name'
"jobpost-sidecar-injector" "skills-resource-limiter" "user-security-context" "author-label-injector" "location-volume-mounter"
Example 4: Count Total Policies

Count mutation policies in cluster.

# Count policies
kubectl get mutatingadmissionpolicies --no-headers | wc -l
5
Example 5: List in Wide Format

Display policies with additional details.

# Wide format listing
kubectl get mutatingadmissionpolicies -o wide
NAME FAILURE-POLICY REINVOCATION-POLICY AGE jobpost-sidecar-injector Fail IfNeeded 5d skills-resource-limiter Fail Never 5d user-security-context Fail IfNeeded 5d author-label-injector Ignore Never 5d location-volume-mounter Fail IfNeeded 5d

Operations

The following operations are available for managing MutatingAdmissionPolicy resources in the anihpj jobpost application for automatic mutation of admission requests.

get - Read specified policy
list - List all policies
create - Create a policy
update - Replace policy
patch - Partially update policy
delete - Delete a policy
deletecollection - Delete multiple policies

get - read the specified MutatingAdmissionPolicy

Read details of a specific MutatingAdmissionPolicy resource.

HTTP Request
GET /apis/admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1beta1/mutatingadmissionpolicies/{name}
Parameters
  • name (in path): string, required - name of the MutatingAdmissionPolicy
  • pretty (in query): string - pretty print output
Return Value
  • 200 (MutatingAdmissionPolicy): OK
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: Get JobPost Sidecar Policy YAML

Retrieve sidecar injection policy configuration.

# Get policy YAML
kubectl get mutatingadmissionpolicy jobpost-sidecar-injector -o yaml
apiVersion: admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1beta1 kind: MutatingAdmissionPolicy metadata: name: jobpost-sidecar-injector creationTimestamp: "2025-12-10T10:00:00Z" spec: failurePolicy: Fail matchConstraints: resourceRules: - apiGroups: ["apps"] apiVersions: ["v1"] operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"] resources: ["deployments"] mutations: - patchType: ApplyConfiguration reinvocationPolicy: IfNeeded
Example 2: Describe Skills Resource Policy

View Skills resource limiter details.

# Describe policy
kubectl describe mutatingadmissionpolicy skills-resource-limiter
Name: skills-resource-limiter API Version: admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1beta1 Kind: MutatingAdmissionPolicy Metadata: Name: skills-resource-limiter Spec: Failure Policy: Fail Reinvocation Policy: Never
Example 3: Get User Security Policy JSON

Retrieve User security context policy as JSON.

# Get policy JSON
kubectl get mutatingadmissionpolicy user-security-context -o json
{ "apiVersion": "admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1beta1", "kind": "MutatingAdmissionPolicy", "metadata": { "name": "user-security-context" }, "spec": { "failurePolicy": "Fail", "reinvocationPolicy": "IfNeeded" } }
Example 4: Extract Author Policy Failure Policy

Get failure policy from Author label injector.

# Extract failure policy
kubectl get mutatingadmissionpolicy author-label-injector -o jsonpath='{.spec.failurePolicy}'
Ignore
Example 5: Get Location Policy Reinvocation

Extract reinvocation policy from Location volume policy.

# Get reinvocation policy
kubectl get mutatingadmissionpolicy location-volume-mounter -o jsonpath='{.spec.reinvocationPolicy}'
IfNeeded

list - list or watch objects of kind MutatingAdmissionPolicy

List all MutatingAdmissionPolicy resources in the cluster with optional filtering.

HTTP Request
GET /apis/admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1beta1/mutatingadmissionpolicies
Parameters
  • allowWatchBookmarks (in query): boolean - watch bookmarks support
  • continue (in query): string - continue token for pagination
  • fieldSelector (in query): string - field selector for filtering
  • labelSelector (in query): string - label selector for filtering
  • limit (in query): integer - maximum number of results
  • pretty (in query): string - pretty print output
  • resourceVersion (in query): string - resource version
  • resourceVersionMatch (in query): string - resource version match strategy
  • sendInitialEvents (in query): boolean - send initial events
  • timeoutSeconds (in query): integer - timeout in seconds
  • watch (in query): boolean - watch for changes
Return Value
  • 200 (MutatingAdmissionPolicyList): OK
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: List All anihpj Policies

List all mutation policies for anihpj services.

# List all policies
kubectl get mutatingadmissionpolicies
NAME FAILURE-POLICY AGE jobpost-sidecar-injector Fail 5d skills-resource-limiter Fail 5d user-security-context Fail 5d author-label-injector Ignore 5d location-volume-mounter Fail 5d
Example 2: List with Custom Columns

Show policy names, failure policies, and reinvocation policies.

# List with custom columns
kubectl get mutatingadmissionpolicies -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,FAILURE:.spec.failurePolicy,REINVOKE:.spec.reinvocationPolicy
NAME FAILURE REINVOKE jobpost-sidecar-injector Fail IfNeeded skills-resource-limiter Fail Never user-security-context Fail IfNeeded author-label-injector Ignore Never location-volume-mounter Fail IfNeeded
Example 3: Watch Policies

Monitor policy changes in real-time.

# Watch policies
kubectl get mutatingadmissionpolicies --watch
NAME FAILURE-POLICY AGE jobpost-sidecar-injector Fail 5d skills-resource-limiter Fail 5d
Example 4: Extract Policy Names

Get JSON array of all policy names.

# Extract names
kubectl get mutatingadmissionpolicies -o json | jq '.items[].metadata.name'
"jobpost-sidecar-injector" "skills-resource-limiter" "user-security-context" "author-label-injector" "location-volume-mounter"
Example 5: List Policies with Fail Policy

Filter policies using Fail as failure policy.

# List Fail policies
kubectl get mutatingadmissionpolicies -o json | jq '.items[] | select(.spec.failurePolicy == "Fail") | .metadata.name'
"jobpost-sidecar-injector" "skills-resource-limiter" "user-security-context" "location-volume-mounter"

create - create a MutatingAdmissionPolicy

Create a new MutatingAdmissionPolicy resource.

HTTP Request
POST /apis/admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1beta1/mutatingadmissionpolicies
Parameters
  • body: MutatingAdmissionPolicy, required - policy to create
  • dryRun (in query): string - simulate the operation without persisting
  • fieldManager (in query): string - name of the manager creating this resource
  • fieldValidation (in query): string - validation level (Strict, Warn, Ignore)
  • pretty (in query): string - pretty print output
Return Value
  • 200 (MutatingAdmissionPolicy): OK
  • 201 (MutatingAdmissionPolicy): Created
  • 202 (MutatingAdmissionPolicy): Accepted
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: Create JobPost Sidecar Policy

Create sidecar injection policy for JobPost deployments.

# Create sidecar policy
kubectl apply -f jobpost-sidecar-injector.yaml
mutatingadmissionpolicy.admissionregistration.k8s.io/jobpost-sidecar-injector created
Example 2: Create Skills Resource Limiter

Create policy to set resource limits on Skills deployments.

# Create resource limiter
kubectl create -f skills-resource-limiter.yaml
mutatingadmissionpolicy.admissionregistration.k8s.io/skills-resource-limiter created
Example 3: Create User Security Policy with Dry Run

Test User security context policy creation.

# Dry-run create
kubectl apply -f user-security-context.yaml --dry-run=client
mutatingadmissionpolicy.admissionregistration.k8s.io/user-security-context created (dry run)
Example 4: Create Author Label Injector

Create label injection policy for Author deployments.

# Create label injector
kubectl apply -f author-label-injector.yaml
mutatingadmissionpolicy.admissionregistration.k8s.io/author-label-injector created
Example 5: Create Location Volume Mounter

Create volume mount injection policy for Location deployments.

# Create volume mounter
kubectl apply -f location-volume-mounter.yaml
mutatingadmissionpolicy.admissionregistration.k8s.io/location-volume-mounter created

update - replace the specified MutatingAdmissionPolicy

Replace an existing MutatingAdmissionPolicy resource.

HTTP Request
PUT /apis/admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1beta1/mutatingadmissionpolicies/{name}
Parameters
  • name (in path): string, required - name of the MutatingAdmissionPolicy
  • body: MutatingAdmissionPolicy, required - updated policy object
  • dryRun (in query): string - simulate the operation without persisting
  • fieldManager (in query): string - name of the manager updating this resource
  • fieldValidation (in query): string - validation level (Strict, Warn, Ignore)
  • pretty (in query): string - pretty print output
Return Value
  • 200 (MutatingAdmissionPolicy): OK
  • 201 (MutatingAdmissionPolicy): Created
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: Update JobPost Policy Failure Policy

Change JobPost policy from Fail to Ignore.

# Edit and update policy
kubectl edit mutatingadmissionpolicy jobpost-sidecar-injector
# Change failurePolicy: "Fail" to "Ignore"
mutatingadmissionpolicy.admissionregistration.k8s.io/jobpost-sidecar-injector edited
Example 2: Update Skills Policy Mutations

Modify Skills resource limiter mutations.

# Update policy
kubectl apply -f skills-resource-limiter-updated.yaml
mutatingadmissionpolicy.admissionregistration.k8s.io/skills-resource-limiter configured
Example 3: Update User Policy Match Constraints

Change User security policy match constraints.

# Replace policy
kubectl replace -f user-security-context-v2.yaml
mutatingadmissionpolicy.admissionregistration.k8s.io/user-security-context replaced
Example 4: Update Author Policy Dry Run

Test Author policy update without applying.

# Dry-run update
kubectl apply -f author-label-injector-updated.yaml --dry-run=server
mutatingadmissionpolicy.admissionregistration.k8s.io/author-label-injector configured (server dry run)
Example 5: Update Location Policy Reinvocation

Change Location policy reinvocation policy.

# Update policy
kubectl apply -f location-volume-mounter-updated.yaml
mutatingadmissionpolicy.admissionregistration.k8s.io/location-volume-mounter configured

patch - partially update the specified MutatingAdmissionPolicy

Partially update a MutatingAdmissionPolicy using JSON patch, merge patch, or strategic merge patch.

HTTP Request
PATCH /apis/admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1beta1/mutatingadmissionpolicies/{name}
Parameters
  • name (in path): string, required - name of the MutatingAdmissionPolicy
  • body: Patch, required - patch to apply
  • dryRun (in query): string - simulate the operation without persisting
  • fieldManager (in query): string - name of the manager patching this resource
  • fieldValidation (in query): string - validation level (Strict, Warn, Ignore)
  • force (in query): boolean - force apply the patch
  • pretty (in query): string - pretty print output
Return Value
  • 200 (MutatingAdmissionPolicy): OK
  • 201 (MutatingAdmissionPolicy): Created
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: Patch JobPost Policy Failure Policy

Change failure policy to Ignore.

# Patch failure policy
kubectl patch mutatingadmissionpolicy jobpost-sidecar-injector --type='merge' -p='{"spec":{"failurePolicy":"Ignore"}}'
mutatingadmissionpolicy.admissionregistration.k8s.io/jobpost-sidecar-injector patched
Example 2: Patch Skills Policy Reinvocation

Change reinvocation policy using JSON patch.

# Patch reinvocation policy
kubectl patch mutatingadmissionpolicy skills-resource-limiter --type='json' -p='[{"op": "replace", "path": "/spec/reinvocationPolicy", "value": "IfNeeded"}]'
mutatingadmissionpolicy.admissionregistration.k8s.io/skills-resource-limiter patched
Example 3: Patch User Policy Match Conditions

Add match condition to User policy.

# Patch match conditions
kubectl patch mutatingadmissionpolicy user-security-context --type='merge' -p='{"spec":{"matchConditions":[{"name":"prod-only","expression":"object.metadata.namespace == \"production\""}]}}'
mutatingadmissionpolicy.admissionregistration.k8s.io/user-security-context patched
Example 4: Patch Author Policy Add Label

Add label to Author policy metadata.

# Patch policy labels
kubectl patch mutatingadmissionpolicy author-label-injector --type='merge' -p='{"metadata":{"labels":{"tier":"standard"}}}'
mutatingadmissionpolicy.admissionregistration.k8s.io/author-label-injector patched
Example 5: Patch Location Policy Variables

Add CEL variable to Location policy.

# Patch variables
kubectl patch mutatingadmissionpolicy location-volume-mounter --type='merge' -p='{"spec":{"variables":[{"name":"volumeName","expression":"\"config-volume\""}]}}'
mutatingadmissionpolicy.admissionregistration.k8s.io/location-volume-mounter patched

delete - delete a MutatingAdmissionPolicy

Delete a specific MutatingAdmissionPolicy resource.

HTTP Request
DELETE /apis/admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1beta1/mutatingadmissionpolicies/{name}
Parameters
  • name (in path): string, required - name of the MutatingAdmissionPolicy
  • body: DeleteOptions - delete options
  • dryRun (in query): string - simulate the operation without persisting
  • gracePeriodSeconds (in query): integer - grace period before deletion
  • ignoreStoreReadErrorWithClusterBreakingPotential (in query): boolean - ignore store read errors
  • pretty (in query): string - pretty print output
  • propagationPolicy (in query): string - propagation policy (Orphan, Background, Foreground)
Return Value
  • 200 (Status): OK
  • 202 (Status): Accepted
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: Delete JobPost Sidecar Policy

Remove JobPost sidecar injection policy.

# Delete policy
kubectl delete mutatingadmissionpolicy jobpost-sidecar-injector
mutatingadmissionpolicy.admissionregistration.k8s.io "jobpost-sidecar-injector" deleted
Example 2: Delete Skills Policy with Confirmation

Delete Skills resource limiter with wait.

# Delete with wait
kubectl delete mutatingadmissionpolicy skills-resource-limiter --wait=true
mutatingadmissionpolicy.admissionregistration.k8s.io "skills-resource-limiter" deleted
Example 3: Delete User Policy with Dry Run

Test User security policy deletion.

# Dry-run delete
kubectl delete mutatingadmissionpolicy user-security-context --dry-run=client
mutatingadmissionpolicy.admissionregistration.k8s.io "user-security-context" deleted (dry run)
Example 4: Delete Author Policy Immediately

Force immediate deletion of Author label injector.

# Force delete
kubectl delete mutatingadmissionpolicy author-label-injector --grace-period=0 --force
warning: Immediate deletion does not wait for confirmation that the running resource has been terminated. mutatingadmissionpolicy.admissionregistration.k8s.io "author-label-injector" force deleted
Example 5: Delete Location Policy from YAML

Remove Location volume mounter using file.

# Delete from file
kubectl delete -f location-volume-mounter.yaml
mutatingadmissionpolicy.admissionregistration.k8s.io "location-volume-mounter" deleted

deletecollection - delete collection of MutatingAdmissionPolicy

Delete multiple MutatingAdmissionPolicies matching specified criteria.

HTTP Request
DELETE /apis/admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1beta1/mutatingadmissionpolicies
Parameters
  • body: DeleteOptions - delete options
  • continue (in query): string - continue token for pagination
  • dryRun (in query): string - simulate the operation without persisting
  • fieldSelector (in query): string - field selector to filter resources
  • gracePeriodSeconds (in query): integer - grace period before deletion
  • ignoreStoreReadErrorWithClusterBreakingPotential (in query): boolean - ignore store read errors
  • labelSelector (in query): string - label selector to filter resources
  • limit (in query): integer - maximum number of resources to delete
  • pretty (in query): string - pretty print output
  • propagationPolicy (in query): string - propagation policy (Orphan, Background, Foreground)
  • resourceVersion (in query): string - resource version
  • resourceVersionMatch (in query): string - resource version match strategy
  • sendInitialEvents (in query): boolean - send initial events
  • timeoutSeconds (in query): integer - timeout in seconds
Return Value
  • 200 (Status): OK
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: Delete All anihpj Policies

Remove all mutation policies.

# Delete all policies
kubectl delete mutatingadmissionpolicies --all
mutatingadmissionpolicy.admissionregistration.k8s.io "jobpost-sidecar-injector" deleted mutatingadmissionpolicy.admissionregistration.k8s.io "skills-resource-limiter" deleted mutatingadmissionpolicy.admissionregistration.k8s.io "user-security-context" deleted mutatingadmissionpolicy.admissionregistration.k8s.io "author-label-injector" deleted mutatingadmissionpolicy.admissionregistration.k8s.io "location-volume-mounter" deleted
Example 2: Delete Policies by Label

Remove policies matching specific label.

# Delete by label (if labeled with tier=standard)
kubectl delete mutatingadmissionpolicies -l tier=standard
mutatingadmissionpolicy.admissionregistration.k8s.io "author-label-injector" deleted
Example 3: Delete Policies with Dry Run

Test deletion of multiple policies.

# Dry-run delete all
kubectl delete mutatingadmissionpolicies --all --dry-run=client
mutatingadmissionpolicy.admissionregistration.k8s.io "jobpost-sidecar-injector" deleted (dry run) mutatingadmissionpolicy.admissionregistration.k8s.io "skills-resource-limiter" deleted (dry run) mutatingadmissionpolicy.admissionregistration.k8s.io "user-security-context" deleted (dry run) mutatingadmissionpolicy.admissionregistration.k8s.io "author-label-injector" deleted (dry run) mutatingadmissionpolicy.admissionregistration.k8s.io "location-volume-mounter" deleted (dry run)
Example 4: Delete Multiple Policies by Name

Remove specific policies using names.

# Delete multiple policies
kubectl delete mutatingadmissionpolicies jobpost-sidecar-injector skills-resource-limiter
mutatingadmissionpolicy.admissionregistration.k8s.io "jobpost-sidecar-injector" deleted mutatingadmissionpolicy.admissionregistration.k8s.io "skills-resource-limiter" deleted
Example 5: Delete Non-Critical Policies

Remove all policies except critical ones (by label).

# Delete non-critical policies (if labeled with tier!=critical)
kubectl delete mutatingadmissionpolicies -l tier!=critical
mutatingadmissionpolicy.admissionregistration.k8s.io "jobpost-sidecar-injector" deleted mutatingadmissionpolicy.admissionregistration.k8s.io "author-label-injector" deleted

MutatingAdmissionPolicyBinding v1alpha1 admissionregistration.k8s.io

📋 MutatingAdmissionPolicyBinding Overview

MutatingAdmissionPolicy - Policy reference
MutatingAdmissionPolicyBindingList - Collection of bindings
Operations - API operations for management
Official Docs: MutatingAdmissionPolicyBinding v1alpha1 API | API Version: admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1alpha1

Full Description

MutatingAdmissionPolicyBinding binds the MutatingAdmissionPolicy with parameterized resources in Kubernetes clusters. In the anihpj Django project's jobpost application, MutatingAdmissionPolicyBindings connect mutation policies to specific namespaces and resources, enabling parameterized admission mutations for JobPost, Skills, User, Author, and Location deployments. Each binding evaluates its policy N times per admission request, where N equals 1 for non-parameterized policies or the number of selected parameters for parameterized policies.

The binding mechanism allows cluster administrators to apply mutation policies with different parameters across various environments. For the jobpost application, bindings can inject sidecars into production JobPost pods using ConfigMap parameters, add resource limits to Skills deployments based on environment-specific settings, enforce security contexts for User services with namespace-scoped parameters, inject monitoring labels into Author pods using cluster-wide configurations, and attach storage volumes to Location deployments with per-namespace volume specifications. Each binding's CEL expressions operate within independent cost budgets, ensuring that adding or removing bindings doesn't affect the execution of existing policy evaluations.

Syntax

# Apply a binding from YAML file
kubectl apply -f mutatingadmissionpolicybinding.yaml

# Get a specific binding
kubectl get mutatingadmissionpolicybinding <binding-name>

# List all bindings
kubectl get mutatingadmissionpolicybindings

# Describe a binding
kubectl describe mutatingadmissionpolicybinding <binding-name>

# Delete a binding
kubectl delete mutatingadmissionpolicybinding <binding-name>

YAML Structure

apiVersion: admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1alpha1
kind: MutatingAdmissionPolicyBinding
metadata:
  name: jobpost-sidecar-binding
spec:
  policyName: jobpost-sidecar-injector  # Required: references MutatingAdmissionPolicy
  matchResources:
    namespaceSelector:
      matchLabels:
        environment: production
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups: ["apps"]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources: ["deployments"]
  paramRef:
    name: sidecar-config
    namespace: anihpj-system
    parameterNotFoundAction: Deny

Parameters Description

  • apiVersion (string): API version admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1alpha1
  • kind (string): Resource kind MutatingAdmissionPolicyBinding
  • metadata (ObjectMeta): Standard object metadata with name, labels, and annotations
  • spec (MutatingAdmissionPolicyBindingSpec): Binding specification containing:
    • policyName (string, required): Name of the MutatingAdmissionPolicy to bind. References must exist or binding is invalid.
    • matchResources (MatchResources): Criteria for selecting resources to apply the mutation policy:
      • namespaceSelector (LabelSelector): Selects namespaces by labels
      • objectSelector (LabelSelector): Selects objects by labels
      • resourceRules ([]NamedRuleWithOperations): Defines which resources and operations trigger mutations
      • excludeResourceRules ([]NamedRuleWithOperations): Excludes specific resources from mutations
      • matchPolicy (string): "Exact" or "Equivalent" matching (default: Equivalent)
    • paramRef (ParamRef): Reference to parameter resource:
      • name (string): Parameter resource name (mutually exclusive with selector)
      • namespace (string): Parameter resource namespace (for namespace-scoped params)
      • selector (LabelSelector): Selects multiple parameter resources (mutually exclusive with name)
      • parameterNotFoundAction (string, required): Action when param not found: "Allow" or "Deny"

Return Value

Returns a MutatingAdmissionPolicyBinding object with complete binding configuration including policy reference, match resources, and parameter references.

  • 200: OK - Binding retrieved or updated successfully
  • 201: Created - New binding created successfully
  • 202: Accepted - Deletion request accepted
  • 401: Unauthorized - Authentication required

5 Practical Django Examples for anihpj/jobpost Application

Example 1: JobPost Sidecar Injection Binding (Production Namespace)

Bind the JobPost sidecar injection policy to production deployments with ConfigMap parameters.

apiVersion: admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1alpha1
kind: MutatingAdmissionPolicyBinding
metadata:
  name: jobpost-sidecar-binding
spec:
  policyName: jobpost-sidecar-injector
  matchResources:
    namespaceSelector:
      matchLabels:
        environment: production
        app: anihpj-jobpost
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups: ["apps"]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources: ["deployments"]
        scope: "Namespaced"
  paramRef:
    name: jobpost-sidecar-config
    namespace: anihpj-system
    parameterNotFoundAction: Deny
# Apply the JobPost sidecar binding to production
kubectl apply -f jobpost-sidecar-binding.yaml
mutatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io/jobpost-sidecar-binding created
Example 2: Skills Resource Limiter Binding (All Namespaces)

Bind resource limit policy to Skills deployments across all namespaces with tier-based parameters.

apiVersion: admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1alpha1
kind: MutatingAdmissionPolicyBinding
metadata:
  name: skills-resource-limiter-binding
spec:
  policyName: skills-resource-limiter
  matchResources:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups: ["apps"]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources: ["deployments"]
        resourceNames: ["skills-*"]
  paramRef:
    selector:
      matchLabels:
        tier: limit-config
    parameterNotFoundAction: Allow
# Apply Skills resource limiter binding
kubectl apply -f skills-limiter-binding.yaml
mutatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io/skills-resource-limiter-binding created
Example 3: User Security Context Binding (Staging Environment)

Bind User service security policy to staging namespace with namespace-scoped security parameters.

apiVersion: admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1alpha1
kind: MutatingAdmissionPolicyBinding
metadata:
  name: user-security-binding
spec:
  policyName: user-security-enforcer
  matchResources:
    namespaceSelector:
      matchLabels:
        environment: staging
    objectSelector:
      matchLabels:
        component: user-service
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups: [""]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: ["CREATE"]
        resources: ["pods"]
  paramRef:
    name: security-defaults
    # Namespace intentionally omitted - uses object's namespace
    parameterNotFoundAction: Deny
# Apply User security binding to staging
kubectl apply -f user-security-binding.yaml
mutatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io/user-security-binding created
Example 4: Author Label Injector Binding (Cluster-Wide)

Bind Author label injection policy cluster-wide with multiple parameter resources using selector.

apiVersion: admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1alpha1
kind: MutatingAdmissionPolicyBinding
metadata:
  name: author-label-binding
spec:
  policyName: author-label-injector
  matchResources:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups: ["apps"]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources: ["deployments", "statefulsets"]
    excludeResourceRules:
      - apiGroups: ["apps"]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: ["*"]
        resources: ["deployments"]
        resourceNames: ["system-*"]
  paramRef:
    selector:
      matchLabels:
        type: label-config
        component: author
    parameterNotFoundAction: Allow
# Apply Author label injector binding cluster-wide
kubectl apply -f author-label-binding.yaml
mutatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io/author-label-binding created
Example 5: Location Volume Mounter Binding (Per-Region)

Bind Location volume mounting policy with region-specific parameter selection and equivalent matching.

apiVersion: admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1alpha1
kind: MutatingAdmissionPolicyBinding
metadata:
  name: location-volume-binding
spec:
  policyName: location-volume-mounter
  matchResources:
    namespaceSelector:
      matchExpressions:
        - key: region
          operator: In
          values: ["us-west", "us-east"]
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups: ["apps"]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: ["CREATE"]
        resources: ["deployments"]
        resourceNames: ["location-*"]
    matchPolicy: Equivalent
  paramRef:
    name: volume-config
    namespace: anihpj-storage
    parameterNotFoundAction: Deny
# Apply Location volume binding for us-west and us-east regions
kubectl apply -f location-volume-binding.yaml
mutatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io/location-volume-binding created

MutatingAdmissionPolicy

References the MutatingAdmissionPolicy resource that defines the mutation logic applied by this binding. The policyName field in the binding spec must match an existing MutatingAdmissionPolicy name. If the referenced policy doesn't exist, the binding is considered invalid. In the anihpj jobpost application, bindings connect to policies like jobpost-sidecar-injector, skills-resource-limiter, user-security-enforcer, author-label-injector, and location-volume-mounter.

Syntax
# Get the policy referenced by a binding
kubectl get mutatingadmissionpolicybinding jobpost-sidecar-binding -o jsonpath='{.spec.policyName}' | xargs kubectl get mutatingadmissionpolicy

# List all policies referenced by bindings
kubectl get mutatingadmissionpolicybindings -o json | jq -r '.items[].spec.policyName' | sort -u

# Check if binding's policy exists
kubectl get mutatingadmissionpolicy $(kubectl get mutatingadmissionpolicybinding user-security-binding -o jsonpath='{.spec.policyName}')
Parameters
  • policyName (string, required): Name of the MutatingAdmissionPolicy to bind
  • Policy Reference: Must point to existing policy or binding is invalid
  • Mutation Logic: Policy defines CEL expressions and mutations applied to matched resources
  • Parameter Types: Policy's paramKind determines what parameter resources bindings can reference
5 Practical Examples
Example 1: Verify JobPost Binding Policy Exists
# Check if jobpost-sidecar-injector policy exists for binding
kubectl get mutatingadmissionpolicy jobpost-sidecar-injector
NAME AGE jobpost-sidecar-injector 15d
Example 2: List Skills Policy Details Referenced by Binding
# Get Skills policy details from binding reference
kubectl get mutatingadmissionpolicybinding skills-resource-limiter-binding -o jsonpath='{.spec.policyName}' | xargs kubectl get mutatingadmissionpolicy -o yaml
apiVersion: admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1beta1 kind: MutatingAdmissionPolicy metadata: name: skills-resource-limiter spec: failurePolicy: Fail matchConstraints: resourceRules: - apiGroups: ["apps"] operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"] resources: ["deployments"]
Example 3: Find All Bindings Using User Security Policy
# List all bindings referencing user-security-enforcer policy
kubectl get mutatingadmissionpolicybindings -o json | jq -r '.items[] | select(.spec.policyName=="user-security-enforcer") | .metadata.name'
user-security-binding user-security-staging-binding user-security-dev-binding
Example 4: Check Author Policy Status for Binding
# Verify Author policy is ready for binding
kubectl get mutatingadmissionpolicy author-label-injector -o jsonpath='{.metadata.name}: Exists'
author-label-injector: Exists
Example 5: List Location Binding and Policy Together
# Show Location binding with its referenced policy
kubectl get mutatingadmissionpolicybinding location-volume-binding -o yaml | grep policyName: && kubectl get mutatingadmissionpolicy location-volume-mounter
policyName: location-volume-mounter NAME AGE location-volume-mounter 8d

MutatingAdmissionPolicyBindingList

MutatingAdmissionPolicyBindingList contains a collection of MutatingAdmissionPolicyBinding resources, used for bulk retrieval and management operations in Kubernetes clusters. For the anihpj jobpost application, lists can retrieve all bindings across namespaces, filter bindings by labels (e.g., environment or application), paginate through large binding sets, or watch for real-time binding changes affecting JobPost, Skills, User, Author, and Location deployments.

Syntax
# List all bindings in the cluster
kubectl get mutatingadmissionpolicybindings

# List bindings with custom columns
kubectl get mutatingadmissionpolicybindings -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,POLICY:.spec.policyName,AGE:.metadata.creationTimestamp

# Get bindings as JSON for processing
kubectl get mutatingadmissionpolicybindings -o json
Parameters
  • apiVersion (string): API version admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1alpha1
  • kind (string): Resource kind MutatingAdmissionPolicyBindingList
  • metadata (ListMeta): Standard list metadata with resourceVersion, continue token, remainingItemCount
  • items ([]MutatingAdmissionPolicyBinding, required): Array of binding objects
Return Value

Returns a MutatingAdmissionPolicyBindingList object containing an array of all matching bindings.

5 Practical Examples
Example 1: List All anihpj Bindings
# List all 5 model bindings in anihpj project
kubectl get mutatingadmissionpolicybindings
NAME AGE jobpost-sidecar-binding 15d skills-resource-limiter-binding 15d user-security-binding 15d author-label-binding 15d location-volume-binding 15d
Example 2: Show Bindings with Policy Names
# Display bindings with their referenced policies
kubectl get mutatingadmissionpolicybindings -o custom-columns=BINDING:.metadata.name,POLICY:.spec.policyName,PARAM-ACTION:.spec.paramRef.parameterNotFoundAction
BINDING POLICY PARAM-ACTION jobpost-sidecar-binding jobpost-sidecar-injector Deny skills-resource-limiter-binding skills-resource-limiter Allow user-security-binding user-security-enforcer Deny author-label-binding author-label-injector Allow location-volume-binding location-volume-mounter Deny
Example 3: Extract Binding Names
# Get just the names of all bindings for scripting
kubectl get mutatingadmissionpolicybindings -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | tr ' ' '\n'
jobpost-sidecar-binding skills-resource-limiter-binding user-security-binding author-label-binding location-volume-binding
Example 4: Count Total Bindings
# Count bindings in anihpj cluster
kubectl get mutatingadmissionpolicybindings --no-headers | wc -l
5
Example 5: List Bindings with Wide Format
# Show bindings with additional details
kubectl get mutatingadmissionpolicybindings -o wide
NAME POLICY AGE jobpost-sidecar-binding jobpost-sidecar-injector 15d skills-resource-limiter-binding skills-resource-limiter 15d user-security-binding user-security-enforcer 15d author-label-binding author-label-injector 15d location-volume-binding location-volume-mounter 15d

Operations

The following operations are available for managing MutatingAdmissionPolicyBinding resources in the anihpj jobpost application for configuring admission mutation bindings:

get - Read the specified MutatingAdmissionPolicyBinding
list - List or watch objects of kind MutatingAdmissionPolicyBinding
create - Create a MutatingAdmissionPolicyBinding
update - Replace the specified MutatingAdmissionPolicyBinding
patch - Partially update the specified MutatingAdmissionPolicyBinding
delete - Delete a MutatingAdmissionPolicyBinding
deletecollection - Delete collection of MutatingAdmissionPolicyBinding

get - Read the specified MutatingAdmissionPolicyBinding

HTTP Request
GET /apis/admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1alpha1/mutatingadmissionpolicybindings/{name}
Parameters
  • name (in path, string, required): Name of the MutatingAdmissionPolicyBinding
  • pretty (in query, string): Pretty-print output
Return Value
  • 200 (MutatingAdmissionPolicyBinding): OK
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Practical Examples
Example 1: Get JobPost Sidecar Binding YAML
# Retrieve JobPost sidecar binding configuration
kubectl get mutatingadmissionpolicybinding jobpost-sidecar-binding -o yaml
apiVersion: admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1alpha1 kind: MutatingAdmissionPolicyBinding metadata: name: jobpost-sidecar-binding creationTimestamp: "2024-11-30T10:15:00Z" spec: policyName: jobpost-sidecar-injector matchResources: namespaceSelector: matchLabels: environment: production resourceRules: - apiGroups: ["apps"] operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"] resources: ["deployments"] paramRef: name: jobpost-sidecar-config namespace: anihpj-system parameterNotFoundAction: Deny
Example 2: Describe Skills Resource Binding
# Get detailed description of Skills resource binding
kubectl describe mutatingadmissionpolicybinding skills-resource-limiter-binding
Name: skills-resource-limiter-binding API Version: admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1alpha1 Kind: MutatingAdmissionPolicyBinding Metadata: Creation Timestamp: 2024-11-30T10:20:00Z Spec: Match Resources: Resource Rules: Api Groups: apps Operations: CREATE UPDATE Resources: deployments Param Ref: Parameter Not Found Action: Allow Selector: Match Labels: tier: limit-config Policy Name: skills-resource-limiter
Example 3: Get User Security Binding JSON
# Retrieve User security binding in JSON format
kubectl get mutatingadmissionpolicybinding user-security-binding -o json
{ "apiVersion": "admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1alpha1", "kind": "MutatingAdmissionPolicyBinding", "metadata": { "name": "user-security-binding", "creationTimestamp": "2024-11-30T10:25:00Z" }, "spec": { "policyName": "user-security-enforcer", "matchResources": { "namespaceSelector": { "matchLabels": { "environment": "staging" } } }, "paramRef": { "name": "security-defaults", "parameterNotFoundAction": "Deny" } } }
Example 4: Extract Author Binding Policy Name
# Get the policy name from Author binding
kubectl get mutatingadmissionpolicybinding author-label-binding -o jsonpath='{.spec.policyName}'
author-label-injector
Example 5: Get Location Binding Parameter Action
# Extract parameter not found action from Location binding
kubectl get mutatingadmissionpolicybinding location-volume-binding -o jsonpath='{.spec.paramRef.parameterNotFoundAction}'
Deny

list - List or watch objects of kind MutatingAdmissionPolicyBinding

HTTP Request
GET /apis/admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1alpha1/mutatingadmissionpolicybindings
Parameters
  • allowWatchBookmarks (in query, boolean): Watch bookmarks support
  • continue (in query, string): Continue token for pagination
  • fieldSelector (in query, string): Field selector to filter results
  • labelSelector (in query, string): Label selector to filter results
  • limit (in query, integer): Maximum number of results
  • pretty (in query, string): Pretty-print output
  • resourceVersion (in query, string): Resource version for list
  • resourceVersionMatch (in query, string): How to match resource version
  • sendInitialEvents (in query, boolean): Send initial events for watch
  • timeoutSeconds (in query, integer): Timeout for list/watch
  • watch (in query, boolean): Watch for changes
Return Value
  • 200 (MutatingAdmissionPolicyBindingList): OK
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Practical Examples
Example 1: List All anihpj Bindings
# List all 5 bindings for JobPost, Skills, User, Author, Location
kubectl get mutatingadmissionpolicybindings
NAME AGE jobpost-sidecar-binding 15d skills-resource-limiter-binding 15d user-security-binding 15d author-label-binding 15d location-volume-binding 15d
Example 2: List with Custom Columns
# Show bindings with policy names and parameter actions
kubectl get mutatingadmissionpolicybindings -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,POLICY:.spec.policyName,PARAM-ACTION:.spec.paramRef.parameterNotFoundAction
NAME POLICY PARAM-ACTION jobpost-sidecar-binding jobpost-sidecar-injector Deny skills-resource-limiter-binding skills-resource-limiter Allow user-security-binding user-security-enforcer Deny author-label-binding author-label-injector Allow location-volume-binding location-volume-mounter Deny
Example 3: Watch for Binding Changes
# Watch for real-time changes to bindings
kubectl get mutatingadmissionpolicybindings --watch
NAME AGE jobpost-sidecar-binding 15d skills-resource-limiter-binding 15d user-security-binding 15d jobpost-sidecar-binding 15d MODIFIED author-label-binding 15d location-volume-binding 15d
Example 4: Extract All Binding Names
# Get names for scripting/automation
kubectl get mutatingadmissionpolicybindings -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | tr ' ' '\n'
jobpost-sidecar-binding skills-resource-limiter-binding user-security-binding author-label-binding location-volume-binding
Example 5: Filter by Label Selector
# List bindings with specific label (if labeled)
kubectl get mutatingadmissionpolicybindings -l environment=production
NAME AGE jobpost-sidecar-binding 15d

create - Create a MutatingAdmissionPolicyBinding

HTTP Request
POST /apis/admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1alpha1/mutatingadmissionpolicybindings
Parameters
  • body (MutatingAdmissionPolicyBinding, required): The binding to create
  • dryRun (in query, string): Dry run mode (All, None)
  • fieldManager (in query, string): Field manager name
  • fieldValidation (in query, string): Field validation level
  • pretty (in query, string): Pretty-print output
Return Value
  • 200 (MutatingAdmissionPolicyBinding): OK
  • 201 (MutatingAdmissionPolicyBinding): Created
  • 202 (MutatingAdmissionPolicyBinding): Accepted
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Practical Examples
Example 1: Create JobPost Sidecar Binding
# Create binding for JobPost sidecar injection in production
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1alpha1
kind: MutatingAdmissionPolicyBinding
metadata:
  name: jobpost-sidecar-binding
spec:
  policyName: jobpost-sidecar-injector
  matchResources:
    namespaceSelector:
      matchLabels:
        environment: production
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups: ["apps"]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources: ["deployments"]
  paramRef:
    name: jobpost-sidecar-config
    namespace: anihpj-system
    parameterNotFoundAction: Deny
EOF
mutatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io/jobpost-sidecar-binding created
Example 2: Create Skills Resource Limiter Binding
# Create binding for Skills resource limits with selector
kubectl create -f skills-limiter-binding.yaml
mutatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io/skills-resource-limiter-binding created
Example 3: Create User Security Binding with Dry Run
# Test User security binding creation without applying
kubectl apply -f user-security-binding.yaml --dry-run=server
mutatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io/user-security-binding created (server dry run)
Example 4: Create Author Label Binding Cluster-Wide
# Create Author label injection binding for all namespaces
kubectl apply -f author-label-binding.yaml
mutatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io/author-label-binding created
Example 5: Create Location Volume Binding with Field Manager
# Create Location volume binding with custom field manager
kubectl apply -f location-volume-binding.yaml --field-manager=anihpj-controller
mutatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io/location-volume-binding created

update - Replace the specified MutatingAdmissionPolicyBinding

HTTP Request
PUT /apis/admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1alpha1/mutatingadmissionpolicybindings/{name}
Parameters
  • name (in path, string, required): Name of the MutatingAdmissionPolicyBinding
  • body (MutatingAdmissionPolicyBinding, required): Updated binding object
  • dryRun (in query, string): Dry run mode (All, None)
  • fieldManager (in query, string): Field manager name
  • fieldValidation (in query, string): Field validation level
  • pretty (in query, string): Pretty-print output
Return Value
  • 200 (MutatingAdmissionPolicyBinding): OK
  • 201 (MutatingAdmissionPolicyBinding): Created
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Practical Examples
Example 1: Update JobPost Binding Parameter Reference
# Update JobPost binding to use new parameter ConfigMap
kubectl get mutatingadmissionpolicybinding jobpost-sidecar-binding -o yaml | \
  sed 's/jobpost-sidecar-config/jobpost-sidecar-config-v2/g' | \
  kubectl replace -f -
mutatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io/jobpost-sidecar-binding replaced
Example 2: Update Skills Binding Match Resources
# Update Skills binding to match additional resource types
kubectl replace -f skills-limiter-binding-updated.yaml
mutatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io/skills-resource-limiter-binding replaced
Example 3: Update User Binding Namespace Selector
# Change User binding from staging to production namespace
kubectl replace -f user-security-binding-prod.yaml
mutatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io/user-security-binding replaced
Example 4: Update Author Binding with Dry Run
# Test Author binding update without applying
kubectl replace -f author-label-binding-updated.yaml --dry-run=server
mutatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io/author-label-binding replaced (server dry run)
Example 5: Update Location Binding Parameter Action
# Change Location binding to Allow when parameter not found
kubectl get mutatingadmissionpolicybinding location-volume-binding -o yaml | \
  sed 's/parameterNotFoundAction: Deny/parameterNotFoundAction: Allow/g' | \
  kubectl replace -f -
mutatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io/location-volume-binding replaced

patch - Partially update the specified MutatingAdmissionPolicyBinding

HTTP Request
PATCH /apis/admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1alpha1/mutatingadmissionpolicybindings/{name}
Parameters
  • name (in path, string, required): Name of the MutatingAdmissionPolicyBinding
  • body (Patch, required): Patch to apply
  • dryRun (in query, string): Dry run mode (All, None)
  • fieldManager (in query, string): Field manager name
  • fieldValidation (in query, string): Field validation level
  • force (in query, boolean): Force apply patch
  • pretty (in query, string): Pretty-print output
Return Value
  • 200 (MutatingAdmissionPolicyBinding): OK
  • 201 (MutatingAdmissionPolicyBinding): Created
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Practical Examples
Example 1: Patch JobPost Binding Parameter Name
# Update only the parameter name in JobPost binding
kubectl patch mutatingadmissionpolicybinding jobpost-sidecar-binding --type=json -p='[
  {"op": "replace", "path": "/spec/paramRef/name", "value": "jobpost-sidecar-config-v2"}
]'
mutatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io/jobpost-sidecar-binding patched
Example 2: Patch Skills Binding Label Selector
# Add label to Skills binding parameter selector
kubectl patch mutatingadmissionpolicybinding skills-resource-limiter-binding --type=strategic -p='
spec:
  paramRef:
    selector:
      matchLabels:
        tier: limit-config
        version: v2
'
mutatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io/skills-resource-limiter-binding patched
Example 3: Patch User Binding Add Resource Rule
# Add StatefulSets to User binding match resources
kubectl patch mutatingadmissionpolicybinding user-security-binding --type=json -p='[
  {"op": "add", "path": "/spec/matchResources/resourceRules/-", "value": {
    "apiGroups": ["apps"],
    "apiVersions": ["v1"],
    "operations": ["CREATE"],
    "resources": ["statefulsets"]
  }}
]'
mutatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io/user-security-binding patched
Example 4: Patch Author Binding Add Label
# Add management label to Author binding metadata
kubectl patch mutatingadmissionpolicybinding author-label-binding --type=merge -p='{"metadata":{"labels":{"managed-by":"anihpj-operator"}}}'
mutatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io/author-label-binding patched
Example 5: Patch Location Binding Match Policy
# Change Location binding match policy to Exact
kubectl patch mutatingadmissionpolicybinding location-volume-binding --type=json -p='[
  {"op": "replace", "path": "/spec/matchResources/matchPolicy", "value": "Exact"}
]'
mutatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io/location-volume-binding patched

delete - Delete a MutatingAdmissionPolicyBinding

HTTP Request
DELETE /apis/admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1alpha1/mutatingadmissionpolicybindings/{name}
Parameters
  • name (in path, string, required): Name of the MutatingAdmissionPolicyBinding
  • body (DeleteOptions): Delete options
  • dryRun (in query, string): Dry run mode (All, None)
  • gracePeriodSeconds (in query, integer): Grace period in seconds
  • ignoreStoreReadErrorWithClusterBreakingPotential (in query, boolean): Ignore store read errors
  • pretty (in query, string): Pretty-print output
  • propagationPolicy (in query, string): Propagation policy (Foreground, Background, Orphan)
Return Value
  • 200 (Status): OK
  • 202 (Status): Accepted
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Practical Examples
Example 1: Delete JobPost Sidecar Binding
# Remove JobPost sidecar injection binding from production
kubectl delete mutatingadmissionpolicybinding jobpost-sidecar-binding
mutatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io "jobpost-sidecar-binding" deleted
Example 2: Delete Skills Binding and Wait
# Delete Skills binding and wait for completion
kubectl delete mutatingadmissionpolicybinding skills-resource-limiter-binding --wait=true
mutatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io "skills-resource-limiter-binding" deleted
Example 3: Delete User Binding with Dry Run
# Test User binding deletion without executing
kubectl delete mutatingadmissionpolicybinding user-security-binding --dry-run=server
mutatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io "user-security-binding" deleted (server dry run)
Example 4: Delete Author Binding Immediately
# Delete Author binding with zero grace period
kubectl delete mutatingadmissionpolicybinding author-label-binding --grace-period=0 --force
mutatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io "author-label-binding" deleted
Example 5: Delete Location Binding from File
# Delete Location binding using YAML manifest
kubectl delete -f location-volume-binding.yaml
mutatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io "location-volume-binding" deleted

deletecollection - Delete collection of MutatingAdmissionPolicyBinding

HTTP Request
DELETE /apis/admissionregistration.k8s.io/v1alpha1/mutatingadmissionpolicybindings
Parameters
  • body (DeleteOptions): Delete options
  • continue (in query, string): Continue token for pagination
  • dryRun (in query, string): Dry run mode (All, None)
  • fieldSelector (in query, string): Field selector to filter
  • gracePeriodSeconds (in query, integer): Grace period in seconds
  • ignoreStoreReadErrorWithClusterBreakingPotential (in query, boolean): Ignore store read errors
  • labelSelector (in query, string): Label selector to filter
  • limit (in query, integer): Maximum number to delete
  • pretty (in query, string): Pretty-print output
  • propagationPolicy (in query, string): Propagation policy
  • resourceVersion (in query, string): Resource version
  • resourceVersionMatch (in query, string): Resource version match strategy
  • sendInitialEvents (in query, boolean): Send initial events
  • timeoutSeconds (in query, integer): Timeout in seconds
Return Value
  • 200 (Status): OK
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Practical Examples
Example 1: Delete All anihpj Bindings
# Delete all 5 model bindings in anihpj project
kubectl delete mutatingadmissionpolicybindings --all
mutatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io "jobpost-sidecar-binding" deleted mutatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io "skills-resource-limiter-binding" deleted mutatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io "user-security-binding" deleted mutatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io "author-label-binding" deleted mutatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io "location-volume-binding" deleted
Example 2: Delete Bindings by Label
# Delete bindings with specific label selector
kubectl delete mutatingadmissionpolicybindings -l environment=staging
mutatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io "user-security-binding" deleted
Example 3: Delete Collection with Dry Run
# Test deletion of all bindings without executing
kubectl delete mutatingadmissionpolicybindings --all --dry-run=server
mutatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io "jobpost-sidecar-binding" deleted (server dry run) mutatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io "skills-resource-limiter-binding" deleted (server dry run) mutatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io "user-security-binding" deleted (server dry run) mutatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io "author-label-binding" deleted (server dry run) mutatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io "location-volume-binding" deleted (server dry run)
Example 4: Delete Specific Bindings by Name Pattern
# Delete JobPost and Skills bindings using field selector
kubectl delete mutatingadmissionpolicybindings --field-selector=metadata.name=jobpost-sidecar-binding
kubectl delete mutatingadmissionpolicybindings --field-selector=metadata.name=skills-resource-limiter-binding
mutatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io "jobpost-sidecar-binding" deleted mutatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io "skills-resource-limiter-binding" deleted
Example 5: Delete Non-Critical Bindings
# Delete bindings excluding critical ones (inverse label selector)
kubectl delete mutatingadmissionpolicybindings -l tier!=critical
mutatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io "author-label-binding" deleted mutatingadmissionpolicybinding.admissionregistration.k8s.io "location-volume-binding" deleted

CustomResourceDefinition v1

CustomResourceDefinition Overview

CustomResourceDefinitionSpec - CRD configuration specification
JSONSchemaProps - OpenAPI v3 schema validation
CustomResourceDefinitionStatus - CRD status information
CustomResourceDefinitionList - Collection of CRDs
Operations - API operations for management
Official Docs: CustomResourceDefinition v1 API | API Version: apiextensions.k8s.io/v1

Full Description

CustomResourceDefinition (CRD) represents a custom resource that extends the Kubernetes API to support domain-specific resources for your anihpj Django application. CRDs enable you to define custom API objects like JobPosting, SkillRequirement, UserProfile, ContentAuthor, and ServiceLocation that behave exactly like native Kubernetes resources, complete with validation, versioning, and CRUD operations.

In the anihpj jobpost application, CRDs allow you to model your Django domain objects as first-class Kubernetes resources with OpenAPI v3 schema validation, multiple API versions with conversion webhooks, custom printer columns for kubectl output, status subresources for separating spec from status, scale subresources for HorizontalPodAutoscaler integration, and validation rules using CEL expressions. CRDs must be named as <plural>.<group> (e.g., jobpostings.anihpj.io), can be namespace-scoped or cluster-scoped, support custom categories for kubectl get all, and enable field selectors for efficient filtering.

Syntax

# Create a CustomResourceDefinition from YAML
kubectl create -f customresourcedefinition.yaml
# List all CRDs in the cluster
kubectl get customresourcedefinitions
# Get specific CRD details
kubectl describe customresourcedefinition {name}
# Delete a CustomResourceDefinition
kubectl delete customresourcedefinition {name}

YAML Structure

apiVersion: apiextensions.k8s.io/v1
kind: CustomResourceDefinition
metadata:
  name: jobpostings.anihpj.io
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    resource-type: custom
spec:
  group: anihpj.io
  names:
    kind: JobPosting
    plural: jobpostings
    singular: jobposting
    shortNames:
    - jp
    - jobpost
    categories:
    - all
  scope: Namespaced
  versions:
  - name: v1
    served: true
    storage: true
    schema:
      openAPIV3Schema:
        type: object
        properties:
          spec:
            type: object
            properties:
              title:
                type: string
              description:
                type: string
              skillIds:
                type: array
                items:
                  type: integer
              locationId:
                type: integer
              authorId:
                type: integer
            required:
            - title
            - description
          status:
            type: object
            properties:
              state:
                type: string
                enum:
                - draft
                - published
                - archived
              publishedAt:
                type: string
                format: date-time
    subresources:
      status: {}
    additionalPrinterColumns:
    - name: Title
      type: string
      jsonPath: .spec.title
    - name: State
      type: string
      jsonPath: .status.state
    - name: Age
      type: date
      jsonPath: .metadata.creationTimestamp

Parameters Description

  • apiVersion (string): Always apiextensions.k8s.io/v1
  • kind (string): Always CustomResourceDefinition
  • metadata (ObjectMeta): Standard object metadata. Name must be <plural>.<group> format
  • spec (CustomResourceDefinitionSpec, required):
    • group (string, required): API group of the custom resource (e.g., anihpj.io)
    • names (CustomResourceDefinitionNames, required): Resource and kind names
      • kind (string, required): Serialized kind, normally CamelCase and singular
      • plural (string, required): Plural name, all lowercase, must match CRD name
      • singular (string): Singular name, defaults to lowercased kind
      • shortNames ([]string): Short names for kubectl commands
      • categories ([]string): Grouped resources (e.g., 'all') for kubectl get all
    • scope (string, required): Cluster or Namespaced
    • versions ([]CustomResourceDefinitionVersion, required): List of all API versions
      • name (string, required): Version name (e.g., v1, v2beta1)
      • served (boolean, required): Enable/disable serving via REST APIs
      • storage (boolean, required): Exactly one version must have storage=true
      • schema (CustomResourceValidation): OpenAPI v3 schema for validation
      • subresources (CustomResourceSubresources): Status and scale subresources
      • additionalPrinterColumns ([]CustomResourceColumnDefinition): Custom kubectl columns
    • conversion (CustomResourceConversion): Conversion settings between versions (None or Webhook strategy)
  • status (CustomResourceDefinitionStatus): Actual state of the CRD (acceptedNames, conditions, storedVersions)

Return Value

  • 200 (CustomResourceDefinition): OK - Request successful, CRD returned
  • 201 (CustomResourceDefinition): Created - CRD successfully created
  • 202 (CustomResourceDefinition): Accepted - Request accepted for processing
  • 401: Unauthorized - Authentication required

5 Practical Django Examples

Example 1: JobPosting Custom Resource Definition
# Create CRD for JobPosting resource with schema validation
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: apiextensions.k8s.io/v1
kind: CustomResourceDefinition
metadata:
  name: jobpostings.anihpj.io
spec:
  group: anihpj.io
  names:
    kind: JobPosting
    plural: jobpostings
    singular: jobposting
    shortNames: [jp, jobpost]
    categories: [all]
  scope: Namespaced
  versions:
  - name: v1
    served: true
    storage: true
    schema:
      openAPIV3Schema:
        type: object
        properties:
          spec:
            type: object
            properties:
              title: {type: string, minLength: 5, maxLength: 200}
              description: {type: string, minLength: 20}
              skillIds: {type: array, items: {type: integer}}
              locationId: {type: integer}
              authorId: {type: integer}
            required: [title, description]
          status:
            type: object
            properties:
              state: {type: string, enum: [draft, published, archived]}
              publishedAt: {type: string, format: date-time}
    subresources:
      status: {}
EOF
customresourcedefinition.apiextensions.k8s.io/jobpostings.anihpj.io created
Example 2: SkillRequirement CRD with Validation Rules
# Create CRD for SkillRequirement with CEL validation
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: apiextensions.k8s.io/v1
kind: CustomResourceDefinition
metadata:
  name: skillrequirements.anihpj.io
spec:
  group: anihpj.io
  names:
    kind: SkillRequirement
    plural: skillrequirements
    singular: skillrequirement
    shortNames: [sr, skill]
  scope: Namespaced
  versions:
  - name: v1
    served: true
    storage: true
    schema:
      openAPIV3Schema:
        type: object
        properties:
          spec:
            type: object
            properties:
              skillName: {type: string}
              level: {type: string, enum: [beginner, intermediate, advanced, expert]}
              yearsRequired: {type: integer, minimum: 0, maximum: 50}
            required: [skillName, level]
            x-kubernetes-validations:
            - rule: "self.yearsRequired <= 20 || self.level == 'expert'"
              message: "Years required must be 20 or less unless level is expert"
EOF
customresourcedefinition.apiextensions.k8s.io/skillrequirements.anihpj.io created
Example 3: UserProfile CRD with Custom Printer Columns
# Create CRD for UserProfile with custom kubectl output columns
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: apiextensions.k8s.io/v1
kind: CustomResourceDefinition
metadata:
  name: userprofiles.anihpj.io
spec:
  group: anihpj.io
  names:
    kind: UserProfile
    plural: userprofiles
    singular: userprofile
    shortNames: [up, user]
  scope: Namespaced
  versions:
  - name: v1
    served: true
    storage: true
    schema:
      openAPIV3Schema:
        type: object
        properties:
          spec:
            type: object
            properties:
              username: {type: string, pattern: '^[a-z0-9]{3,20}$'}
              email: {type: string, format: email}
              role: {type: string, enum: [admin, editor, viewer]}
          status:
            type: object
            properties:
              active: {type: boolean}
              lastLogin: {type: string, format: date-time}
    additionalPrinterColumns:
    - name: Username
      type: string
      jsonPath: .spec.username
    - name: Role
      type: string
      jsonPath: .spec.role
    - name: Active
      type: boolean
      jsonPath: .status.active
    - name: Age
      type: date
      jsonPath: .metadata.creationTimestamp
EOF
customresourcedefinition.apiextensions.k8s.io/userprofiles.anihpj.io created
Example 4: ContentAuthor Cluster-Scoped CRD
# Create cluster-scoped CRD for ContentAuthor resource
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: apiextensions.k8s.io/v1
kind: CustomResourceDefinition
metadata:
  name: contentauthors.anihpj.io
spec:
  group: anihpj.io
  names:
    kind: ContentAuthor
    plural: contentauthors
    singular: contentauthor
    shortNames: [ca, author]
    categories: [all, anihpj]
  scope: Cluster
  versions:
  - name: v1
    served: true
    storage: true
    schema:
      openAPIV3Schema:
        type: object
        properties:
          spec:
            type: object
            properties:
              fullName: {type: string, minLength: 2}
              bio: {type: string}
              expertise: {type: array, items: {type: string}}
              verified: {type: boolean, default: false}
            required: [fullName]
EOF
customresourcedefinition.apiextensions.k8s.io/contentauthors.anihpj.io created
Example 5: ServiceLocation CRD with Multiple Versions
# Create CRD with v1 and v1beta1 versions for ServiceLocation
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: apiextensions.k8s.io/v1
kind: CustomResourceDefinition
metadata:
  name: servicelocations.anihpj.io
spec:
  group: anihpj.io
  names:
    kind: ServiceLocation
    plural: servicelocations
    singular: servicelocation
    shortNames: [sl, location]
  scope: Namespaced
  versions:
  - name: v1
    served: true
    storage: true
    schema:
      openAPIV3Schema:
        type: object
        properties:
          spec:
            type: object
            properties:
              city: {type: string}
              region: {type: string}
              country: {type: string}
              coordinates: 
                type: object
                properties:
                  latitude: {type: number, minimum: -90, maximum: 90}
                  longitude: {type: number, minimum: -180, maximum: 180}
  - name: v1beta1
    served: true
    storage: false
    deprecated: true
    deprecationWarning: "v1beta1 is deprecated, use v1 instead"
    schema:
      openAPIV3Schema:
        type: object
        properties:
          spec:
            type: object
            properties:
              location: {type: string}
  conversion:
    strategy: None
EOF
customresourcedefinition.apiextensions.k8s.io/servicelocations.anihpj.io created

CustomResourceDefinitionSpec

CustomResourceDefinitionSpec describes how a user wants their custom resource to appear. The spec defines the API group, names (kind, plural, singular, shortNames, categories), scope (Namespaced or Cluster), and versions with schemas. For anihpj custom resources like JobPosting, SkillRequirement, UserProfile, ContentAuthor, and ServiceLocation, the spec controls how these resources are structured, validated, versioned, and discovered by kubectl. Each version can have different schemas to support API evolution while maintaining backward compatibility.

Syntax
# Get CRD spec details for JobPosting
kubectl get crd jobpostings.anihpj.io -o jsonpath='{.spec}' | jq
# Extract group and scope from SkillRequirement CRD
kubectl get crd skillrequirements.anihpj.io -o jsonpath='{.spec.group}, {.spec.scope}'
# List all versions for UserProfile CRD
kubectl get crd userprofiles.anihpj.io -o jsonpath='{.spec.versions[*].name}'
Parameters
  • group (string, required): API group of the custom resource (e.g., anihpj.io)
  • names (CustomResourceDefinitionNames, required): kind (required CamelCase singular), plural (required lowercase), singular, shortNames, categories
  • scope (string, required): Cluster or Namespaced scope
  • versions ([]CustomResourceDefinitionVersion, required): name (required version string), served (required boolean), storage (required boolean, exactly one true), schema (OpenAPI v3), subresources (status/scale), additionalPrinterColumns
  • conversion (CustomResourceConversion): strategy (None or Webhook), webhook configuration
  • preserveUnknownFields (boolean): Preserve fields not in schema (deprecated, use x-preserve-unknown-fields)
5 Practical Examples
Example 1: Extract JobPosting CRD Group and Names
# Get group and names configuration for JobPosting
kubectl get crd jobpostings.anihpj.io -o yaml | grep -A 10 "spec:"
spec: group: anihpj.io names: kind: JobPosting plural: jobpostings singular: jobposting shortNames: [jp, jobpost] categories: [all] scope: Namespaced
Example 2: List SkillRequirement Versions and Storage
# Show all versions with served and storage flags
kubectl get crd skillrequirements.anihpj.io -o jsonpath='{range .spec.versions[*]}{.name}{"\t"}{.served}{"\t"}{.storage}{"\n"}{end}'
v1 true true
Example 3: Check UserProfile Scope and Categories
# Verify scope and categories for UserProfile CRD
kubectl get crd userprofiles.anihpj.io -o jsonpath='Scope: {.spec.scope}, Categories: {.spec.names.categories}'
Scope: Namespaced, Categories: ["all"]
Example 4: View ContentAuthor Conversion Strategy
# Check conversion configuration for ContentAuthor
kubectl get crd contentauthors.anihpj.io -o jsonpath='{.spec.conversion.strategy}'
None
Example 5: List ServiceLocation Short Names
# Get all short names for ServiceLocation resource
kubectl get crd servicelocations.anihpj.io -o jsonpath='{.spec.names.shortNames[*]}'
sl location

JSONSchemaProps

JSONSchemaProps is a JSON-Schema following Specification Draft 4 that defines the OpenAPI v3 schema for validating custom resources. In anihpj, JSONSchemaProps validates JobPosting titles (minLength, maxLength), SkillRequirement levels (enum values), UserProfile emails (format validation), ContentAuthor expertise arrays, and ServiceLocation coordinates (minimum/maximum ranges). The schema supports type definitions (string, integer, boolean, object, array), validation constraints (required, pattern, min/max), default values, CEL validation rules via x-kubernetes-validations, and special extensions like x-kubernetes-embedded-resource, x-kubernetes-int-or-string, and x-kubernetes-preserve-unknown-fields.

Syntax
# Get JobPosting schema from CRD
kubectl get crd jobpostings.anihpj.io -o jsonpath='{.spec.versions[0].schema.openAPIV3Schema}' | jq
# Extract required fields for SkillRequirement
kubectl get crd skillrequirements.anihpj.io -o jsonpath='{.spec.versions[0].schema.openAPIV3Schema.properties.spec.required[*]}'
# View UserProfile validation rules
kubectl get crd userprofiles.anihpj.io -o jsonpath='{.spec.versions[0].schema.openAPIV3Schema.properties.spec}'
Parameters
  • type (string): JSON type: object, string, integer, number, boolean, array
  • properties (map[string]JSONSchemaProps): Object property definitions with nested schemas
  • required ([]string): Required property names
  • format (string): OpenAPI format: email, uri, date-time, ipv4, ipv6, uuid, etc.
  • enum ([]JSON): List of allowed values
  • pattern (string): Regular expression for string validation
  • minimum/maximum (double): Numeric range constraints
  • minLength/maxLength (int64): String length constraints
  • items (JSONSchemaPropsOrArray): Array item schema
  • default (JSON): Default value for undefined fields
  • x-kubernetes-validations ([]ValidationRule): CEL validation rules with rule expression and message
  • x-kubernetes-preserve-unknown-fields (boolean): Stop pruning of unspecified fields
5 Practical Examples
Example 1: View JobPosting Schema Properties
# Get all spec properties for JobPosting
kubectl get crd jobpostings.anihpj.io -o jsonpath='{.spec.versions[0].schema.openAPIV3Schema.properties.spec.properties}' | jq 'keys'
["title", "description", "skillIds", "locationId", "authorId"]
Example 2: Extract SkillRequirement Enum Values
# Get allowed values for level field
kubectl get crd skillrequirements.anihpj.io -o jsonpath='{.spec.versions[0].schema.openAPIV3Schema.properties.spec.properties.level.enum[*]}'
beginner intermediate advanced expert
Example 3: Check UserProfile Email Format Validation
# Verify format constraint for email field
kubectl get crd userprofiles.anihpj.io -o jsonpath='{.spec.versions[0].schema.openAPIV3Schema.properties.spec.properties.email.format}'
email
Example 4: View ContentAuthor Default Values
# Get default value for verified field
kubectl get crd contentauthors.anihpj.io -o jsonpath='{.spec.versions[0].schema.openAPIV3Schema.properties.spec.properties.verified.default}'
false
Example 5: Extract ServiceLocation Coordinate Ranges
# Get min/max for latitude and longitude
kubectl get crd servicelocations.anihpj.io -o jsonpath='{.spec.versions[0].schema.openAPIV3Schema.properties.spec.properties.coordinates.properties}'
{"latitude":{"maximum":90,"minimum":-90,"type":"number"},"longitude":{"maximum":180,"minimum":-180,"type":"number"}}

CustomResourceDefinitionStatus

CustomResourceDefinitionStatus indicates the actual state of the CustomResourceDefinition including acceptedNames (the names actually being used for discovery), conditions (Established, NamesAccepted, Terminating types with status/message/reason), and storedVersions (all versions ever persisted in etcd for migration tracking). For anihpj CRDs, the status helps verify that JobPosting, SkillRequirement, UserProfile, ContentAuthor, and ServiceLocation resources are properly established and ready to accept custom resource instances. The Established condition indicates the CRD is ready to serve requests.

Syntax
# Get status of JobPosting CRD
kubectl get crd jobpostings.anihpj.io -o jsonpath='{.status}' | jq
# Check if SkillRequirement CRD is established
kubectl get crd skillrequirements.anihpj.io -o jsonpath='{.status.conditions[?(@.type=="Established")].status}'
# List stored versions for UserProfile
kubectl get crd userprofiles.anihpj.io -o jsonpath='{.status.storedVersions[*]}'
Parameters
  • acceptedNames (CustomResourceDefinitionNames): Names actually being used (kind, plural, singular, shortNames, categories)
  • conditions ([]CustomResourceDefinitionCondition): type (Established, NamesAccepted, Terminating), status (True, False, Unknown), lastTransitionTime, message, reason
  • storedVersions ([]string): All versions ever persisted to etcd, enables migration path
5 Practical Examples
Example 1: Check JobPosting CRD Establishment
# Verify JobPosting CRD is ready to use
kubectl get crd jobpostings.anihpj.io -o jsonpath='{.status.conditions[?(@.type=="Established")]}'
{"lastTransitionTime":"2025-01-15T10:30:00Z","message":"the initial names have been accepted","reason":"InitialNamesAccepted","status":"True","type":"Established"}
Example 2: View SkillRequirement Accepted Names
# Get names being used for discovery
kubectl get crd skillrequirements.anihpj.io -o jsonpath='{.status.acceptedNames}'
{"kind":"SkillRequirement","plural":"skillrequirements","shortNames":["sr","skill"],"singular":"skillrequirement"}
Example 3: Check UserProfile Stored Versions
# List all versions persisted to etcd
kubectl get crd userprofiles.anihpj.io -o jsonpath='Stored Versions: {.status.storedVersions[*]}'
Stored Versions: v1
Example 4: Monitor ContentAuthor CRD Conditions
# Get all condition types and statuses
kubectl get crd contentauthors.anihpj.io -o jsonpath='{range .status.conditions[*]}{.type}: {.status}{"\n"}{end}'
NamesAccepted: True Established: True
Example 5: Verify ServiceLocation NamesAccepted Condition
# Check if names are accepted
kubectl get crd servicelocations.anihpj.io -o jsonpath='{.status.conditions[?(@.type=="NamesAccepted")].message}'
no conflicts found

CustomResourceDefinitionList

CustomResourceDefinitionList is a collection of CustomResourceDefinition objects used for listing all CRDs in the cluster. In anihpj, this allows retrieving all custom resources (JobPosting, SkillRequirement, UserProfile, ContentAuthor, ServiceLocation) at once for inventory management, bulk operations, filtering by labels, and monitoring CRD deployments. The list includes metadata for pagination (resourceVersion, continue token, remainingItemCount) and supports watch operations for real-time updates.

Syntax
# List all CustomResourceDefinitions
kubectl get customresourcedefinitions
# List CRDs with custom columns
kubectl get crds -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,GROUP:.spec.group,SCOPE:.spec.scope
# List anihpj CRDs only
kubectl get crds -l app=anihpj
Parameters
  • apiVersion (string): Always apiextensions.k8s.io/v1
  • kind (string): Always CustomResourceDefinitionList
  • metadata (ListMeta): resourceVersion, continue, remainingItemCount
  • items ([]CustomResourceDefinition, required): Array of CRD objects
Return Value

Returns CustomResourceDefinitionList object containing all matching CRDs with pagination metadata

5 Practical Examples
Example 1: List All anihpj CRDs
# Show all 5 custom resources for anihpj
kubectl get crds | grep anihpj.io
contentauthors.anihpj.io 2025-01-15T10:25:00Z jobpostings.anihpj.io 2025-01-15T10:20:00Z servicelocations.anihpj.io 2025-01-15T10:30:00Z skillrequirements.anihpj.io 2025-01-15T10:22:00Z userprofiles.anihpj.io 2025-01-15T10:27:00Z
Example 2: Custom Columns for anihpj CRDs
# Display NAME, GROUP, SCOPE for all anihpj CRDs
