In anihpj Django deployments, APIServiceList enables you to query and monitor all custom API extensions at once. You can list all Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost related APIServices, filter by labels (app=anihpj, model=skills), check their availability status, and watch for configuration changes in real-time. This is useful for operational monitoring, debugging API aggregation issues, and auditing custom API endpoints.

Syntax

# List all APIServices
kubectl get apiservices
# List APIServices with custom columns
kubectl get apiservices -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,SERVICE:.spec.service.name,AVAILABLE:.status.conditions[0].status
# List anihpj APIServices with label selector
kubectl get apiservices -l app=anihpj
# Watch APIServices for changes
kubectl get apiservices --watch

Parameters Description

  • apiVersion: string - API version (apiregistration.k8s.io/v1)
  • kind: string - Resource type (APIServiceList)
  • metadata: ListMeta - Standard list metadata with resourceVersion, continue token, remainingItemCount
  • items: []APIService, required - Array of APIService objects

Return Value

  • APIServiceList: List object containing items array and metadata

5 Practical Django Examples

Example 1: List All anihpj APIServices

Retrieve all APIServices for anihpj Django application.

# List all anihpj APIServices
kubectl get apiservices -l app=anihpj
NAME SERVICE AVAILABLE AGE v1.ml.anihpj.io anihpj-ml/skills-ml-api True 2d v1beta1.analytics.anihpj.io anihpj-analytics/user-analytics-api True 1d v2.content.anihpj.io anihpj-content/author-content-api True 12h v1.geo.anihpj.io anihpj-geo/location-geo-api True 6h v1alpha1.recommendations.anihpj.io anihpj-recommendations/jobpost-recommendations-api True 3h
Example 2: List APIServices with Custom Columns

Display APIServices with custom formatting showing key fields.

# List with custom columns
kubectl get apiservices -l app=anihpj -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,MODEL:.metadata.labels.model,GROUP:.spec.group,VERSION:.spec.version,PRIORITY:.spec.groupPriorityMinimum
NAME MODEL GROUP VERSION PRIORITY v1.ml.anihpj.io skills ml.anihpj.io v1 2000 v1beta1.analytics.anihpj.io user analytics.anihpj.io v1beta1 1500 v2.content.anihpj.io author content.anihpj.io v2 1000 v1.geo.anihpj.io location geo.anihpj.io v1 1200 v1alpha1.recommendations.anihpj.io jobpost recommendations.anihpj.io v1alpha1 1000
Example 3: Count APIServices by Model

Count how many APIServices exist for each Django model.

# Count APIServices per model
kubectl get apiservices -l app=anihpj -o json | jq '.items | group_by(.metadata.labels.model) | map({model: .[0].metadata.labels.model, count: length})'
[ {"model": "author", "count": 1}, {"model": "jobpost", "count": 1}, {"model": "location", "count": 1}, {"model": "skills", "count": 1}, {"model": "user", "count": 1} ]
Example 4: Watch APIServices for Changes

Monitor APIServices for real-time status changes.

# Watch all anihpj APIServices
kubectl get apiservices -l app=anihpj --watch
NAME SERVICE AVAILABLE AGE v1.ml.anihpj.io anihpj-ml/skills-ml-api True 2d v1beta1.analytics.anihpj.io anihpj-analytics/user-analytics-api False 1d v1beta1.analytics.anihpj.io anihpj-analytics/user-analytics-api True 1d
Example 5: Extract APIService Names and Endpoints

Get list of APIService names with their service endpoints.

# Extract names and service references
kubectl get apiservices -l app=anihpj -o jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{.metadata.name}{"\t"}{.spec.service.namespace}{"/"}{.spec.service.name}{"\n"}{end}'
v1.ml.anihpj.io anihpj-ml/skills-ml-api v1beta1.analytics.anihpj.io anihpj-analytics/user-analytics-api v2.content.anihpj.io anihpj-content/author-content-api v1.geo.anihpj.io anihpj-geo/location-geo-api v1alpha1.recommendations.anihpj.io anihpj-recommendations/jobpost-recommendations-api

APIService Operations

Operations Overview

get - Read the specified APIService
get status - Read status of the specified APIService
list - List or watch objects of kind APIService
create - Create an APIService
update - Replace the specified APIService
update status - Replace status of the specified APIService
patch - Partially update the specified APIService
patch status - Partially update status of the specified APIService
delete - Delete an APIService
deletecollection - Delete collection of APIService

Operations allow you to interact with APIService resources through the Kubernetes API. These operations enable you to register, manage, and monitor custom API extensions that extend the Kubernetes API surface for your anihpj Django application, including Skills ML APIs, User analytics, Author CMS, Location geospatial services, and JobPost recommendations.

get - read the specified APIService

Read detailed information about a specific APIService object including its spec configuration (service reference, group, version, priorities, CA bundle) and current status conditions.

HTTP Request
GET /apis/apiregistration.k8s.io/v1/apiservices/{name}
Parameters
  • name: string, required - Name of the APIService (format: "version.group")
  • pretty: string - If "true", pretty-print output
Return Value
  • 200 OK: APIService object with full spec and status
  • 401 Unauthorized: Authentication required
5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: Get Skills ML APIService

Retrieve complete details for Skills machine learning APIService.

# Get Skills ML APIService full details
kubectl get apiservice v1.ml.anihpj.io -o yaml
apiVersion: apiregistration.k8s.io/v1 kind: APIService metadata: name: v1.ml.anihpj.io labels: app: anihpj model: skills spec: service: name: skills-ml-api namespace: anihpj-ml port: 443 group: ml.anihpj.io version: v1 groupPriorityMinimum: 2000 versionPriority: 20 caBundle: LS0tLS1CRUdJTi... insecureSkipTLSVerify: false status: conditions: - type: Available status: "True" lastTransitionTime: "2024-11-03T10:15:00Z" reason: Passed message: All checks passed
Example 2: Get User Analytics APIService Name Only

Retrieve just the name field from User analytics APIService.

# Get User analytics APIService name
kubectl get apiservice v1beta1.analytics.anihpj.io -o jsonpath='{.metadata.name}'
v1beta1.analytics.anihpj.io
Example 3: Get Author CMS APIService Priority Settings

Extract priority configuration from Author CMS APIService.

# Get Author CMS priority settings
kubectl get apiservice v2.content.anihpj.io -o jsonpath='{.spec.groupPriorityMinimum} {.spec.versionPriority}'
1000 25
Example 4: Get Location Geo APIService Service Reference

Retrieve service reference details for Location geo APIService.

# Get Location geo service reference
kubectl get apiservice v1.geo.anihpj.io -o jsonpath='{.spec.service.namespace}/{.spec.service.name}:{.spec.service.port}'
anihpj-geo/location-geo-api:9443
Example 5: Get JobPost Recommendations APIService JSON

Retrieve JobPost recommendations APIService in JSON format.

# Get JobPost recommendations in JSON
kubectl get apiservice v1alpha1.recommendations.anihpj.io -o json
{ "apiVersion": "apiregistration.k8s.io/v1", "kind": "APIService", "metadata": { "name": "v1alpha1.recommendations.anihpj.io", "labels": { "app": "anihpj", "model": "jobpost" } }, "spec": { "service": { "name": "jobpost-recommendations-api", "namespace": "anihpj-recommendations", "port": 443 }, "group": "recommendations.anihpj.io", "version": "v1alpha1", "groupPriorityMinimum": 1000, "versionPriority": 5 } }

get - read status of the specified APIService

Read only the status subresource of an APIService, including availability conditions and health information for your anihpj Django custom API endpoint.

HTTP Request
GET /apis/apiregistration.k8s.io/v1/apiservices/{name}/status
Parameters
  • name: string, required - Name of the APIService (format: "version.group")
  • pretty: string - If "true", pretty-print output
Return Value
  • 200 OK: APIService object with status only
  • 401 Unauthorized: Authentication required
5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: Get Skills ML APIService Status

Check availability status of Skills machine learning API.

# Get Skills ML status
kubectl get apiservice v1.ml.anihpj.io --subresource=status -o yaml
apiVersion: apiregistration.k8s.io/v1 kind: APIService metadata: name: v1.ml.anihpj.io status: conditions: - type: Available status: "True" lastTransitionTime: "2024-11-03T10:15:00Z" reason: Passed message: All checks passed
Example 2: Check User Analytics API Availability

Verify if User analytics API is available.

# Check User analytics availability
kubectl get apiservice v1beta1.analytics.anihpj.io --subresource=status -o jsonpath='{.status.conditions[?(@.type=="Available")].status}'
True
Example 3: Get Author CMS API Status Message

Retrieve status message from Author CMS API.

# Get Author CMS status message
kubectl get apiservice v2.content.anihpj.io --subresource=status -o jsonpath='{.status.conditions[0].message}'
All checks passed
Example 4: Monitor Location Geo API Status

Watch Location geo API status for changes.

# Monitor Location geo status
kubectl get apiservice v1.geo.anihpj.io --subresource=status --watch
NAME AVAILABLE AGE v1.geo.anihpj.io True 2h v1.geo.anihpj.io False 2h v1.geo.anihpj.io True 2h
Example 5: Get JobPost Recommendations Status Reason

Extract failure reason from JobPost recommendations API status.

# Get JobPost status reason
kubectl get apiservice v1alpha1.recommendations.anihpj.io --subresource=status -o jsonpath='{.status.conditions[?(@.type=="Available")].reason}'
Passed

list - list or watch objects of kind APIService

List all APIService objects in the cluster or watch for changes to APIServices. Useful for monitoring custom API endpoints in your anihpj Django application.

HTTP Request
GET /apis/apiregistration.k8s.io/v1/apiservices
Parameters
  • labelSelector: string - Filter by labels (e.g., "app=anihpj")
  • fieldSelector: string - Filter by fields (e.g., "metadata.name=v1.ml.anihpj.io")
  • watch: boolean - Watch for changes
  • pretty: string - If "true", pretty-print output
Return Value
  • 200 OK: APIServiceList object
  • 401 Unauthorized: Authentication required
5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: List All anihpj APIServices

List all custom APIServices for anihpj application.

# List all anihpj APIServices
kubectl get apiservices -l app=anihpj
NAME SERVICE AVAILABLE AGE v1.ml.anihpj.io anihpj-ml/skills-ml-api True 2d v1beta1.analytics.anihpj.io anihpj-analytics/user-analytics-api True 1d v2.content.anihpj.io anihpj-content/author-content-api True 12h v1.geo.anihpj.io anihpj-geo/location-geo-api True 6h v1alpha1.recommendations.anihpj.io anihpj-recommendations/jobpost-recommendations-api True 3h
Example 2: List Skills Model APIServices

Filter APIServices for Skills Django model only.

# List Skills APIServices
kubectl get apiservices -l model=skills
NAME SERVICE AVAILABLE AGE v1.ml.anihpj.io anihpj-ml/skills-ml-api True 2d
Example 3: Watch All APIServices

Monitor all APIServices for real-time changes.

# Watch all APIServices
kubectl get apiservices --watch
NAME SERVICE AVAILABLE AGE v1.ml.anihpj.io anihpj-ml/skills-ml-api True 2d v1beta1.analytics.anihpj.io anihpj-analytics/user-analytics-api False 1d v1beta1.analytics.anihpj.io anihpj-analytics/user-analytics-api True 1d
Example 4: List Unavailable APIServices

Find APIServices that are currently unavailable.

# List unavailable APIServices
kubectl get apiservices -o json | jq '.items[] | select(.status.conditions[0].status != "True") | .metadata.name'
"v1beta1.analytics.anihpj.io"
Example 5: List APIServices by Priority

Display APIServices sorted by group priority.

# Sort APIServices by priority
kubectl get apiservices -l app=anihpj -o json | jq '.items | sort_by(.spec.groupPriorityMinimum) | reverse | .[] | {name: .metadata.name, priority: .spec.groupPriorityMinimum}'
{"name": "v1.ml.anihpj.io", "priority": 2000} {"name": "v1beta1.analytics.anihpj.io", "priority": 1500} {"name": "v1.geo.anihpj.io", "priority": 1200} {"name": "v2.content.anihpj.io", "priority": 1000} {"name": "v1alpha1.recommendations.anihpj.io", "priority": 1000}

create - create an APIService

Create a new APIService to register a custom API GroupVersion that extends the Kubernetes API surface for your anihpj Django application services.

HTTP Request
POST /apis/apiregistration.k8s.io/v1/apiservices
Parameters
  • body: APIService object, required - The APIService definition including spec with service reference, group, version, and priorities
  • dryRun: string - Dry run mode: "All" for server-side validation only
  • fieldManager: string - Manager name for field ownership
  • pretty: string - If "true", pretty-print output
Return Value
  • 200 OK: APIService created successfully
  • 201 Created: APIService created
  • 202 Accepted: APIService accepted
  • 401 Unauthorized: Authentication required
5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: Create Skills ML APIService

Register a custom API for Skills machine learning model serving.

# Create Skills ML APIService
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: apiregistration.k8s.io/v1
kind: APIService
metadata:
  name: v1.ml.anihpj.io
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    model: skills
spec:
  service:
    name: skills-ml-api
    namespace: anihpj-ml
    port: 443
  group: ml.anihpj.io
  version: v1
  groupPriorityMinimum: 2000
  versionPriority: 20
  caBundle: LS0tLS1CRUdJTiBDRVJUSUZJQ0FURS0tLS0tCk1JSURDekNDQWZPZ0F3SUJBZ0lRVjMxOUJ...
  insecureSkipTLSVerify: false
EOF
apiservice.apiregistration.k8s.io/v1.ml.anihpj.io created
Example 2: Create User Analytics APIService

Register User analytics API with high priority for PaaS-level importance.

# Create User analytics APIService
kubectl create -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: apiregistration.k8s.io/v1
kind: APIService
metadata:
  name: v1beta1.analytics.anihpj.io
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    model: user
spec:
  service:
    name: user-analytics-api
    namespace: anihpj-analytics
    port: 8443
  group: analytics.anihpj.io
  version: v1beta1
  groupPriorityMinimum: 1500
  versionPriority: 10
  caBundle: LS0tLS1CRUdJTiBDRVJUSUZJQ0FURS0tLS0tCk1JSURGakNDQWY2Z0F3SUJBZ0lVYkU5Nk9...
  insecureSkipTLSVerify: false
EOF
apiservice.apiregistration.k8s.io/v1beta1.analytics.anihpj.io created
Example 3: Create Author CMS APIService with Dry Run

Validate Author content API configuration without actually creating it.

# Dry run create Author CMS APIService
kubectl apply --dry-run=server -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: apiregistration.k8s.io/v1
kind: APIService
metadata:
  name: v2.content.anihpj.io
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    model: author
spec:
  service:
    name: author-content-api
    namespace: anihpj-content
    port: 443
  group: content.anihpj.io
  version: v2
  groupPriorityMinimum: 1000
  versionPriority: 25
  caBundle: LS0tLS1CRUdJTiBDRVJUSUZJQ0FURS0tLS0tCk1JSURIVENDQWdXZ0F3SUJBZ0lVY0FnOUg...
  insecureSkipTLSVerify: false
EOF
apiservice.apiregistration.k8s.io/v2.content.anihpj.io created (server dry run)
Example 4: Create Location Geo APIService

Register Location geospatial API with custom port.

# Create Location geo APIService
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: apiregistration.k8s.io/v1
kind: APIService
metadata:
  name: v1.geo.anihpj.io
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    model: location
spec:
  service:
    name: location-geo-api
    namespace: anihpj-geo
    port: 9443
  group: geo.anihpj.io
  version: v1
  groupPriorityMinimum: 1200
  versionPriority: 15
  caBundle: LS0tLS1CRUdJTiBDRVJUSUZJQ0FURS0tLS0tCk1JSURJekNDQWd1Z0F3SUJBZ0lVYmw5cUM...
  insecureSkipTLSVerify: false
EOF
apiservice.apiregistration.k8s.io/v1.geo.anihpj.io created
Example 5: Create JobPost Recommendations APIService

Register alpha version JobPost recommendations API.

# Create JobPost recommendations APIService
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: apiregistration.k8s.io/v1
kind: APIService
metadata:
  name: v1alpha1.recommendations.anihpj.io
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    model: jobpost
spec:
  service:
    name: jobpost-recommendations-api
    namespace: anihpj-recommendations
    port: 443
  group: recommendations.anihpj.io
  version: v1alpha1
  groupPriorityMinimum: 1000
  versionPriority: 5
  caBundle: LS0tLS1CRUdJTiBDRVJUSUZJQ0FURS0tLS0tCk1JSURLRENDQWhDZ0F3SUJBZ0lVY3JYckg...
  insecureSkipTLSVerify: false
EOF
apiservice.apiregistration.k8s.io/v1alpha1.recommendations.anihpj.io created

update - replace the specified APIService

Replace the entire specification of an existing APIService with new configuration, useful for updating service references, priorities, or TLS settings in your anihpj Django APIs.

HTTP Request
PUT /apis/apiregistration.k8s.io/v1/apiservices/{name}
Parameters
  • name: string, required - Name of the APIService (format: "version.group")
  • body: APIService object, required - Complete APIService specification
  • dryRun: string - Dry run mode: "All" for validation only
  • fieldManager: string - Manager name for field ownership
  • pretty: string - If "true", pretty-print output
Return Value
  • 200 OK: APIService updated successfully
  • 201 Created: APIService created (if didn't exist)
  • 401 Unauthorized: Authentication required
5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: Update Skills ML APIService Priority

Increase groupPriorityMinimum for Skills ML API.

# Update Skills ML priority
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: apiregistration.k8s.io/v1
kind: APIService
metadata:
  name: v1.ml.anihpj.io
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    model: skills
spec:
  service:
    name: skills-ml-api
    namespace: anihpj-ml
    port: 443
  group: ml.anihpj.io
  version: v1
  groupPriorityMinimum: 2500
  versionPriority: 25
  caBundle: LS0tLS1CRUdJTiBDRVJUSUZJQ0FURS0tLS0tCk1JSURDekNDQWZPZ0F3SUJBZ0lRVjMxOUJ...
  insecureSkipTLSVerify: false
EOF
apiservice.apiregistration.k8s.io/v1.ml.anihpj.io configured
Example 2: Update User Analytics Service Port

Change User analytics API port from 8443 to 443.

# Update User analytics port
kubectl get apiservice v1beta1.analytics.anihpj.io -o yaml | sed 's/port: 8443/port: 443/' | kubectl apply -f -
apiservice.apiregistration.k8s.io/v1beta1.analytics.anihpj.io configured
Example 3: Update Author CMS CA Bundle

Replace Author content API CA certificate bundle.

# Update Author CMS caBundle
NEW_CA_BUNDLE=$(cat new-ca-cert.pem | base64 -w 0)
kubectl get apiservice v2.content.anihpj.io -o yaml | \
  sed "s/caBundle: .*/caBundle: $NEW_CA_BUNDLE/" | kubectl apply -f -
apiservice.apiregistration.k8s.io/v2.content.anihpj.io configured
Example 4: Update Location Geo Service Reference

Change Location geo API service name.

# Update Location geo service reference
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: apiregistration.k8s.io/v1
kind: APIService
metadata:
  name: v1.geo.anihpj.io
  labels:
    app: anihpj
    model: location
spec:
  service:
    name: location-geo-api-v2
    namespace: anihpj-geo
    port: 9443
  group: geo.anihpj.io
  version: v1
  groupPriorityMinimum: 1200
  versionPriority: 15
  caBundle: LS0tLS1CRUdJTiBDRVJUSUZJQ0FURS0tLS0tCk1JSURJekNDQWd1Z0F3SUJBZ0lVYmw5cUM...
  insecureSkipTLSVerify: false
EOF
apiservice.apiregistration.k8s.io/v1.geo.anihpj.io configured
Example 5: Update JobPost Recommendations Version Priority

Increase versionPriority for JobPost recommendations as it stabilizes.

# Update JobPost versionPriority
kubectl get apiservice v1alpha1.recommendations.anihpj.io -o json | \
  jq '.spec.versionPriority = 10' | kubectl apply -f -
apiservice.apiregistration.k8s.io/v1alpha1.recommendations.anihpj.io configured

update - replace status of the specified APIService

Replace the status subresource of an APIService. Typically managed by the API aggregator controller, but can be used for testing or manual status updates.

HTTP Request
PUT /apis/apiregistration.k8s.io/v1/apiservices/{name}/status
Parameters
  • name: string, required - Name of the APIService (format: "version.group")
  • body: APIService object, required - APIService with updated status
  • dryRun: string - Dry run mode: "All" for validation only
  • fieldManager: string - Manager name for field ownership
  • pretty: string - If "true", pretty-print output
Return Value
  • 200 OK: Status updated successfully
  • 201 Created: Status created
  • 401 Unauthorized: Authentication required
5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: Mark Skills ML API as Unavailable

Manually update Skills ML APIService status (for testing).

# Update Skills ML status
kubectl apply --subresource=status -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: apiregistration.k8s.io/v1
kind: APIService
metadata:
  name: v1.ml.anihpj.io
status:
  conditions:
  - type: Available
    status: "False"
    lastTransitionTime: "2024-11-03T11:00:00Z"
    reason: ServiceDown
    message: Skills ML service is temporarily unavailable
EOF
apiservice.apiregistration.k8s.io/v1.ml.anihpj.io configured
Example 2: Update User Analytics Status

Set User analytics API status to available.

# Update User analytics status
kubectl apply --subresource=status -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: apiregistration.k8s.io/v1
kind: APIService
metadata:
  name: v1beta1.analytics.anihpj.io
status:
  conditions:
  - type: Available
    status: "True"
    lastTransitionTime: "2024-11-03T11:05:00Z"
    reason: Passed
    message: All checks passed
EOF
apiservice.apiregistration.k8s.io/v1beta1.analytics.anihpj.io configured
Example 3: Set Author CMS Unknown Status

Mark Author content API status as unknown during maintenance.

# Set Author CMS unknown status
kubectl apply --subresource=status -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: apiregistration.k8s.io/v1
kind: APIService
metadata:
  name: v2.content.anihpj.io
status:
  conditions:
  - type: Available
    status: "Unknown"
    lastTransitionTime: "2024-11-03T11:10:00Z"
    reason: MaintenanceInProgress
    message: Service status unknown during maintenance window
EOF
apiservice.apiregistration.k8s.io/v2.content.anihpj.io configured
Example 4: Update Location Geo Certificate Error

Record certificate validation failure in Location geo status.

# Update Location geo with cert error
kubectl apply --subresource=status -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: apiregistration.k8s.io/v1
kind: APIService
metadata:
  name: v1.geo.anihpj.io
status:
  conditions:
  - type: Available
    status: "False"
    lastTransitionTime: "2024-11-03T11:15:00Z"
    reason: FailedDiscoveryCheck
    message: 'x509: certificate has expired or is not yet valid'
EOF
apiservice.apiregistration.k8s.io/v1.geo.anihpj.io configured
Example 5: Restore JobPost Recommendations Healthy Status

Update JobPost recommendations API to healthy after recovery.

# Restore JobPost healthy status
kubectl apply --subresource=status -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: apiregistration.k8s.io/v1
kind: APIService
metadata:
  name: v1alpha1.recommendations.anihpj.io
status:
  conditions:
  - type: Available
    status: "True"
    lastTransitionTime: "2024-11-03T11:20:00Z"
    reason: Passed
    message: Service recovered and all checks passed
EOF
apiservice.apiregistration.k8s.io/v1alpha1.recommendations.anihpj.io configured

patch - partially update the specified APIService

Partially update specific fields of an APIService without replacing the entire object. Useful for targeted changes to priorities, labels, or service configuration in your anihpj Django APIs.

HTTP Request
PATCH /apis/apiregistration.k8s.io/v1/apiservices/{name}
Parameters
  • name: string, required - Name of the APIService (format: "version.group")
  • body: Patch object, required - JSON patch, merge patch, or strategic merge patch
  • dryRun: string - Dry run mode: "All" for validation only
  • fieldManager: string - Manager name for field ownership
  • pretty: string - If "true", pretty-print output
Return Value
  • 200 OK: APIService patched successfully
  • 201 Created: APIService created (if didn't exist)
  • 401 Unauthorized: Authentication required
5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: Patch Skills ML APIService Priority

Update only groupPriorityMinimum for Skills ML API.

# Patch Skills ML priority
kubectl patch apiservice v1.ml.anihpj.io --type=merge -p '{"spec":{"groupPriorityMinimum":2200}}'
apiservice.apiregistration.k8s.io/v1.ml.anihpj.io patched
Example 2: Patch User Analytics Labels

Add monitoring label to User analytics APIService.

# Patch User analytics labels
kubectl patch apiservice v1beta1.analytics.anihpj.io --type=merge -p '{"metadata":{"labels":{"monitoring":"enabled","tier":"critical"}}}'
apiservice.apiregistration.k8s.io/v1beta1.analytics.anihpj.io patched
Example 3: Patch Author CMS Version Priority

Update versionPriority for Author content API.

# Patch Author CMS versionPriority
kubectl patch apiservice v2.content.anihpj.io --type=json -p '[{"op":"replace","path":"/spec/versionPriority","value":30}]'
apiservice.apiregistration.k8s.io/v2.content.anihpj.io patched
Example 4: Patch Location Geo Service Namespace

Update service namespace for Location geo API.

# Patch Location geo namespace
kubectl patch apiservice v1.geo.anihpj.io --type=merge -p '{"spec":{"service":{"namespace":"anihpj-geo-v2"}}}'
apiservice.apiregistration.k8s.io/v1.geo.anihpj.io patched
Example 5: Patch JobPost Recommendations Skip TLS

Temporarily disable TLS verification for JobPost recommendations (not recommended for production).

# Patch JobPost insecureSkipTLSVerify
kubectl patch apiservice v1alpha1.recommendations.anihpj.io --type=merge -p '{"spec":{"insecureSkipTLSVerify":true}}'
apiservice.apiregistration.k8s.io/v1alpha1.recommendations.anihpj.io patched

patch - partially update status of the specified APIService

Partially update the status subresource of an APIService. Typically managed by the API aggregator, but useful for testing or manual status corrections in anihpj Django environments.

HTTP Request
PATCH /apis/apiregistration.k8s.io/v1/apiservices/{name}/status
Parameters
  • name: string, required - Name of the APIService (format: "version.group")
  • body: Patch object, required - JSON patch, merge patch, or strategic merge patch for status
  • dryRun: string - Dry run mode: "All" for validation only
  • fieldManager: string - Manager name for field ownership
  • pretty: string - If "true", pretty-print output
Return Value
  • 200 OK: Status patched successfully
  • 201 Created: Status created
  • 401 Unauthorized: Authentication required
5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: Patch Skills ML Status Message

Update just the status message for Skills ML API.

# Patch Skills ML status message
kubectl patch apiservice v1.ml.anihpj.io --subresource=status --type=merge -p '{"status":{"conditions":[{"type":"Available","status":"True","message":"Skills ML service healthy"}]}}'
apiservice.apiregistration.k8s.io/v1.ml.anihpj.io patched
Example 2: Patch User Analytics Availability

Change User analytics API availability to False.

# Patch User analytics availability
kubectl patch apiservice v1beta1.analytics.anihpj.io --subresource=status --type=merge -p '{"status":{"conditions":[{"type":"Available","status":"False","reason":"Unreachable"}]}}'
apiservice.apiregistration.k8s.io/v1beta1.analytics.anihpj.io patched
Example 3: Patch Author CMS Status Reason

Update status reason for Author content API.

# Patch Author CMS status reason
kubectl patch apiservice v2.content.anihpj.io --subresource=status --type=json -p '[{"op":"replace","path":"/status/conditions/0/reason","value":"NetworkTimeout"}]'
apiservice.apiregistration.k8s.io/v2.content.anihpj.io patched
Example 4: Patch Location Geo to Unknown

Set Location geo API status to Unknown during investigation.

# Patch Location geo to Unknown
kubectl patch apiservice v1.geo.anihpj.io --subresource=status --type=merge -p '{"status":{"conditions":[{"type":"Available","status":"Unknown","message":"Investigating connectivity issues"}]}}'
apiservice.apiregistration.k8s.io/v1.geo.anihpj.io patched
Example 5: Patch JobPost Recommendations Back to Healthy

Restore JobPost recommendations API healthy status after recovery.

# Patch JobPost to healthy
kubectl patch apiservice v1alpha1.recommendations.anihpj.io --subresource=status --type=merge -p '{"status":{"conditions":[{"type":"Available","status":"True","reason":"Passed","message":"Service recovered"}]}}'
apiservice.apiregistration.k8s.io/v1alpha1.recommendations.anihpj.io patched

delete - delete an APIService

Delete a single APIService, removing the custom API GroupVersion registration from your anihpj Django application cluster. This makes the custom API unavailable.

HTTP Request
DELETE /apis/apiregistration.k8s.io/v1/apiservices/{name}
Parameters
  • name: string, required - Name of the APIService to delete (format: "version.group")
  • gracePeriodSeconds: integer - Deletion grace period in seconds
  • propagationPolicy: string - Deletion propagation policy: "Foreground", "Background", or "Orphan"
  • dryRun: string - Dry run mode: "All" for validation only
  • pretty: string - If "true", pretty-print output
Return Value
  • 200 OK: APIService deleted successfully
  • 202 Accepted: Deletion accepted
  • 401 Unauthorized: Authentication required
5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: Delete Skills ML APIService

Remove Skills machine learning API registration.

# Delete Skills ML APIService
kubectl delete apiservice v1.ml.anihpj.io
apiservice.apiregistration.k8s.io "v1.ml.anihpj.io" deleted
Example 2: Delete User Analytics with Dry Run

Validate User analytics APIService deletion without actually deleting.

# Dry run delete User analytics
kubectl delete apiservice v1beta1.analytics.anihpj.io --dry-run=server
apiservice.apiregistration.k8s.io "v1beta1.analytics.anihpj.io" deleted (server dry run)
Example 3: Delete Author CMS with Grace Period

Delete Author content APIService with 30-second grace period.

# Delete Author CMS with grace period
kubectl delete apiservice v2.content.anihpj.io --grace-period=30
apiservice.apiregistration.k8s.io "v2.content.anihpj.io" deleted
Example 4: Delete Location Geo Immediately

Force immediate deletion of Location geo APIService.

# Immediate delete Location geo
kubectl delete apiservice v1.geo.anihpj.io --grace-period=0 --force
warning: Immediate deletion does not wait for confirmation that the running resource has been terminated apiservice.apiregistration.k8s.io "v1.geo.anihpj.io" force deleted
Example 5: Delete JobPost Recommendations from YAML

Delete JobPost recommendations APIService using manifest file.

# Delete JobPost from file
kubectl delete -f jobpost-recommendations-apiservice.yaml
apiservice.apiregistration.k8s.io "v1alpha1.recommendations.anihpj.io" deleted

deletecollection - delete collection of APIService

Delete multiple APIService objects matching specified selectors. Useful for bulk cleanup of custom API registrations in your anihpj Django application cluster.

HTTP Request
DELETE /apis/apiregistration.k8s.io/v1/apiservices
Parameters
  • labelSelector: string - Filter by labels (e.g., "app=anihpj,model=skills")
  • fieldSelector: string - Filter by fields
  • gracePeriodSeconds: integer - Deletion grace period in seconds
  • propagationPolicy: string - Deletion propagation policy
  • dryRun: string - Dry run mode: "All" for validation only
  • pretty: string - If "true", pretty-print output
Return Value
  • 200 OK: APIServices deleted successfully
  • 401 Unauthorized: Authentication required
5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: Delete All anihpj APIServices

Remove all custom APIServices for anihpj application.

# Delete all anihpj APIServices
kubectl delete apiservices -l app=anihpj
apiservice.apiregistration.k8s.io "v1.ml.anihpj.io" deleted apiservice.apiregistration.k8s.io "v1beta1.analytics.anihpj.io" deleted apiservice.apiregistration.k8s.io "v2.content.anihpj.io" deleted apiservice.apiregistration.k8s.io "v1.geo.anihpj.io" deleted apiservice.apiregistration.k8s.io "v1alpha1.recommendations.anihpj.io" deleted
Example 2: Delete Skills Model APIServices

Remove all APIServices tagged with Skills model label.

# Delete Skills APIServices
kubectl delete apiservices -l model=skills
apiservice.apiregistration.k8s.io "v1.ml.anihpj.io" deleted
Example 3: Delete Alpha Version APIServices

Remove all alpha version custom APIs from anihpj.

# Delete alpha APIServices
kubectl delete apiservices -l app=anihpj,stability=alpha
apiservice.apiregistration.k8s.io "v1alpha1.recommendations.anihpj.io" deleted
Example 4: Dry Run Delete Collection

Validate bulk deletion without actually removing APIServices.

# Dry run delete all anihpj
kubectl delete apiservices -l app=anihpj --dry-run=server
apiservice.apiregistration.k8s.io "v1.ml.anihpj.io" deleted (server dry run) apiservice.apiregistration.k8s.io "v1beta1.analytics.anihpj.io" deleted (server dry run) apiservice.apiregistration.k8s.io "v2.content.anihpj.io" deleted (server dry run) apiservice.apiregistration.k8s.io "v1.geo.anihpj.io" deleted (server dry run) apiservice.apiregistration.k8s.io "v1alpha1.recommendations.anihpj.io" deleted (server dry run)
Example 5: Delete Non-Critical APIServices

Remove APIServices that are not marked as critical tier.

# Delete non-critical APIServices
kubectl delete apiservices -l 'app=anihpj,tier!=critical'
apiservice.apiregistration.k8s.io "v2.content.anihpj.io" deleted apiservice.apiregistration.k8s.io "v1.geo.anihpj.io" deleted apiservice.apiregistration.k8s.io "v1alpha1.recommendations.anihpj.io" deleted

ComponentStatus

ComponentStatus Overview

ComponentStatusList - Collection of ComponentStatus objects
Operations - API operations for management
Official Docs: ComponentStatus v1 API | API Version: v1

ComponentStatus holds cluster validation information, providing health status of cluster components.

Full Description

ComponentStatus (and ComponentStatusList) holds the cluster validation info for your anihpj Django application cluster. This resource provides health status information about critical Kubernetes cluster components such as the scheduler, controller-manager, and etcd. Each ComponentStatus includes conditions that indicate whether the component is healthy, allowing you to monitor the overall health of your cluster infrastructure.

Note that ComponentStatus is deprecated in Kubernetes v1.19+ as the component health endpoints have been migrated to dedicated health check endpoints. However, it remains available for clusters that need backward compatibility. In your anihpj deployment, you can use ComponentStatus to verify that all cluster components are functioning correctly before deploying Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost services.

Syntax

# Get a specific ComponentStatus
kubectl get componentstatus {name}
# Get a specific ComponentStatus (short form)
kubectl get cs {name}
# List all ComponentStatuses
kubectl get componentstatuses
# List all ComponentStatuses (short form)
kubectl get cs
# Get detailed information about a specific ComponentStatus
kubectl describe componentstatus {name}
# Get ComponentStatus in YAML format
kubectl get componentstatus {name} -o yaml

YAML Structure

apiVersion: v1
kind: ComponentStatus
metadata:
  name: scheduler
conditions:
- type: Healthy
  status: "True"
  message: ok
  error: ""

Parameters Description

  • apiVersion: string - API version, always "v1" for ComponentStatus
  • kind: string - Resource type, always "ComponentStatus"
  • metadata: ObjectMeta - Standard object metadata including name
    • name: string - Component name (e.g., "scheduler", "controller-manager", "etcd-0")
  • conditions: []ComponentCondition - List of component conditions observed
    • type: string, required - Type of condition for a component (Valid value: "Healthy")
    • status: string, required - Status of the condition ("True", "False", or "Unknown")
    • message: string - Human-readable message about the condition
    • error: string - Condition error code for health check failures

Return Value

  • 200 (ComponentStatus): OK - Request successful, ComponentStatus returned
  • 401: Unauthorized - Authentication required

5 Practical Django Examples

Example 1: Check Scheduler Health for Skills Deployment

This example demonstrates checking scheduler component health before deploying Skills model services.

# Check scheduler health before Skills deployment
kubectl get componentstatus scheduler
NAME STATUS MESSAGE ERROR scheduler Healthy ok
Example 2: Verify Controller Manager for User Services

This example shows checking controller-manager health before User authentication services deployment.

# Verify controller-manager health
kubectl get cs controller-manager -o yaml
apiVersion: v1 kind: ComponentStatus metadata: name: controller-manager conditions: - type: Healthy status: "True" message: ok
Example 3: Check etcd Health for Author Content Storage

This example illustrates checking etcd health before deploying Author content management services.

# Check etcd health
kubectl get componentstatus etcd-0
NAME STATUS MESSAGE ERROR etcd-0 Healthy ok
Example 4: List All Components for Location Service Deployment

This example demonstrates listing all cluster components before deploying Location geolocation services.

# List all cluster components
kubectl get cs
NAME STATUS MESSAGE ERROR scheduler Healthy ok controller-manager Healthy ok etcd-0 Healthy ok
Example 5: Monitor All Components for JobPost Application Health

This example shows comprehensive cluster health check before deploying the entire JobPost application stack.

# Get detailed component status
kubectl describe cs scheduler
kubectl describe cs controller-manager
kubectl describe cs etcd-0
Name: scheduler Conditions: Type: Healthy Status: True Message: ok Name: controller-manager Conditions: Type: Healthy Status: True Message: ok Name: etcd-0 Conditions: Type: Healthy Status: True Message: ok

ComponentStatusList

Official Docs: ComponentStatusList API | API Version: v1

Status of all the conditions for the component as a list of ComponentStatus objects.

Full Description

ComponentStatusList is a collection resource that contains multiple ComponentStatus objects representing the health status of all cluster components. This list provides a comprehensive view of your anihpj Django application cluster's control plane health, including the scheduler, controller-manager, and etcd components. The list includes standard metadata and an items array containing all ComponentStatus objects.

Note that ComponentStatusList is deprecated in Kubernetes v1.19+ along with ComponentStatus. For monitoring cluster component health in newer deployments, consider using the dedicated health check endpoints. However, ComponentStatusList remains useful for legacy systems where you need to quickly assess the health of all critical cluster components before deploying or troubleshooting your Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost services.

Syntax

# List all component statuses
kubectl get componentstatuses
# List with short form
kubectl get cs
# List with custom output
kubectl get cs -o wide
# List with YAML output
kubectl get cs -o yaml
# List with JSON output
kubectl get cs -o json

Parameters Description

  • apiVersion: string - API version (v1)
  • kind: string - Resource type (ComponentStatusList)
  • metadata: ListMeta - Standard list metadata with resourceVersion
  • items: []ComponentStatus, required - Array of ComponentStatus objects representing each cluster component's health

Return Value

  • ComponentStatusList: List object containing items array and metadata

5 Practical Django Examples

Example 1: List All Components for Skills Cluster Health Check

Retrieve all component statuses to verify cluster health before Skills ML deployment.

# List all component statuses
kubectl get cs
NAME STATUS MESSAGE ERROR scheduler Healthy ok controller-manager Healthy ok etcd-0 Healthy ok
Example 2: Get Component List with JSON Output for User Services

Display component statuses in JSON format for automated User service health checks.

# Get component list as JSON
kubectl get cs -o json | jq '.items[] | {name: .metadata.name, status: .conditions[0].status}'
{"name": "scheduler", "status": "True"} {"name": "controller-manager", "status": "True"} {"name": "etcd-0", "status": "True"}
Example 3: Export All Component Statuses for Author Service Monitoring

Export complete component status list in YAML format for Author CMS monitoring.

# Export component statuses
kubectl get cs -o yaml > anihpj-cluster-components.yaml
componentstatuses exported to anihpj-cluster-components.yaml
Example 4: Count Healthy Components for Location Service Deployment

Count the number of healthy components before deploying Location geospatial services.

# Count healthy components
kubectl get cs -o json | jq '[.items[] | select(.conditions[0].status == "True")] | length'
3
Example 5: Monitor Component Status for JobPost Application

Continuously monitor all cluster components for JobPost application stack health.

# Watch component statuses
kubectl get cs --watch
NAME STATUS MESSAGE ERROR scheduler Healthy ok controller-manager Healthy ok etcd-0 Healthy ok

ComponentStatus Operations

Operations Overview

get - Read the specified ComponentStatus
list - List objects of kind ComponentStatus

Operations allow you to interact with ComponentStatus resources through the Kubernetes API. These operations enable you to monitor the health of cluster components for your anihpj Django application, ensuring that the control plane is operational before deploying your Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost services.

get - read the specified ComponentStatus

Read details of a specific ComponentStatus by name to check the health of an individual cluster component.

HTTP Request
GET /api/v1/componentstatuses/{name}
Parameters
  • name (in path): string, required - name of the ComponentStatus (e.g., "scheduler", "controller-manager", "etcd-0")
  • pretty (in query): string - pretty print output
Return Value
  • 200 (ComponentStatus): OK
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: Get Scheduler Status for Skills Deployment

This example demonstrates retrieving scheduler component status before Skills model deployment.

# Get scheduler component status
kubectl get componentstatus scheduler -o yaml
apiVersion: v1 kind: ComponentStatus metadata: name: scheduler conditions: - type: Healthy status: "True" message: ok
Example 2: Check Controller Manager for User Authentication

This example shows checking controller-manager health before User authentication deployment.

# Check controller-manager health
kubectl get cs controller-manager
NAME STATUS MESSAGE ERROR controller-manager Healthy ok
Example 3: Verify etcd Health for Author Content

This example illustrates checking etcd health before Author content management deployment.

# Verify etcd health
kubectl describe componentstatus etcd-0
Name: etcd-0 Conditions: Type: Healthy Status: True Message: ok
Example 4: Get Scheduler Health JSON for Location Services

This example demonstrates retrieving scheduler health in JSON format for Location services.

# Get scheduler status as JSON
kubectl get componentstatus scheduler -o json | jq '.conditions[0]'
{ "type": "Healthy", "status": "True", "message": "ok" }
Example 5: Check Component Health Before JobPost Deployment

This example shows verifying all components before JobPost application deployment.

# Check all critical components
kubectl get cs scheduler -o jsonpath='{.conditions[0].status}'
kubectl get cs controller-manager -o jsonpath='{.conditions[0].status}'
kubectl get cs etcd-0 -o jsonpath='{.conditions[0].status}'
True True True

list - list objects of kind ComponentStatus

List all ComponentStatus objects in the cluster to get a comprehensive view of all component health.

HTTP Request
GET /api/v1/componentstatuses
Parameters
  • allowWatchBookmarks (in query): boolean - allow watch bookmarks
  • continue (in query): string - continue token for pagination
  • fieldSelector (in query): string - field selector to filter results
  • labelSelector (in query): string - label selector to filter results
  • limit (in query): integer - maximum number of results
  • pretty (in query): string - pretty print output
  • resourceVersion (in query): string - resource version
  • resourceVersionMatch (in query): string - how to match resource version
  • sendInitialEvents (in query): boolean - send initial events
  • timeoutSeconds (in query): integer - timeout in seconds
  • watch (in query): boolean - watch for changes
Return Value
  • 200 (ComponentStatusList): OK
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: List All Components for Skills Cluster Validation

This example demonstrates listing all cluster components before Skills ML deployment.

# List all cluster components
kubectl get componentstatuses
NAME STATUS MESSAGE ERROR scheduler Healthy ok controller-manager Healthy ok etcd-0 Healthy ok
Example 2: List Components with Wide Output for User Services

This example shows listing components with additional details for User service monitoring.

# List components with wide output
kubectl get cs -o wide
NAME STATUS MESSAGE ERROR scheduler Healthy ok controller-manager Healthy ok etcd-0 Healthy ok
Example 3: Export Component List for Author Service Monitoring

This example illustrates exporting all component statuses for Author CMS monitoring.

# Export component list
kubectl get cs -o yaml > cluster-health-report.yaml
cat cluster-health-report.yaml
apiVersion: v1 items: - apiVersion: v1 kind: ComponentStatus metadata: name: scheduler conditions: - type: Healthy status: "True" message: ok - apiVersion: v1 kind: ComponentStatus metadata: name: controller-manager conditions: - type: Healthy status: "True" message: ok
Example 4: Watch Component Changes for Location Services

This example demonstrates watching component status changes for Location service deployment.

# Watch component status changes
kubectl get cs --watch
NAME STATUS MESSAGE ERROR scheduler Healthy ok controller-manager Healthy ok etcd-0 Healthy ok
Example 5: List Components with JSON for JobPost Health Check

This example shows listing components in JSON format for JobPost application health validation.

# List components as JSON
kubectl get cs -o json | jq '.items[] | {component: .metadata.name, healthy: .conditions[0].status}'
{"component": "scheduler", "healthy": "True"} {"component": "controller-manager", "healthy": "True"} {"component": "etcd-0", "healthy": "True"}

Event

Event Overview

EventList - Collection of Event objects
Operations - API operations for management
Official Docs: Event v1 API | API Version: events.k8s.io/v1

Event is a report of an event somewhere in the cluster, providing informative, best-effort supplemental data about system state changes.

Full Description

Event is a report of an event somewhere in the cluster. It generally denotes some state change in the system for your anihpj Django application cluster. Events provide valuable insights into the activities and health of your Kubernetes cluster, such as pod scheduling, image pulls, container starts/stops, and resource issues. Events are particularly useful for debugging and monitoring cluster behavior, helping you understand what's happening with your Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost workloads.

Events have a limited retention time and triggers and messages may evolve with time. Event consumers should not rely on the timing of an event with a given Reason reflecting a consistent underlying trigger, or the continued existence of events with that Reason. Events should be treated as informative, best-effort, supplemental data. The events.k8s.io/v1 API provides improved precision with microsecond-level timestamps and better support for event series, making it easier to track patterns and troubleshoot issues in your cluster.

Syntax
# Get a specific event
kubectl get event <event-name> -n <namespace>

# List all events in a namespace
kubectl get events -n <namespace>

# List all events across all namespaces
kubectl get events --all-namespaces

# Watch events in real-time
kubectl get events -n <namespace> --watch

# Get events for a specific object
kubectl get events -n <namespace> --field-selector involvedObject.name=<object-name>
YAML Structure
apiVersion: events.k8s.io/v1
kind: Event
metadata:
  name: skills-deployment-scheduled.17d8c9a1b2e3f4a5
  namespace: default
eventTime: "2025-12-15T10:30:45.123456Z"
action: Scheduled
reason: Scheduled
type: Normal
reportingController: default-scheduler
reportingInstance: scheduler-node1
regarding:
  apiVersion: v1
  kind: Pod
  name: skills-pod-abc123
  namespace: default
  uid: 12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789abc
note: "Successfully assigned default/skills-pod-abc123 to worker-node-2"
Parameters Description
  • apiVersion (string, required): events.k8s.io/v1 - API version
  • kind (string, required): Event - Resource type
  • metadata (ObjectMeta, required): Standard object metadata with name and namespace
  • eventTime (MicroTime, required): Time when the event was first observed (microsecond precision)
  • action (string): Machine-readable action taken/failed (max 128 chars)
  • reason (string): Human-readable reason for the action (max 128 chars)
  • type (string): Event type - Normal or Warning
  • reportingController (string): Name of controller that emitted the event
  • reportingInstance (string): ID of the controller instance (max 128 chars)
  • regarding (ObjectReference): Object this event is about
  • related (ObjectReference): Optional secondary object for complex actions
  • note (string): Human-readable description (max 1kB, libraries should handle up to 64kB)
  • series (EventSeries): Data about event series or nil for singleton events
Return Value
  • 200: Event object (for read operations)
  • 201: Event created successfully
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: List Events for Skills Deployment

This example demonstrates listing recent events for Skills deployment troubleshooting.

# List recent events in the Skills namespace
kubectl get events -n skills --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -20
LAST SEEN TYPE REASON OBJECT MESSAGE 2m30s Normal Scheduled pod/skills-pod-abc123 Successfully assigned skills/skills-pod-abc123 to worker-node-2 2m28s Normal Pulling pod/skills-pod-abc123 Pulling image "skills-api:v2.1" 2m15s Normal Pulled pod/skills-pod-abc123 Successfully pulled image "skills-api:v2.1" 2m14s Normal Created pod/skills-pod-abc123 Created container skills-api 2m13s Normal Started pod/skills-pod-abc123 Started container skills-api
Example 2: Watch Events for User Service Issues

This example shows watching events in real-time for User service monitoring.

# Watch events for the User service
kubectl get events -n user-service --watch
LAST SEEN TYPE REASON OBJECT MESSAGE 0s Normal Scheduled pod/user-auth-xyz789 Successfully assigned user-service/user-auth-xyz789 to worker-node-1 1s Normal Pulling pod/user-auth-xyz789 Pulling image "user-auth:v1.5" 5s Warning Failed pod/user-auth-xyz789 Failed to pull image "user-auth:v1.5": ImagePullBackOff 10s Warning BackOff pod/user-auth-xyz789 Back-off pulling image "user-auth:v1.5"
Example 3: Get Events for Specific Author Pod

This example demonstrates getting events for a specific Author pod using field selectors.

# Get events for a specific Author pod
kubectl get events -n author-content --field-selector involvedObject.name=author-content-pod-def456
LAST SEEN TYPE REASON OBJECT MESSAGE 5m Normal Scheduled pod/author-content-pod-def456 Successfully assigned author-content/author-content-pod-def456 to worker-node-3 5m Normal Pulled pod/author-content-pod-def456 Container image "author-cms:v3.0" already present on machine 5m Normal Created pod/author-content-pod-def456 Created container author-cms 5m Normal Started pod/author-content-pod-def456 Started container author-cms
Example 4: List Warning Events for Location Services

This example shows filtering events to show only warnings for Location services.

# List only warning events for Location namespace
kubectl get events -n location --field-selector type=Warning
LAST SEEN TYPE REASON OBJECT MESSAGE 3m Warning FailedScheduling pod/location-api-ghi789 0/3 nodes are available: insufficient memory 2m Warning Unhealthy pod/location-api-ghi789 Readiness probe failed: HTTP probe failed with statuscode: 503 1m Warning BackOff pod/location-api-ghi789 Back-off restarting failed container
Example 5: Get Events Across All Namespaces for JobPost

This example demonstrates getting events across all namespaces for comprehensive cluster monitoring.

# Get all events across all namespaces
kubectl get events --all-namespaces --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | grep jobpost
jobpost-prod 3m Normal ScalingReplicaSet deployment/jobpost-api Scaled up replica set jobpost-api-abc to 5 jobpost-prod 2m Normal Scheduled pod/jobpost-api-abc-1 Successfully assigned jobpost-prod/jobpost-api-abc-1 to worker-node-1 jobpost-prod 2m Normal Pulled pod/jobpost-api-abc-1 Container image "jobpost:v4.2" already present on machine jobpost-prod 1m Normal Started pod/jobpost-api-abc-1 Started container jobpost-api

EventList

Official Docs: Event v1 API | API Version: events.k8s.io/v1

EventList is a collection of Event objects, providing a list of events in the cluster.

Full Description

EventList is a list of Event objects in your anihpj Django application cluster. This resource provides a comprehensive view of all events that have occurred within a specific namespace or across the entire cluster. EventLists are useful for analyzing patterns, troubleshooting issues, and monitoring the overall health and activity of your Kubernetes environment. By examining EventLists, you can quickly identify recurring problems, track deployment progress, and understand the sequence of events that led to a particular state.

EventLists support standard Kubernetes list operations including filtering by field selectors (e.g., type, involvedObject), label selectors, and sorting by timestamps. This makes it easy to query specific events related to your Skills, User, Author, Location, or JobPost services. The events.k8s.io/v1 API provides improved event tracking capabilities with microsecond precision timestamps and better support for event series aggregation.

Syntax
# List all events in a namespace
kubectl get events -n <namespace>

# List all events with wide output
kubectl get events -n <namespace> -o wide

# List events sorted by timestamp
kubectl get events -n <namespace> --sort-by='.lastTimestamp'

# List events in YAML format
kubectl get events -n <namespace> -o yaml

# List events in JSON format
kubectl get events -n <namespace> -o json
Parameters Description
  • apiVersion (string, required): events.k8s.io/v1 - API version
  • kind (string, required): EventList - Resource type
  • metadata (ListMeta): Standard list metadata with resourceVersion and continue token
  • items ([]Event, required): Array of Event objects
Return Value

Returns an EventList object containing an array of Event resources.

5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: List All Events for Skills Namespace

This example demonstrates listing all events in the Skills namespace for cluster health assessment.

# List all events in Skills namespace
kubectl get events -n skills
LAST SEEN TYPE REASON OBJECT MESSAGE 5m Normal Scheduled pod/skills-pod-abc123 Successfully assigned skills/skills-pod-abc123 to worker-node-2 4m Normal Pulling pod/skills-pod-abc123 Pulling image "skills-api:v2.1" 3m Normal Pulled pod/skills-pod-abc123 Successfully pulled image "skills-api:v2.1" 3m Normal Created pod/skills-pod-abc123 Created container skills-api 3m Normal Started pod/skills-pod-abc123 Started container skills-api
Example 2: Export User Service Events as JSON

This example shows exporting User service events in JSON format for automated processing.

# Get events in JSON format for User service
kubectl get events -n user-service -o json | jq '.items[] | {time: .lastTimestamp, type: .type, reason: .reason, message: .message}'
{"time": "2025-12-15T10:35:20Z", "type": "Normal", "reason": "Scheduled", "message": "Successfully assigned user-service/user-auth-xyz789 to worker-node-1"} {"time": "2025-12-15T10:35:25Z", "type": "Normal", "reason": "Pulling", "message": "Pulling image user-auth:v1.5"} {"time": "2025-12-15T10:35:30Z", "type": "Normal", "reason": "Pulled", "message": "Successfully pulled image user-auth:v1.5"} {"time": "2025-12-15T10:35:31Z", "type": "Normal", "reason": "Created", "message": "Created container user-auth"}
Example 3: Export Author Events to YAML for Analysis

This example demonstrates exporting Author namespace events to YAML for documentation.

# Export events to YAML file for Author namespace
kubectl get events -n author-content -o yaml > author-events.yaml
apiVersion: v1 items: - action: Scheduled apiVersion: events.k8s.io/v1 eventTime: "2025-12-15T10:40:15.123456Z" kind: Event metadata: name: author-content-pod-def456.scheduled namespace: author-content reason: Scheduled type: Normal
Example 4: Count Warning Events for Location Services

This example shows counting warning events to assess Location service health.

# Count warning events in Location namespace
kubectl get events -n location --field-selector type=Warning -o json | jq '.items | length'
7
Example 5: List Recent Events Across All Namespaces for JobPost

This example demonstrates listing recent events across all namespaces for comprehensive monitoring.

# List recent events across all namespaces
kubectl get events --all-namespaces --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
NAMESPACE LAST SEEN TYPE REASON OBJECT MESSAGE jobpost-prod 30s Normal Scheduled pod/jobpost-api-abc-1 Successfully assigned jobpost-prod/jobpost-api-abc-1 to worker-node-1 jobpost-prod 28s Normal Pulling pod/jobpost-api-abc-1 Pulling image "jobpost:v4.2" jobpost-prod 20s Normal Pulled pod/jobpost-api-abc-1 Successfully pulled image "jobpost:v4.2" jobpost-prod 19s Normal Created pod/jobpost-api-abc-1 Created container jobpost-api jobpost-prod 18s Normal Started pod/jobpost-api-abc-1 Started container jobpost-api

Event Operations

Operations Overview

get - Read a specific Event
list - List Events in namespace
list - List Events in all namespaces
create - Create an Event
update - Replace an Event
patch - Partially update an Event
delete - Delete an Event
deletecollection - Delete collection of Events

Event operations allow you to manage cluster events for your anihpj Django application. These operations enable reading, creating, updating, and deleting events that track system activities and state changes for Skills, User, Author, Location, and JobPost workloads.

get - Read the Specified Event

HTTP Request

GET /apis/events.k8s.io/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/events/{name}

Parameters
  • name (path, string, required): Name of the Event
  • namespace (path, string, required): Namespace of the Event
  • pretty (query, string): Pretty-print output
Return Value
  • 200: Event - OK
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: Get Specific Event for Skills Deployment

This example demonstrates getting a specific event for Skills pod scheduling.

# Get a specific event by name
kubectl get event skills-pod-abc123.scheduled -n skills
NAME LAST SEEN TYPE REASON OBJECT MESSAGE skills-pod-abc123.scheduled 2m Normal Scheduled pod/skills-pod-abc123 Successfully assigned skills/skills-pod-abc123 to worker-node-2
Example 2: Get Event Details for User Authentication Issue

This example shows getting detailed event information for User service debugging.

# Get event with detailed output
kubectl get event user-auth-xyz789.imagepullbackoff -n user-service -o yaml
apiVersion: events.k8s.io/v1 kind: Event metadata: name: user-auth-xyz789.imagepullbackoff namespace: user-service eventTime: "2025-12-15T10:45:30.987654Z" reason: Failed type: Warning action: Pulling note: "Failed to pull image user-auth:v1.5: ImagePullBackOff"
Example 3: Get Event for Author Content Pod Start

This example demonstrates retrieving an event related to Author pod container start.

# Get specific event for Author pod
kubectl get event author-content-pod-def456.started -n author-content -o json
{ "apiVersion": "events.k8s.io/v1", "kind": "Event", "metadata": { "name": "author-content-pod-def456.started", "namespace": "author-content" }, "eventTime": "2025-12-15T10:50:15.123456Z", "type": "Normal", "reason": "Started", "note": "Started container author-cms" }
Example 4: Get Warning Event for Location Service

This example shows retrieving a warning event for Location service troubleshooting.

# Get warning event details
kubectl get event location-api-ghi789.failedscheduling -n location -o wide
NAME LAST SEEN TYPE REASON OBJECT MESSAGE location-api-ghi789.failedscheduling 3m Warning FailedScheduling pod/location-api-ghi789 0/3 nodes are available: insufficient memory
Example 5: Get Event for JobPost Scaling Action

This example demonstrates getting an event related to JobPost deployment scaling.

# Get event for scaling action
kubectl get event jobpost-api.scalingreplicaset -n jobpost-prod
NAME LAST SEEN TYPE REASON OBJECT MESSAGE jobpost-api.scalingreplicaset 5m Normal ScalingReplicaSet deployment/jobpost-api Scaled up replica set jobpost-api-abc to 5

list - List or Watch Objects of Kind Event

HTTP Request

GET /apis/events.k8s.io/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/events

Parameters
  • namespace (path, string, required): Namespace to list events from
  • allowWatchBookmarks (query, boolean): Watch bookmarks support
  • continue (query, string): Continue token for pagination
  • fieldSelector (query, string): Field selector to filter events
  • labelSelector (query, string): Label selector to filter events
  • limit (query, integer): Maximum number of results
  • pretty (query, string): Pretty-print output
  • resourceVersion (query, string): Resource version for list
  • resourceVersionMatch (query, string): Resource version match strategy
  • sendInitialEvents (query, boolean): Send initial events in watch
  • timeoutSeconds (query, integer): Timeout for request
  • watch (query, boolean): Watch for changes
Return Value
  • 200: EventList - OK
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: List All Events in Skills Namespace

This example demonstrates listing all events in the Skills namespace.

# List all events in Skills namespace
kubectl get events -n skills
LAST SEEN TYPE REASON OBJECT MESSAGE 10m Normal Scheduled pod/skills-pod-abc123 Successfully assigned skills/skills-pod-abc123 to worker-node-2 9m Normal Pulling pod/skills-pod-abc123 Pulling image "skills-api:v2.1" 8m Normal Pulled pod/skills-pod-abc123 Successfully pulled image "skills-api:v2.1" 8m Normal Created pod/skills-pod-abc123 Created container skills-api 8m Normal Started pod/skills-pod-abc123 Started container skills-api
Example 2: List Warning Events for User Service

This example shows listing only warning events in the User service namespace.

# List warning events using field selector
kubectl get events -n user-service --field-selector type=Warning
LAST SEEN TYPE REASON OBJECT MESSAGE 5m Warning Failed pod/user-auth-xyz789 Failed to pull image "user-auth:v1.5": ImagePullBackOff 3m Warning BackOff pod/user-auth-xyz789 Back-off pulling image "user-auth:v1.5" 1m Warning FailedMount pod/user-auth-xyz789 Unable to attach or mount volumes
Example 3: Watch Events in Real-Time for Author Service

This example demonstrates watching events in real-time for Author content service.

# Watch events in real-time
kubectl get events -n author-content --watch
LAST SEEN TYPE REASON OBJECT MESSAGE 0s Normal Scheduled pod/author-content-pod-def456 Successfully assigned author-content/author-content-pod-def456 to worker-node-3 2s Normal Pulling pod/author-content-pod-def456 Pulling image "author-cms:v3.0" 8s Normal Pulled pod/author-content-pod-def456 Successfully pulled image "author-cms:v3.0" 9s Normal Created pod/author-content-pod-def456 Created container author-cms
Example 4: List Events for Specific Location Object

This example shows listing events for a specific Location service object.

# List events for a specific pod using field selector
kubectl get events -n location --field-selector involvedObject.name=location-api-ghi789
LAST SEEN TYPE REASON OBJECT MESSAGE 15m Normal Scheduled pod/location-api-ghi789 Successfully assigned location/location-api-ghi789 to worker-node-1 14m Normal Pulling pod/location-api-ghi789 Pulling image "location-service:v2.3" 13m Normal Pulled pod/location-api-ghi789 Successfully pulled image "location-service:v2.3" 13m Normal Created pod/location-api-ghi789 Created container location-service 13m Normal Started pod/location-api-ghi789 Started container location-service
Example 5: List Recent Events Sorted by Timestamp for JobPost

This example demonstrates listing recent events sorted by timestamp for JobPost namespace.

# List events sorted by timestamp
kubectl get events -n jobpost-prod --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
LAST SEEN TYPE REASON OBJECT MESSAGE 2m Normal ScalingReplicaSet deployment/jobpost-api Scaled up replica set jobpost-api-abc to 5 1m Normal Scheduled pod/jobpost-api-abc-1 Successfully assigned jobpost-prod/jobpost-api-abc-1 to worker-node-1 1m Normal Pulling pod/jobpost-api-abc-1 Pulling image "jobpost:v4.2" 50s Normal Pulled pod/jobpost-api-abc-1 Successfully pulled image "jobpost:v4.2" 48s Normal Created pod/jobpost-api-abc-1 Created container jobpost-api

list - List or Watch Objects of Kind Event (All Namespaces)

HTTP Request

GET /apis/events.k8s.io/v1/events

Parameters
  • allowWatchBookmarks (query, boolean): Watch bookmarks support
  • continue (query, string): Continue token for pagination
  • fieldSelector (query, string): Field selector to filter events
  • labelSelector (query, string): Label selector to filter events
  • limit (query, integer): Maximum number of results
  • pretty (query, string): Pretty-print output
  • resourceVersion (query, string): Resource version for list
  • resourceVersionMatch (query, string): Resource version match strategy
  • sendInitialEvents (query, boolean): Send initial events in watch
  • timeoutSeconds (query, integer): Timeout for request
  • watch (query, boolean): Watch for changes
Return Value
  • 200: EventList - OK
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: List Events Across All Namespaces for Skills

This example demonstrates listing events across all namespaces related to Skills.

# List all events across all namespaces
kubectl get events --all-namespaces
NAMESPACE LAST SEEN TYPE REASON OBJECT MESSAGE skills 10m Normal Scheduled pod/skills-pod-abc123 Successfully assigned skills/skills-pod-abc123 to worker-node-2 user-service 5m Warning Failed pod/user-auth-xyz789 Failed to pull image "user-auth:v1.5" author-content 3m Normal Started pod/author-content-pod-def456 Started container author-cms location 2m Warning Unhealthy pod/location-api-ghi789 Readiness probe failed jobpost-prod 1m Normal Created pod/jobpost-api-abc-1 Created container jobpost-api
Example 2: List Warning Events Across All Namespaces for User Services

This example shows listing only warning events across all namespaces.

# List warning events across all namespaces
kubectl get events --all-namespaces --field-selector type=Warning
NAMESPACE LAST SEEN TYPE REASON OBJECT MESSAGE user-service 10m Warning Failed pod/user-auth-xyz789 Failed to pull image "user-auth:v1.5" user-service 8m Warning BackOff pod/user-auth-xyz789 Back-off pulling image "user-auth:v1.5" location 5m Warning FailedScheduling pod/location-api-ghi789 0/3 nodes are available: insufficient memory location 3m Warning Unhealthy pod/location-api-ghi789 Readiness probe failed
Example 3: Watch Events Across All Namespaces for Author Content

This example demonstrates watching events across all namespaces in real-time.

# Watch events across all namespaces
kubectl get events --all-namespaces --watch
NAMESPACE LAST SEEN TYPE REASON OBJECT MESSAGE author-content 0s Normal Scheduled pod/author-content-pod-jkl012 Successfully assigned author-content/author-content-pod-jkl012 to worker-node-2 author-content 2s Normal Pulling pod/author-content-pod-jkl012 Pulling image "author-cms:v3.1" author-content 8s Normal Pulled pod/author-content-pod-jkl012 Successfully pulled image "author-cms:v3.1"
Example 4: List Events with Output Formatting for Location Analysis

This example shows listing events with custom output formatting across all namespaces.

# List events with custom columns
kubectl get events --all-namespaces -o custom-columns=NAMESPACE:.metadata.namespace,NAME:.metadata.name,TYPE:.type,REASON:.reason,MESSAGE:.note
NAMESPACE NAME TYPE REASON MESSAGE location location-api-ghi789.scheduled Normal Scheduled Successfully assigned location/location-api-ghi789 to worker-node-1 location location-api-ghi789.pulling Normal Pulling Pulling image "location-service:v2.3" location location-api-ghi789.pulled Normal Pulled Successfully pulled image "location-service:v2.3"
Example 5: Count Total Events Across All Namespaces for JobPost Monitoring

This example demonstrates counting total events across all namespaces.

# Count total events across all namespaces
kubectl get events --all-namespaces -o json | jq '.items | length'
247

create - Create an Event

HTTP Request

POST /apis/events.k8s.io/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/events

Parameters
  • namespace (path, string, required): Namespace to create the Event in
  • body (Event, required): Event object to create
  • dryRun (query, string): Dry run mode
  • fieldManager (query, string): Field manager name
  • fieldValidation (query, string): Field validation level
  • pretty (query, string): Pretty-print output
Return Value
  • 200: Event - OK
  • 201: Event - Created
  • 202: Event - Accepted
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: Create Custom Event for Skills Deployment

This example demonstrates creating a custom event for Skills deployment tracking.

# Create custom event from YAML file
cat <<EOF | kubectl create -f -
apiVersion: events.k8s.io/v1
kind: Event
metadata:
  name: skills-deployment-custom.event001
  namespace: skills
eventTime: "$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%6NZ)"
action: DeploymentStarted
reason: CustomDeployment
type: Normal
reportingController: skills-deployment-controller
reportingInstance: controller-node1
regarding:
  apiVersion: apps/v1
  kind: Deployment
  name: skills-deployment
  namespace: skills
note: "Skills deployment v2.1 started by admin"
EOF
event.events.k8s.io/skills-deployment-custom.event001 created
Example 2: Create Warning Event for User Service Issue

This example shows creating a warning event for User service monitoring.

# Create warning event
kubectl create -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: events.k8s.io/v1
kind: Event
metadata:
  name: user-auth-maintenance.warn001
  namespace: user-service
eventTime: "$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%6NZ)"
action: MaintenanceScheduled
reason: ScheduledMaintenance
type: Warning
reportingController: user-maintenance-controller
reportingInstance: maintenance-scheduler
regarding:
  apiVersion: v1
  kind: Service
  name: user-auth-service
  namespace: user-service
note: "Scheduled maintenance window for User authentication service at 2025-12-16 02:00 UTC"
EOF
event.events.k8s.io/user-auth-maintenance.warn001 created
Example 3: Create Event for Author Content Update

This example demonstrates creating an event to track Author content update.

# Create event for content update
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: events.k8s.io/v1
kind: Event
metadata:
  name: author-content-update.event002
  namespace: author-content
eventTime: "$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%6NZ)"
action: ContentUpdated
reason: BulkContentUpdate
type: Normal
reportingController: author-content-controller
reportingInstance: content-manager-1
regarding:
  apiVersion: apps/v1
  kind: StatefulSet
  name: author-cms
  namespace: author-content
note: "Bulk content update completed: 150 articles processed"
EOF
event.events.k8s.io/author-content-update.event002 created
Example 4: Create Event for Location Service Scale Event

This example shows creating an event to record Location service scaling.

# Create scaling event
kubectl create -f location-scale-event.yaml
event.events.k8s.io/location-api-scale.event003 created # location-scale-event.yaml content: apiVersion: events.k8s.io/v1 kind: Event metadata: name: location-api-scale.event003 namespace: location eventTime: "2025-12-15T11:15:00.123456Z" action: Scaled reason: AutoScale type: Normal reportingController: horizontal-pod-autoscaler reportingInstance: hpa-controller regarding: apiVersion: apps/v1 kind: Deployment name: location-api namespace: location note: "Scaled Location API from 3 to 5 replicas due to high traffic"
Example 5: Create Event for JobPost Backup Completion

This example demonstrates creating an event to record JobPost backup completion.

# Create backup completion event
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: events.k8s.io/v1
kind: Event
metadata:
  name: jobpost-backup-complete.event004
  namespace: jobpost-prod
eventTime: "$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%6NZ)"
action: BackupCompleted
reason: ScheduledBackup
type: Normal
reportingController: backup-controller
reportingInstance: backup-manager-1
regarding:
  apiVersion: v1
  kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
  name: jobpost-data-pvc
  namespace: jobpost-prod
note: "Daily backup completed successfully: 2.3GB data backed up to S3"
EOF
event.events.k8s.io/jobpost-backup-complete.event004 created

update - Replace the Specified Event

HTTP Request

PUT /apis/events.k8s.io/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/events/{name}

Parameters
  • name (path, string, required): Name of the Event
  • namespace (path, string, required): Namespace of the Event
  • body (Event, required): Updated Event object
  • dryRun (query, string): Dry run mode
  • fieldManager (query, string): Field manager name
  • fieldValidation (query, string): Field validation level
  • pretty (query, string): Pretty-print output
Return Value
  • 200: Event - OK
  • 201: Event - Created
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: Update Event Message for Skills Deployment

This example demonstrates updating an event message for Skills deployment.

# Get event, modify, and update
kubectl get event skills-deployment-custom.event001 -n skills -o yaml > event-update.yaml
# Edit event-update.yaml to update the note field
kubectl replace -f event-update.yaml
event.events.k8s.io/skills-deployment-custom.event001 replaced
Example 2: Update Event Type for User Service

This example shows updating an event type from Normal to Warning for User service.

# Update event with kubectl apply
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: events.k8s.io/v1
kind: Event
metadata:
  name: user-auth-maintenance.warn001
  namespace: user-service
eventTime: "2025-12-15T11:20:00.123456Z"
action: MaintenanceScheduled
reason: ScheduledMaintenance
type: Warning
reportingController: user-maintenance-controller
reportingInstance: maintenance-scheduler
regarding:
  apiVersion: v1
  kind: Service
  name: user-auth-service
  namespace: user-service
note: "URGENT: Maintenance window moved to 2025-12-16 01:00 UTC - action required"
EOF
event.events.k8s.io/user-auth-maintenance.warn001 configured
Example 3: Update Event Note for Author Content

This example demonstrates updating event details for Author content processing.

# Update event note with new information
kubectl get event author-content-update.event002 -n author-content -o json | \
  jq '.note = "Bulk content update completed: 200 articles processed (updated count)"' | \
  kubectl replace -f -
event.events.k8s.io/author-content-update.event002 replaced
Example 4: Update Event Reason for Location Service

This example shows updating event reason for Location service scaling.

# Update event reason
kubectl replace -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: events.k8s.io/v1
kind: Event
metadata:
  name: location-api-scale.event003
  namespace: location
eventTime: "2025-12-15T11:15:00.123456Z"
action: Scaled
reason: ManualScale
type: Normal
reportingController: horizontal-pod-autoscaler
reportingInstance: hpa-controller
regarding:
  apiVersion: apps/v1
  kind: Deployment
  name: location-api
  namespace: location
note: "Manually scaled Location API from 3 to 5 replicas for load testing"
EOF
event.events.k8s.io/location-api-scale.event003 replaced
Example 5: Update Event Series Data for JobPost

This example demonstrates updating event series information for JobPost monitoring.

# Update event with series data
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: events.k8s.io/v1
kind: Event
metadata:
  name: jobpost-backup-complete.event004
  namespace: jobpost-prod
eventTime: "2025-12-15T11:25:00.123456Z"
action: BackupCompleted
reason: ScheduledBackup
type: Normal
reportingController: backup-controller
reportingInstance: backup-manager-1
regarding:
  apiVersion: v1
  kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
  name: jobpost-data-pvc
  namespace: jobpost-prod
series:
  count: 5
  lastObservedTime: "2025-12-15T11:25:00.123456Z"
note: "Daily backup completed successfully: 2.5GB data backed up to S3 (series: 5 backups)"
EOF
event.events.k8s.io/jobpost-backup-complete.event004 configured

patch - Partially Update the Specified Event

HTTP Request

PATCH /apis/events.k8s.io/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/events/{name}

Parameters
  • name (path, string, required): Name of the Event
  • namespace (path, string, required): Namespace of the Event
  • body (Patch, required): Patch data
  • dryRun (query, string): Dry run mode
  • fieldManager (query, string): Field manager name
  • fieldValidation (query, string): Field validation level
  • force (query, boolean): Force update
  • pretty (query, string): Pretty-print output
Return Value
  • 200: Event - OK
  • 201: Event - Created
  • 401: Unauthorized
5 Practical Django Examples
Example 1: Patch Event Note for Skills Deployment

This example demonstrates patching an event note field for Skills deployment.

# Patch event note field
kubectl patch event skills-deployment-custom.event001 -n skills --type='merge' -p '{
  "note": "Skills deployment v2.1 completed successfully with zero downtime"
}'
event.events.k8s.io/skills-deployment-custom.event001 patched
Example 2: Patch Event Type for User Service Alert

This example shows patching an event type to escalate User service alert.

# Patch event type to Warning
kubectl patch event user-auth-maintenance.warn001 -n user-service --type='json' -p='[
  {"op": "replace", "path": "/type", "value": "Warning"},
  {"op": "replace", "path": "/note", "value": "CRITICAL: Maintenance window requires immediate attention"}
]'
event.events.k8s.io/user-auth-maintenance.warn001 patched
Example 3: Patch Event Action for Author Content

This example demonstrates patching event action for Author content update.

# Patch event action
kubectl patch event author-content-update.event002 -n author-content --type='merge' -p '{
  "action": "ContentSynced",
  "note": "Content sync completed: 200 articles synchronized across regions"
}'
event.events.k8s.io/author-content-update.event002 patched
Example 4: Patch Event Reason for Location Service

This example shows patching event reason for Location service scaling.

# Patch event reason with JSON patch
kubectl patch event location-api-scale.event003 -n location --type='json' -p='[
  {"op": "replace", "path": "/reason", "value": "AutoScaleTriggered"},
  {"op": "replace", "path": "/note", "value": "Auto-scaled Location API: CPU utilization exceeded 80% threshold"}
]'
event.events.k8s.io/location-api-scale.event003 patched
Example 5: Patch Event Series Count for JobPost

This example demonstrates patching event series count for JobPost backup tracking.

# Patch event series information
kubectl patch event jobpost-backup-complete.event004 -n jobpost-prod --type='merge' -p '{
  "series": {
    "count": 10,
    "lastObservedTime": "2025-12-15T11:30:00.123456Z"
  },
  "note": "Daily backup series: 10 successful backups completed"
}'
event.events.k8s.io/jobpost-backup-complete.event004 patched

delete - Delete an Event
